@return               an optional extension\n   100\u2192     */\n   101\u2192    default <T extends ComponentExtension> Optional<T> getExtension(Class<T> extensionType) {\n   102\u2192        return Optional.empty();\n   103\u2192    }\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192    /**\n   106\u2192     * Set the {@link Component} context if the component is an instance of {@link ComponentAware}.\n   107\u2192     */\n   108\u2192    static <T> T trySetComponent(T object, Component component) {\n   109\u2192        if (object instanceof ComponentAware componentAware) {\n   110\u2192            componentAware.setComponent(component);\n   111\u2192        }\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192        return object;\n   114\u2192    }\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192    /**\n   117\u2192     * Gets the default name of the component.\n   118\u2192     */\n   119\u2192    default String getDefaultName() {\n   120\u2192        return null;\n   121\u2192    }\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192    /**\n   124\u2192     * Whether autowiring is enabled. This is used for automatic autowiring options (the option must be marked as\n   125\u2192     * autowired) by looking up in the registry to find if there is a single instance of matching type, which then gets\n   126\u2192     * configured on the component. This can be used for automatic configuring JDBC data sources, JMS connection\n   127\u2192     * factories, AWS Clients, etc.\n   128\u2192     */\n   129\u2192    default boolean isAutowiredEnabled() {\n   130\u2192        return true;\n   131\u2192    }\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192}\n   134\u2192"
6. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/core/camel-api/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/Endpoint.java"
}
Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     3\u2192 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\n     4\u2192 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n     5\u2192 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n     6\u2192 * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n     7\u2192 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     8\u2192 *\n     9\u2192 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    10\u2192 *\n    11\u2192 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    12\u2192 * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192package org.apache.camel;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import java.util.Map;\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192import org.apache.camel.support.service.ServiceSupport;\n    22\u2192import org.apache.camel.util.StringHelper;\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192/**\n    25\u2192 * An <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/endpoint.html\">endpoint</a> implements the\n    26\u2192 * <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/message-endpoint.html\">Message Endpoint</a> pattern and represents an endpoint that\n    27\u2192 * can send and receive message exchanges\n    28\u2192 *\n    29\u2192 * @see Exchange\n    30\u2192 * @see Message\n    31\u2192 */\n    32\u2192public interface Endpoint extends IsSingleton, Service, ComponentAware {\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192    /**\n    35\u2192     * Returns the string representation of the endpoint URI\n    36\u2192     *\n    37\u2192     * @return the endpoint URI\n    38\u2192     */\n    39\u2192    String getEndpointUri();\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192    /**\n    42\u2192     * Returns the default exchange pattern to use when creating an exchange.\n    43\u2192     */\n    44\u2192    ExchangePattern getExchangePattern();\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192    /**\n    47\u2192     * Returns the string representation of the base endpoint URI (without query parameters).\n    48\u2192     */\n    49\u2192    default String getEndpointBaseUri() {\n    50\u2192        String value = getEndpointUri();\n    51\u2192        return StringHelper.before(value, \"?\", value);\n    52\u2192    }\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192    /**\n    55\u2192     * Returns a string key of this endpoint.\n    56\u2192     * <p/>\n    57\u2192     * This key is used by {@link org.apache.camel.spi.LifecycleStrategy} when registering endpoint. This allows to\n    58\u2192     * register different instances of endpoints with the same key.\n    59\u2192     * <p/>\n    60\u2192     * For JMX mbeans this allows us to use the same JMX Mbean for all endpoints that are logical the same but have\n    61\u2192     * different parameters. For instance the http endpoint.\n    62\u2192     *\n    63\u2192     * @return the endpoint key\n    64\u2192     */\n    65\u2192    String getEndpointKey();\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192    /**\n    68\u2192     * Create a new exchange for communicating with this endpoint.\n    69\u2192     * <p/>\n    70\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> Consumers should use {@link Consumer#createExchange(boolean)} to create an exchange for which\n    71\u2192     * the consumer received a message.\n    72\u2192     *\n    73\u2192     * @return a new exchange\n    74\u2192     */\n    75\u2192    Exchange createExchange();\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192    /**\n    78\u2192     * Create a new exchange for communicating with this endpoint with the specified {@link ExchangePattern} such as\n    79\u2192     * whether its going to be an {@link ExchangePattern#InOnly} or {@link ExchangePattern#InOut} exchange\n    80\u2192     * <p/>\n    81\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> Consumers should use {@link Consumer#createExchange(boolean)} to create an exchange for which\n    82\u2192     * the consumer received a message.\n    83\u2192     *\n    84\u2192     * @param  pattern the message exchange pattern for the exchange\n    85\u2192     * @return         a new exchange\n    86\u2192     */\n    87\u2192    Exchange createExchange(ExchangePattern pattern);\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192    /**\n    90\u2192     * Configures a newly created {@link Exchange}.\n    91\u2192     *\n    92\u2192     * @param exchange the new exchange\n    93\u2192     */\n    94\u2192    void configureExchange(Exchange exchange);\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192    /**\n    97\u2192     * Returns the context which created the endpoint\n    98\u2192     *\n    99\u2192     * @return the context which created the endpoint\n   100\u2192     */\n   101\u2192    CamelContext getCamelContext();\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192    /**\n   104\u2192     * Creates a new producer which is used send messages into the endpoint.\n   105\u2192     *\n   106\u2192     * Important: Do not do any initialization in the constructor of the {@link Producer}. Instead use\n   107\u2192     * {@link ServiceSupport#doInit()} or {@link ServiceSupport#doStart()}.\n   108\u2192     *\n   109\u2192     * @return           a newly created producer\n   110\u2192     * @throws Exception can be thrown\n   111\u2192     */\n   112\u2192    Producer createProducer() throws Exception;\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192    /**\n   115\u2192     * Whether this endpoint creates singleton producers\n   116\u2192     */\n   117\u2192    default boolean isSingletonProducer() {\n   118\u2192        return isSingleton();\n   119\u2192    }\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192    /**\n   122\u2192     * Creates a new producer which is used send messages into the endpoint\n   123\u2192     *\n   124\u2192     * Important: Do not do any initialization in the constructor of the {@link Producer}. Instead use\n   125\u2192     * {@link ServiceSupport#doInit()} or {@link ServiceSupport#doStart()}.\n   126\u2192     *\n   127\u2192     * @return           a newly created producer\n   128\u2192     * @throws Exception can be thrown\n   129\u2192     */\n   130\u2192    AsyncProducer createAsyncProducer() throws Exception;\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192    /**\n   133\u2192     * Creates a new <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/event-driven-consumer.html\">Event Driven Consumer</a> which\n   134\u2192     * consumes messages from the endpoint using the given processor\n   135\u2192     *\n   136\u2192     * Important: Do not do any initialization in the constructor of the {@link Consumer}. Instead use\n   137\u2192     * {@link ServiceSupport#doInit()} or {@link ServiceSupport#doStart()}.\n   138\u2192     *\n   139\u2192     * @param  processor the given processor\n   140\u2192     * @return           a newly created consumer\n   141\u2192     * @throws Exception can be thrown\n   142\u2192     */\n   143\u2192    Consumer createConsumer(Processor processor) throws Exception;\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192    /**\n   146\u2192     * Creates a new <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/polling-consumer.html\">Polling Consumer</a> so that the caller can\n   147\u2192     * poll message exchanges from the consumer using {@link PollingConsumer#receive()},\n   148\u2192     * {@link PollingConsumer#receiveNoWait()} or {@link PollingConsumer#receive(long)} whenever it is ready to do so\n   149\u2192     * rather than using the <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/event-driven-consumer.html\">Event Based Consumer</a>\n   150\u2192     * returned by {@link #createConsumer(Processor)}\n   151\u2192     *\n   152\u2192     * Important: Do not do any initialization in the constructor of the {@link PollingConsumer}. Instead use\n   153\u2192     * {@link ServiceSupport#doInit()} or {@link ServiceSupport#doStart()}.\n   154\u2192     *\n   155\u2192     * @return           a newly created pull consumer\n   156\u2192     * @throws Exception if the pull consumer could not be created\n   157\u2192     */\n   158\u2192    PollingConsumer createPollingConsumer() throws Exception;\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192    /**\n   161\u2192     * Configure properties on this endpoint.\n   162\u2192     *\n   163\u2192     * @param options the options (properties)\n   164\u2192     */\n   165\u2192    void configureProperties(Map<String, Object> options);\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192    /**\n   168\u2192     * Sets the camel context.\n   169\u2192     *\n   170\u2192     * @param context the camel context\n   171\u2192     */\n   172\u2192    void setCamelContext(CamelContext context);\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192    /**\n   175\u2192     * Should all properties be known or does the endpoint allow unknown options?\n   176\u2192     * <p/>\n   177\u2192     * <tt>lenient = false</tt> means that the endpoint should validate that all given options is known and configured\n   178\u2192     * properly. <tt>lenient = true</tt> means that the endpoint allows additional unknown options to be passed to it\n   179\u2192     * but does not throw a ResolveEndpointFailedException when creating the endpoint.\n   180\u2192     * <p/>\n   181\u2192     * This options is used by a few components for instance the HTTP based that can have dynamic URI options appended\n   182\u2192     * that is targeted for an external system.\n   183\u2192     * <p/>\n   184\u2192     * Most endpoints is configured to be <b>not</b> lenient.\n   185\u2192     *\n   186\u2192     * @return whether properties is lenient or not\n   187\u2192     */\n   188\u2192    boolean isLenientProperties();\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192    /**\n   191\u2192     * Whether this endpoint can connect to remote system, such as cloud providers, messaging brokers, databases. A\n   192\u2192     * local endpoint operates locally only, such as an internal message transformer, logger, or such as direct/seda\n   193\u2192     * components.\n   194\u2192     */\n   195\u2192    default boolean isRemote() {\n   196\u2192        return true;\n   197\u2192    }\n   198\u2192}\n   199\u2192"

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     3\u2192 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\n     4\u2192 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n     5\u2192 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n     6\u2192 * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n     7\u2192 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     8\u2192 *\n     9\u2192 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    10\u2192 *\n    11\u2192 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    12\u2192 * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192package org.apache.camel;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.UnitOfWork;\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192/**\n    22\u2192 * A consumer of message exchanges from an {@link Endpoint}.\n    23\u2192 * <p/>\n    24\u2192 * Important: Do not do any initialization in the constructor. Instead use\n    25\u2192 * {@link org.apache.camel.support.service.ServiceSupport#doInit()} or\n    26\u2192 * {@link org.apache.camel.support.service.ServiceSupport#doStart()}.\n    27\u2192 */\n    28\u2192public interface Consumer extends Service, EndpointAware {\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192    /**\n    31\u2192     * The processor that will process the {@link Exchange} that was consumed.\n    32\u2192     */\n    33\u2192    Processor getProcessor();\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192    /**\n    36\u2192     * Creates an {@link Exchange} that was consumed.\n    37\u2192     * <p/>\n    38\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> If the auto release parameter is set to <tt>false</tt> then the consumer is responsible for\n    39\u2192     * calling the {@link #releaseExchange(Exchange, boolean)} when the {@link Exchange} is done being routed. This is\n    40\u2192     * for advanced consumers that need to have this control in their own hands. For normal use-cases then a consumer\n    41\u2192     * can use autoRelease <tt>true</tt> and then Camel will automatic release the exchange after routing.\n    42\u2192     *\n    43\u2192     * @param autoRelease whether to auto release the exchange when routing is complete via {@link UnitOfWork}\n    44\u2192     */\n    45\u2192    Exchange createExchange(boolean autoRelease);\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192    /**\n    48\u2192     * Releases the {@link Exchange} when its completed processing and no longer needed.\n    49\u2192     *\n    50\u2192     * @param exchange    the exchange\n    51\u2192     * @param autoRelease whether the exchange was created with auto release\n    52\u2192     */\n    53\u2192    void releaseExchange(Exchange exchange, boolean autoRelease);\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192    /**\n    56\u2192     * The default callback to use with the consumer when calling the processor using asynchronous routing.\n    57\u2192     *\n    58\u2192     * This implementation will use {@link org.apache.camel.spi.ExceptionHandler} to handle any exception on the\n    59\u2192     * exchange and afterwards release the exchange.\n    60\u2192     *\n    61\u2192     * @param  exchange    the exchange\n    62\u2192     * @param  autoRelease whether the exchange was created with auto release\n    63\u2192     * @return             the default callback\n    64\u2192     */\n    65\u2192    default AsyncCallback defaultConsumerCallback(Exchange exchange, boolean autoRelease) {\n    66\u2192        return null;\n    67\u2192    }\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192}\n    70\u2192"

You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     8\u2192 *\n     9\u2192 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    10\u2192 *\n    11\u2192 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    12\u2192 * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192package org.apache.camel;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192/**\n    20\u2192 * Provides a channel on which clients can create and invoke message exchanges on an {@link Endpoint}.\n    21\u2192 * <p/>\n    22\u2192 * Important: Do not do any initialization in the constructor. Instead use\n    23\u2192 * {@link org.apache.camel.support.service.ServiceSupport#doInit()} or\n    24\u2192 * {@link org.apache.camel.support.service.ServiceSupport#doStart()}.\n    25\u2192 */\n    26\u2192public interface Producer extends Processor, Service, IsSingleton, EndpointAware {\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192}\n    29\u2192"

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     3\u2192 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\n     4\u2192 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n     5\u2192 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n     6\u2192 * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n     7\u2192 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     8\u2192 *\n     9\u2192 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    10\u2192 *\n    11\u2192 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    12\u2192 * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192package org.apache.camel;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192/**\n    20\u2192 * A <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/processor.html\">processor</a> is used to implement the\n    21\u2192 * <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/event-driven-consumer.html\"> Event Driven Consumer</a> and\n    22\u2192 * <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/message-translator.html\"> Message Translator</a> patterns and to process message\n    23\u2192 * exchanges.\n    24\u2192 * <p/>\n    25\u2192 * Notice if you use a {@link Processor} in a Camel route, then make sure to write the {@link Processor} in a\n    26\u2192 * thread-safe way, as the Camel routes can potentially be executed by concurrent threads, and therefore multiple\n    27\u2192 * threads can call the same {@link Processor} instance.\n    28\u2192 */\n    29\u2192@FunctionalInterface\n    30\u2192public interface Processor {\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192    /**\n    33\u2192     * Processes the message exchange\n    34\u2192     *\n    35\u2192     * @param  exchange  the message exchange\n    36\u2192     * @throws Exception if an internal processing error has occurred.\n    37\u2192     */\n    38\u2192    void process(Exchange exchange) throws Exception;\n    39\u2192}\n    40\u2192"
10. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/core/camel-api/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/Exchange.java"
}
Output
"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     3\u2192 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\n     4\u2192 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n     5\u2192 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n     6\u2192 * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n     7\u2192 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     8\u2192 *\n     9\u2192 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    10\u2192 *\n    11\u2192 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    12\u2192 * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192package org.apache.camel;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import java.util.Map;\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192import org.apache.camel.clock.Clock;\n    22\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.Metadata;\n    23\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.UnitOfWork;\n    24\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.annotations.ConstantProvider;\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192/**\n    27\u2192 * An Exchange is the message container holding the information during the entire routing of a {@link Message} received\n    28\u2192 * by a {@link Consumer}.\n    29\u2192 * <p/>\n    30\u2192 * During processing down the {@link Processor} chain, the {@link Exchange} provides access to the current (not the\n    31\u2192 * original) request and response {@link Message} messages. The {@link Exchange} also holds meta-data during its entire\n    32\u2192 * lifetime stored as properties accessible using the various {@link #getProperty(String)} methods. The\n    33\u2192 * {@link #setProperty(String, Object)} is used to store a property. For example, you can use this to store security,\n    34\u2192 * SLA related data or any other information deemed useful throughout processing. If an {@link Exchange} failed during\n    35\u2192 * routing the {@link Exception} that caused the failure is stored and accessible via the {@link #getException()}\n    36\u2192 * method.\n    37\u2192 * <p/>\n    38\u2192 * An Exchange is created when a {@link Consumer} receives a request. A new {@link Message} is created, the request is\n    39\u2192 * set as the body of the {@link Message} and depending on the {@link Consumer} other {@link Endpoint} and protocol\n    40\u2192 * related information is added as headers on the {@link Message}. Then an Exchange is created and the newly created\n    41\u2192 * {@link Message} is set as the in on the Exchange. Therefore, an Exchange starts its life in a {@link Consumer}. The\n    42\u2192 * Exchange is then sent down the {@link Route} for processing along a {@link Processor} chain. The {@link Processor} as\n    43\u2192 * the name suggests is what processes the {@link Message} in the Exchange and Camel, in addition to providing\n    44\u2192 * out-of-the-box a large number of useful processors, it also allows you to create your own. The rule Camel uses is to\n    45\u2192 * take the out {@link Message} produced by the previous {@link Processor} and set it as the in for the next\n    46\u2192 * {@link Processor}. If the previous {@link Processor} did not produce an out, then the in of the previous\n    47\u2192 * {@link Processor} is sent as the next in. At the end of the processing chain, depending on the {@link ExchangePattern\n    48\u2192 * Message Exchange Pattern} (or MEP) the last out (or in of no out available) is sent by the {@link Consumer} back to\n    49\u2192 * the original caller.\n    50\u2192 * <p/>\n    51\u2192 * Camel, in addition to providing out-of-the-box a large number of useful processors, it also allows you to implement\n    52\u2192 * and use your own. When the Exchange is passed to a {@link Processor}, it always contains an in {@link Message} and no\n    53\u2192 * out {@link Message}. The {@link Processor} <b>may</b> produce an out, depending on the nature of the\n    54\u2192 * {@link Processor}. The in {@link Message} can be accessed using the {@link #getIn()} method. Since the out message is\n    55\u2192 * null when entering the {@link Processor}, the {@link #getOut()} method is actually a convenient factory method that\n    56\u2192 * will lazily instantiate a {@link org.apache.camel.support.DefaultMessage} which you could populate. As an alternative\n    57\u2192 * you could also instantiate your specialized {@link Message} and set it on the exchange using the\n    58\u2192 * {@link #setOut(org.apache.camel.Message)} method. Please note that a {@link Message} contains not only the body but\n    59\u2192 * also headers and attachments. If you are creating a new {@link Message} the headers and attachments of the in\n    60\u2192 * {@link Message} are not automatically copied to the out by Camel, and you'll have to set the headers and attachments\n    61\u2192 * you need yourself. If your {@link Processor} is not producing a different {@link Message} but only needs to slightly\n    62\u2192 * modify the in, you can simply update the in {@link Message} returned by {@link #getIn()}.\n    63\u2192 * <p/>\n    64\u2192 * See this <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/using-getin-or-getout-methods-on-exchange.html\">FAQ entry</a> for more\n    65\u2192 * details.\n    66\u2192 */\n    67\u2192@ConstantProvider(\"org.apache.camel.ExchangeConstantProvider\")\n    68\u2192public interface Exchange extends VariableAware {\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192    String AUTHENTICATION = \"CamelAuthentication\";\n    71\u2192    String AUTHENTICATION_FAILURE_POLICY_ID = \"CamelAuthenticationFailurePolicyId\";\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"aggregate\", description = \"Number of exchanges that was grouped together.\", javaType = \"int\",\n    74\u2192              important = true)\n    75\u2192    String AGGREGATED_SIZE = \"CamelAggregatedSize\";\n    76\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"aggregate\", description = \"The time in millis this group will timeout\", javaType = \"long\")\n    77\u2192    String AGGREGATED_TIMEOUT = \"CamelAggregatedTimeout\";\n    78\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"aggregate\", description = \"Enum that tell how this group was completed\",\n    79\u2192              enums = \"consumer,force,interval,predicate,size,strategy,timeout\", javaType = \"String\")\n    80\u2192    String AGGREGATED_COMPLETED_BY = \"CamelAggregatedCompletedBy\";\n    81\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"aggregate\", description = \"The correlation key for this aggregation group\", javaType = \"String\",\n    82\u2192              important = true)\n    83\u2192    String AGGREGATED_CORRELATION_KEY = \"CamelAggregatedCorrelationKey\";\n    84\u2192    String AGGREGATED_COLLECTION_GUARD = \"CamelAggregatedCollectionGuard\";\n    85\u2192    String AGGREGATION_STRATEGY = \"CamelAggregationStrategy\";\n    86\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"consumer,aggregate\",\n    87\u2192              description = \"Input property. Set to true to force completing the current group. This allows to overrule any existing completion predicates, sizes, timeouts etc, and complete the group.\",\n    88\u2192              javaType = \"boolean\")\n    89\u2192    String AGGREGATION_COMPLETE_CURRENT_GROUP = \"CamelAggregationCompleteCurrentGroup\";\n    90\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"consumer,aggregate\",\n    91\u2192              description = \"Input property. Set to true to force completing all the groups (excluding this message). This allows to overrule any existing completion predicates, sizes, timeouts etc, and complete the group.\"\n    92\u2192                            + \" This message is considered a signal message only, the message headers/contents will not be processed otherwise. Instead use CamelAggregationCompleteAllGroupsInclusive if this message should be included in the aggregator.\",\n    93\u2192              javaType = \"boolean\")\n    94\u2192    String AGGREGATION_COMPLETE_ALL_GROUPS = \"CamelAggregationCompleteAllGroups\";\n    95\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"consumer,aggregate\",\n    96\u2192              description = \"Input property. Set to true to force completing all the groups (including this message). This allows to overrule any existing completion predicates, sizes, timeouts etc, and complete the group.\",\n    97\u2192              javaType = \"boolean\")\n    98\u2192    String AGGREGATION_COMPLETE_ALL_GROUPS_INCLUSIVE = \"CamelAggregationCompleteAllGroupsInclusive\";\n    99\u2192    String ASYNC_WAIT = \"CamelAsyncWait\";\n   100\u2192    @Metadata(description = \"The number of Multipart files uploaded with a single request\")\n   101\u2192    String ATTACHMENTS_SIZE = \"CamelAttachmentsSize\";\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192    String BATCH_INDEX = \"CamelBatchIndex\";\n   104\u2192    String BATCH_SIZE = \"CamelBatchSize\";\n   105\u2192    String BATCH_COMPLETE = \"CamelBatchComplete\";\n   106\u2192    String BINDING = \"CamelBinding\";\n   107\u2192    // do not prefix with Camel and use a lower-case starting letter as it's a shared key\n   108\u2192    // used across other Apache products such as AMQ, SMX etc.\n   109\u2192    String BREADCRUMB_ID = \"breadcrumbId\";\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192    String CHARSET_NAME = \"CamelCharsetName\";\n   112\u2192    @Deprecated(since = \"4.5.0\")\n   113\u2192    String CIRCUIT_BREAKER_STATE = \"CamelCircuitBreakerState\";\n   114\u2192    String CLAIM_CHECK_REPOSITORY = \"CamelClaimCheckRepository\";\n   115\u2192    String CONTENT_ENCODING = \"Content-Encoding\";\n   116\u2192    String CONTENT_LENGTH = \"Content-Length\";\n   117\u2192    @Metadata(important = true)\n   118\u2192    String CONTENT_TYPE = \"Content-Type\";\n   119\u2192    String COOKIE_HANDLER = \"CamelCookieHandler\";\n   120\u2192    String CORRELATION_ID = \"CamelCorrelationId\";\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192    // The schema of the message payload\n   123\u2192    String CONTENT_SCHEMA = \"CamelContentSchema\";\n   124\u2192    // The schema type of the message payload (json schema, avro, etc)\n   125\u2192    String CONTENT_SCHEMA_TYPE = \"CamelContentSchemaType\";\n   126\u2192\n 132\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"idempotentConsumer\",\n   133\u2192              description = \"Whether this exchange is a duplicate detected by the Idempotent Consumer EIP\",\n   134\u2192 The counter starts from 0.\",\n   239\u2192              javaType = \"int\")\n   240\u2192    String MULTICAST_INDEX = \"CamelMulticastIndex\";\n   241\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"multicast\", be propagated to any remote system supporting the LRA framework\n   268\u2192    String SAGA_LONG_RUNNING_ACTION = \"Long-Running-Action\";\n   269\u2192    String SCHEDULER_POLLED_MESSAGES = SKIP_WWW_FORM_URLENCODED = \"CamelSkipWwwFormUrlEncoding\";\n   273\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"routingSlip\", description = \"The endpoint uri of this routing slip\", javaType = \"String\")\n   274\u2192    String SLIP_ENDPOINT = \"CamelSlipEndpoint\";\n   275\u2192    String SLIP_PRODUCER = \"CamelSlipProducer\";\n   276\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"split\",\n   277\u2192 The counter starts from 0.\",\n   278\u2192              javaType = \"int\",\n   279\u2192              important = true)\n   280\u2192    String SPLIT_INDEX = \"CamelSplitIndex\";\n   281\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"split\", description = \"Whether this Exchange is the last.\", javaType = \"boolean\")\n   282\u2192    String SPLIT_COMPLETE = \"CamelSplitComplete\";\n   283\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"split\",\n   284\u2192              description = \"The total number of Exchanges that was split. This property is not applied for stream based splitting, except for the very last message because then Camel knows the total size.\",\n   285\u2192              javaType = \"int\",\n   286\u2192              important = true)\n   287\u2192    String SPLIT_SIZE = \"CamelSplitSize\";\n   288\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"step\", description = \"The id of the Step EIP\", javaType = \"String\")\n   289\u2192    String STEP_ID = \"CamelStepId\";\n   290\u2192\n   291\u2192    @Metadata(important = true)\n   292\u2192    String TIMER_COUNTER = \"CamelTimerCounter\";\n   293\u2192    String \"CamelTimerTime\";\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"enrich,multicast,pollEnrich,recipientList,routingSlip,toD,to,wireTap\",\n   299\u2192              description = \"Endpoint URI where this Exchange is being sent to\", javaType = \"String\")\n   300\u2192    String TO_ENDPOINT = \"CamelToEndpoint\";\n   301\u2192    @Deprecated(since = \"4.0.0\")\n   302\u2192    String \"CamelXsltWarning\";\n   319\u2192\n   320\u2192    // special for camel-tracing/open-telemetry\n   321\u2192    String OTEL_ACTIVE_SPAN = \"OpenTracing.activeSpan\";\n   322\u2192    String OTEL_CLOSE_CLIENT_SCOPE = \"OpenTracing.closeClientScope\";\n   323\u2192\n   324\u2192    /**\n   325\u2192     * Returns the {@link ExchangePattern} (MEP) of this exchange.\n   326\u2192     *\n   327\u2192     * @return the message exchange pattern of this exchange\n   328\u2192     */\n   329\u2192    ExchangePattern getPattern();\n   330\u2192\n   331\u2192    /**\n   332\u2192     * Allows the {@link ExchangePattern} (MEP) of this exchange to be customized.\n   333\u2192     *\n   334\u2192     * This typically won't be required as an exchange can be created with a specific MEP by calling\n   335\u2192     * {@link Endpoint#createExchange(ExchangePattern)} but it is here just in case it is needed.\n   336\u2192     *\n   337\u2192     * @param pattern the pattern\n   338\u2192     */\n   339\u2192    void setPattern(ExchangePattern pattern);\n   340\u2192\n   341\u2192    /**\n   342\u2192     * Returns a property associated with this exchange by the key\n   343\u2192     *\n   344\u2192     * @param  key the exchange key\n   345\u2192     * @return     the value of the given property or <tt>null</tt> if there is no property for the given key\n   346\u2192     */\n   347\u2192    Object getProperty(ExchangePropertyKey key);\n   348\u2192\n   349\u2192    /**\n   350\u2192     * Returns a property associated with this exchange by the key and specifying the type required\n   351\u2192     *\n   352\u2192     * @param  key  the exchange key\n   353\u2192     * @param  type the type of the property\n   354\u2192     * @return      the value of the given property or <tt>null</tt> if there is no property for the given name or\n   355\u2192     *              <tt>null</tt> if it cannot be converted to the given type\n   356\u2192     */\n   357\u2192    <T> T getProperty(ExchangePropertyKey key, Class<T> type);\n   358\u2192\n   359\u2192    /**\n   360\u2192     * Returns a property associated with this exchange by name and specifying the type required\n   361\u2192     *\n   362\u2192     * @param  key          the exchange key\n   363\u2192     * @param  defaultValue the default value to return if property was absent\n   364\u2192     * @param  type         the type of the property\n   365\u2192     * @return              the value of the given property or <tt>defaultValue</tt> if there is no property for the\n   366\u2192     *                      given name or <tt>null</tt> if it cannot be converted to the given type\n   367\u2192     */\n   368\u2192    <T> T getProperty(ExchangePropertyKey key, Object defaultValue, Class<T> type);\n   369\u2192\n   370\u2192    /**\n   371\u2192     * Sets a property on the exchange\n   372\u2192     *\n   373\u2192     * @param key   the exchange key\n   374\u2192     * @param value to associate with the name\n   375\u2192     */\n   376\u2192    void setProperty(ExchangePropertyKey key, Object value);\n   377\u2192\n   378\u2192    /**\n   379\u2192     * Removes the given property on the exchange\n   380\u2192     *\n   381\u2192     * @param  key the exchange key\n   382\u2192     * @return     the old value of the property\n   383\u2192     */\n   384\u2192    Object removeProperty(ExchangePropertyKey key);\n   385\u2192\n   386\u2192    /**\n   387\u2192     * Returns a property associated with this exchange by name\n   388\u2192     *\n   389\u2192     * @param  name the name of the property\n   390\u2192     * @return      the value of the given property or <tt>null</tt> if there is no property for the given name\n   391\u2192     */\n   392\u2192    Object getProperty(String name);\n   393\u2192\n   394\u2192    /**\n   395\u2192     * Returns a property associated with this exchange by name and specifying the type required\n   396\u2192     *\n   397\u2192     * @param  name the name of the property\n   398\u2192     * @param  type the type of the property\n   399\u2192     * @return      the value of the given property or <tt>null</tt> if there is no property for the given name or\n   400\u2192     *              <tt>null</tt> if it cannot be converted to the given type\n   401\u2192     */\n   402\u2192    <T> T getProperty(String name, Class<T> type);\n   403\u2192\n   404\u2192    /**\n   405\u2192     * Returns a property associated with this exchange by name and specifying the type required\n   406\u2192     *\n   407\u2192     * @param  name         the name of the property\n   408\u2192     * @param  defaultValue the default value to return if property was absent\n   409\u2192     * @param  type         the type of the property\n   410\u2192     * @return              the value of the given property or <tt>defaultValue</tt> if there is no property for the\n   411\u2192     *                      given name or <tt>null</tt> if it cannot be converted to the given type\n   412\u2192     */\n   413\u2192    <T> T getProperty(String name, Object defaultValue, Class<T> type);\n   414\u2192\n   415\u2192    /**\n   416\u2192     * Sets a property on the exchange\n   417\u2192     *\n   418\u2192     * @param name  of the property\n   419\u2192     * @param value to associate with the name\n   420\u2192     */\n   421\u2192    void setProperty(String name, Object value);\n   422\u2192\n   423\u2192    /**\n   424\u2192     * Removes the given property on the exchange\n   425\u2192     *\n   426\u2192     * @param  name of the property\n   427\u2192     * @return      the old value of the property\n   428\u2192     */\n   429\u2192    Object removeProperty(String name);\n   430\u2192\n   431\u2192    /**\n   432\u2192     * Remove all the properties associated with the exchange matching a specific pattern\n   433\u2192     *\n   434\u2192     * @param  pattern pattern of names\n   435\u2192     * @return         boolean whether any properties matched\n   436\u2192     */\n   437\u2192    boolean removeProperties(String pattern);\n   438\u2192\n   439\u2192    /**\n   440\u2192     * Removes the properties from this exchange that match the given <tt>pattern</tt>, except for the ones matching one\n   441\u2192     * or more <tt>excludePatterns</tt>\n   442\u2192     *\n   443\u2192     * @param  pattern         pattern of names that should be removed\n   444\u2192     * @param  excludePatterns one or more pattern of properties names that should be excluded (= preserved)\n   445\u2192     * @return                 boolean whether any properties matched\n   446\u2192     */\n   447\u2192    boolean removeProperties(String pattern, String... excludePatterns);\n   448\u2192\n   449\u2192    /**\n   450\u2192     * Returns the properties associated with the exchange\n   451\u2192     *\n   452\u2192     * @return the properties in a Map\n   453\u2192     * @see    #getAllProperties()\n   454\u2192     */\n   455\u2192    Map<String, Object> getProperties();\n   456\u2192\n   457\u2192    /**\n   458\u2192     * Returns all (both internal and custom) properties associated with the exchange\n   459\u2192     *\n   460\u2192     * @return all (both internal and custom) properties in a Map\n   461\u2192     * @see    #getProperties()\n   462\u2192     */\n   463\u2192    Map<String, Object> getAllProperties();\n   464\u2192\n   465\u2192    /**\n   466\u2192     * Returns whether any properties have been set\n   467\u2192     *\n   468\u2192     * @return <tt>true</tt> if any property has been set\n   469\u2192     */\n   470\u2192    boolean hasProperties();\n   471\u2192\n   472\u2192    /**\n   473\u2192     * Returns a variable by name\n   474\u2192     *\n   475\u2192     * @param  name the variable name. Can be prefixed with repo-id:name to lookup the variable from a specific\n   476\u2192     *              repository. If no repo-id is provided, then variables will be from the current exchange.\n   477\u2192     * @return      the value of the given variable or <tt>null</tt> if there is no variable for the given name\n   478\u2192     */\n   479\u2192    Object getVariable(String name);\n   480\u2192\n   481\u2192    /**\n   482\u2192     * Returns a variable by name and specifying the type required\n   483\u2192     *\n   484\u2192     * @param  name the variable name. Can be prefixed with repo-id:name to lookup the variable from a specific\n   485\u2192     *              repository. If no repo-id is provided, then variables will be from the current exchange.\n   486\u2192     * @param  type the type of the variable\n   487\u2192     * @return      the value of the given variable or <tt>null</tt> if there is no variable for the given name or\n   488\u2192     *              <tt>null</tt> if it cannot be converted to the given type\n   489\u2192     */\n   490\u2192    <T> T getVariable(String name, Class<T> type);\n   491\u2192\n   492\u2192    /**\n   493\u2192     * Returns a variable by name and specifying the type required\n   494\u2192     *\n   495\u2192     * @param  name         the variable name. Can be prefixed with repo-id:name to look up the variable from a specific\n   496\u2192     *                      repository. If no repo-id is provided, then variables will be from the current exchange.\n   497\u2192     * @param  defaultValue the default value to return if variable was absent\n   498\u2192     * @param  type         the type of the variable\n   499\u2192     * @return              the value of the given variable or <tt>defaultValue</tt> if there is no variable for the\n   500\u2192     *                      given name or <tt>null</tt> if it cannot be converted to the given type\n   501\u2192     */\n   502\u2192    <T> T getVariable(String name, Object defaultValue, Class<T> type);\n   503\u2192\n   504\u2192    /**\n   505\u2192     * Sets a variable on the exchange\n   506\u2192     *\n   507\u2192     * @param name  the variable name. Can be prefixed with repo-id:name to store the variable in a specific repository.\n   508\u2192     *              If no repo-id is provided, then variables will be stored in the current exchange.\n   509\u2192     * @param value the value of the variable\n   510\u2192     */\n   511\u2192    void setVariable(String name, Object value);\n   512\u2192\n   513\u2192    /**\n   514\u2192     * Removes the given variable\n   515\u2192     *\n   516\u2192     * If the name is <tt>*</tt> then all variables from the current exchange is removed, and null is returned.\n   517\u2192     *\n   518\u2192     * @param  name the variable name. Can be prefixed with repo-id:name to remove the variable in a specific\n   519\u2192     *              repository. If no repo-id is provided, then the variable from the current exchange will be removed\n   520\u2192     * @return      the old value of the variable, or <tt>null</tt> if there was no variable for the given name\n   521\u2192     */\n   522\u2192    Object removeVariable(String name);\n   523\u2192\n   524\u2192    /**\n   525\u2192     * Returns the variables from the current exchange\n   526\u2192     *\n   527\u2192     * @return the variables from the current exchange in a Map.\n   528\u2192     */\n   529\u2192    Map<String, Object> getVariables();\n   530\u2192\n   531\u2192    /**\n   532\u2192     * Returns whether any variables have been set on the current exchange\n   533\u2192     *\n   534\u2192     * @return <tt>true</tt> if any variables has been set on the current exchange\n   535\u2192     */\n   536\u2192    boolean hasVariables();\n   537\u2192\n   538\u2192    /**\n   539\u2192     * Returns the inbound request message\n   540\u2192     *\n   541\u2192     * @return the message\n   542\u2192     */\n   543\u2192    Message getIn();\n   544\u2192\n   545\u2192    /**\n   546\u2192     * Returns the current message\n   547\u2192     *\n   548\u2192     * @return the current message\n   549\u2192     */\n   550\u2192    Message getMessage();\n   551\u2192\n   552\u2192    /**\n   553\u2192     * Returns the current message as the given type\n   554\u2192     *\n   555\u2192     * @param  type the given type\n   556\u2192     * @return      the message as the given type or <tt>null</tt> if not possible to covert to given type\n   557\u2192     */\n   558\u2192    <T> T getMessage(Class<T> type);\n   559\u2192\n   560\u2192    /**\n   561\u2192     * Replace the current message instance.\n   562\u2192     *\n   563\u2192     * @param message the new message\n   564\u2192     */\n   565\u2192    void setMessage(Message message);\n   566\u2192\n   567\u2192    /**\n   568\u2192     * Returns the inbound request message as the given type\n   569\u2192     *\n   570\u2192     * @param  type the given type\n   571\u2192     * @return      the message as the given type or <tt>null</tt> if not possible to covert to given type\n   572\u2192     */\n   573\u2192    <T> T getIn(Class<T> type);\n   574\u2192\n   575\u2192    /**\n   576\u2192     * Sets the inbound message instance\n   577\u2192     *\n   578\u2192     * @param in the inbound message\n   579\u2192     */\n   580\u2192    void setIn(Message in);\n   581\u2192\n   582\u2192    /**\n   583\u2192     * Returns the outbound message, lazily creating one if one has not already been associated with this exchange.\n   584\u2192     * <p/>\n   585\u2192     * <br/>\n   586\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> If you want to change the current message, then use {@link #getIn()} instead as it will ensure\n   587\u2192     * headers etc. is kept and propagated when routing continues. Bottom line end users should rarely use this method.\n   588\u2192     * <p/>\n   589\u2192     * <br/>\n   590\u2192     * If you want to test whether an OUT message has been set or not, use the {@link #hasOut()} method.\n   591\u2192     * <p/>\n   592\u2192     * See also the class Javadoc for this {@link Exchange} for more details and this\n   593\u2192     * <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/using-getin-or-getout-methods-on-exchange.html\">FAQ entry</a>.\n   594\u2192     *\n   595\u2192     * @return     the response\n   596\u2192     * @see        #getIn()\n   597\u2192     * @deprecated use {@link #getMessage()}\n   598\u2192     */\n   599\u2192    @Deprecated(since = \"3.0.0\")\n   600\u2192    Message getOut();\n   601\u2192\n   602\u2192    /**\n   603\u2192     * Returns the outbound request message as the given type\n   604\u2192     * <p/>\n   605\u2192     * <br/>\n   606\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> If you want to change the current message, then use {@link #getIn()} instead as it will ensure\n   607\u2192     * headers etc. is kept and propagated when routing continues. Bottom line end users should rarely use this method.\n   608\u2192     * <p/>\n   609\u2192     * <br/>\n   610\u2192     * If you want to test whether an OUT message has been set or not, use the {@link #hasOut()} method.\n   611\u2192     * <p/>\n   612\u2192     * See also the class Javadoc for this {@link Exchange} for more details and this\n   613\u2192     * <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/using-getin-or-getout-methods-on-exchange.html\">FAQ entry</a>.\n   614\u2192     *\n   615\u2192     * @param      type the given type\n   616\u2192     * @return          the message as the given type or <tt>null</tt> if not possible to covert to given type\n   617\u2192     * @see             #getIn(Class)\n   618\u2192     * @deprecated      use {@link #getMessage(Class)}\n   619\u2192     */\n   620\u2192    @Deprecated(since = \"3.0.0\")\n   621\u2192    <T> T getOut(Class<T> type);\n   622\u2192\n   623\u2192    /**\n   624\u2192     * Returns whether an OUT message has been set or not.\n   625\u2192     *\n   626\u2192     * @return     <tt>true</tt> if an OUT message exists, <tt>false</tt> otherwise.\n   627\u2192     * @deprecated use {@link #getMessage()}\n   628\u2192     */\n   629\u2192    @Deprecated(since = \"3.0.0\")\n   630\u2192    boolean hasOut();\n   631\u2192\n   632\u2192    /**\n   633\u2192     * Sets the outbound message\n   634\u2192     *\n   635\u2192     * @param      out the outbound message\n   636\u2192     * @deprecated     use {@link #setMessage(Message)}\n   637\u2192     */\n   638\u2192    @Deprecated(since = \"3.0.0\")\n   639\u2192    void setOut(Message out);\n   640\u2192\n   641\u2192    /**\n   642\u2192     * Returns the exception associated with this exchange\n   643\u2192     *\n   644\u2192     * @return the exception (or null if no faults)\n   645\u2192     */\n   646\u2192    Exception getException();\n   647\u2192\n   648\u2192    /**\n   649\u2192     * Returns the exception associated with this exchange.\n   650\u2192     * <p/>\n   651\u2192     * Is used to get the caused exception that typically have been wrapped in some sort of Camel wrapper exception\n   652\u2192     * <p/>\n   653\u2192     * The strategy is to look in the exception hierarchy to find the first given cause that matches the type. Will\n   654\u2192     * start from the bottom (the real cause) and walk upwards.\n   655\u2192     *\n   656\u2192     * @param  type the exception type\n   657\u2192     * @return      the exception (or <tt>null</tt> if no caused exception matched)\n   658\u2192     */\n   659\u2192    <T> T getException(Class<T> type);\n   660\u2192\n   661\u2192    /**\n   662\u2192     * Sets the exception associated with this exchange\n   663\u2192     * <p/>\n   664\u2192     * Camel will wrap {@link Throwable} into {@link Exception} type to accommodate for the {@link #getException()}\n   665\u2192     * method returning a plain {@link Exception} type.\n   666\u2192     *\n   667\u2192     * @param t the caused exception\n   668\u2192     */\n   669\u2192    void setException(Throwable t);\n   670\u2192\n   671\u2192    /**\n   672\u2192     * Returns true if this exchange failed due to an exception\n   673\u2192     *\n   674\u2192     * @return true if this exchange failed due to an exception\n   675\u2192     * @see    Exchange#getException()\n   676\u2192     */\n   677\u2192    boolean isFailed();\n   678\u2192\n   679\u2192    /**\n   680\u2192     * Returns true if this exchange is transacted\n   681\u2192     */\n   682\u2192    boolean isTransacted();\n   683\u2192\n   684\u2192    /**\n   685\u2192     * Returns true if this exchange is marked to stop and not continue routing.\n   686\u2192     */\n   687\u2192    boolean isRouteStop();\n   688\u2192\n   689\u2192    /**\n   690\u2192     * Sets whether this exchange is marked to stop and not continue routing.\n   691\u2192     *\n   692\u2192     * @param routeStop <tt>true</tt> to stop routing\n   693\u2192     */\n   694\u2192    void setRouteStop(boolean routeStop);\n   695\u2192\n   696\u2192    /**\n   697\u2192     * Returns true if this exchange is an external initiated redelivered message (such as a JMS broker).\n   698\u2192     * <p/>\n   699\u2192     * <b>Important: </b> It is not always possible to determine if the message is a redelivery or not, and therefore\n   700\u2192     * <tt>false</tt> is returned. Such an example would be a JDBC message. However JMS brokers provides details if a\n   701\u2192     * message is redelivered.\n   702\u2192     *\n   703\u2192     * @return <tt>true</tt> if redelivered, <tt>false</tt> if not or not able to determine\n   704\u2192     */\n   705\u2192    boolean isExternalRedelivered();\n   706\u2192\n   707\u2192    /**\n   708\u2192     * Returns true if this exchange is marked for rollback\n   709\u2192     */\n   710\u2192    boolean isRollbackOnly();\n   711\u2192\n   712\u2192    /**\n   713\u2192     * Sets whether to mark this exchange for rollback\n   714\u2192     */\n   715\u2192    void setRollbackOnly(boolean rollbackOnly);\n   716\u2192\n   717\u2192    /**\n   718\u2192     * Returns true if this exchange is marked for rollback (only last transaction section)\n   719\u2192     */\n   720\u2192    boolean isRollbackOnlyLast();\n   721\u2192\n   722\u2192    /**\n   723\u2192     * Sets whether to mark this exchange for rollback (only last transaction section)\n   724\u2192     */\n   725\u2192    void setRollbackOnlyLast(boolean rollbackOnlyLast);\n   726\u2192\n   727\u2192    /**\n   728\u2192     * Returns the container so that a processor can resolve endpoints from URIs\n   729\u2192     *\n   730\u2192     * @return the container which owns this exchange\n   731\u2192     */\n   732\u2192    CamelContext getContext();\n   733\u2192\n   734\u2192    /**\n   735\u2192     * Creates a copy of the current message exchange so that it can be forwarded to another destination\n   736\u2192     */\n   737\u2192    Exchange copy();\n   738\u2192\n   739\u2192    /**\n   740\u2192     * Returns the endpoint which originated this message exchange if a consumer on an endpoint created the message\n   741\u2192     * exchange, otherwise his property will be <tt>null</tt>.\n   742\u2192     *\n   743\u2192     * Note: In case this message exchange has been cloned through another parent message exchange (which itself has\n   744\u2192     * been created through the consumer of it's own endpoint), then if desired one could still retrieve the consumer\n   745\u2192     * endpoint of such a parent message exchange as the following:\n   746\u2192     *\n   747\u2192     * <pre>\n   748\u2192     * getContext().getRoute(getFromRouteId()).getEndpoint()\n   749\u2192     * </pre>\n   750\u2192     */\n   751\u2192    Endpoint getFromEndpoint();\n   752\u2192\n   753\u2192    /**\n   754\u2192     * Returns the route id which originated this message exchange if a route consumer on an endpoint created the\n   755\u2192     * message exchange, otherwise his property will be <tt>null</tt>.\n   756\u2192     *\n   757\u2192     * Note: In case this message exchange has been cloned through another parent message exchange then this method\n   758\u2192     * would return the <tt>fromRouteId<tt> property of that exchange.\n   759\u2192     */\n   760\u2192    String getFromRouteId();\n   761\u2192\n   762\u2192    /**\n   763\u2192     * Returns the unit of work that this exchange belongs to; which may map to zero, one or more physical transactions\n   764\u2192     */\n   765\u2192    UnitOfWork getUnitOfWork();\n   766\u2192\n   767\u2192    /**\n   768\u2192     * Returns the exchange id (unique)\n   769\u2192     */\n   770\u2192    String getExchangeId();\n   771\u2192\n   772\u2192    /**\n   773\u2192     * Set the exchange id\n   774\u2192     */\n   775\u2192    void setExchangeId(String id);\n   776\u2192\n   777\u2192    /**\n   778\u2192     * Gets the timestamp in millis when this exchange was created.\n   779\u2192     *\n   780\u2192     * @see Clock#getCreated()\n   781\u2192     */\n   782\u2192    @Deprecated(since = \"4.4.0\")\n   783\u2192    long getCreated();\n   784\u2192\n   785\u2192    /**\n   786\u2192     * Gets the {@link ExchangeExtension} that contains the extension points for internal exchange APIs. These APIs are\n   787\u2192     * intended for internal usage within Camel and end-users should avoid using them.\n   788\u2192     *\n   789\u2192     * @return the {@link ExchangeExtension} point for this exchange.\n   790\u2192     */\n   791\u2192    ExchangeExtension getExchangeExtension();\n   792\u2192\n   793\u2192    /**\n   794\u2192     * Gets {@link Clock} that holds time information about the exchange\n   795\u2192     */\n   796\u2192    Clock getClock();\n   797\u2192\n   798\u2192}\n   799\u2192"

19\u2192import java.util.Collection;\n    20\u2192import java.util.List;\n    21\u2192import java.util.Map;\n    22\u2192import java.util.Set;\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192import org.apache.camel.resume.ConsumerListener;\n    25\u2192import org.apache.camel.resume.ResumeStrategy;\n    26\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.InterceptStrategy;\n    27\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.ManagementInterceptStrategy;\n    28\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.Resource;\n    29\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.RouteController;\n    30\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.RouteError;\n    31\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.RoutePolicy;\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192/**\n    34\u2192 * A <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/routes.html\">Route</a> defines the processing used on an inbound message exchange\n    35\u2192 * from a specific {@link org.apache.camel.Endpoint} within a {@link org.apache.camel.CamelContext}.\n    36\u2192 * <p/>\n    37\u2192 * Use the API from {@link org.apache.camel.CamelContext} to control the lifecycle of a route, such as starting and\n    38\u2192 * stopping using the {@link org.apache.camel.spi.RouteController#startRoute(String)} and\n    39\u2192 * {@link org.apache.camel.spi.RouteController#stopRoute(String)} methods.\n    40\u2192 */\n    41\u2192public interface Route extends RuntimeConfiguration {\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192    String ID_PROPERTY = \"id\";\n    44\u2192    String CUSTOM_ID_PROPERTY = \"customId\";\n    45\u2192    String PARENT_PROPERTY = \"parent\";\n    46\u2192    String GROUP_PROPERTY = \"group\";\n    47\u2192    String NODE_PREFIX_ID_PROPERTY = \"nodePrefixId\";\n    48\u2192    String REST_PROPERTY = \"rest\";\n    49\u2192    String TEMPLATE_PROPERTY = \"template\";\n    50\u2192    String KAMELET_PROPERTY = \"kamelet\";\n    51\u2192    String DESCRIPTION_PROPERTY = \"description\";\n    52\u2192    String NOTE_PROPERTY = \"note\";\n    53\u2192    String CONFIGURATION_ID_PROPERTY = \"configurationId\";\n    54\u2192    String SUPERVISED = \"supervised\";\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192    /**\n    57\u2192     * Gets the route id\n    58\u2192     *\n    59\u2192     * @return the route id\n    60\u2192     */\n    61\u2192    String getId();\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192    /**\n    64\u2192     * Gets the node prefix id\n    65\u2192     */\n    66\u2192    String getNodePrefixId();\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192    /**\n    69\u2192     * Whether the route id is custom assigned or auto assigned\n    70\u2192     *\n    71\u2192     * @return true if custom id, false if auto assigned id\n    72\u2192     */\n    73\u2192    boolean isCustomId();\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192    /**\n    76\u2192     * Whether this route is a Rest DSL route.\n    77\u2192     */\n    78\u2192    boolean isCreatedByRestDsl();\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192    /**\n    81\u2192     * Whether this route was created from a route template (or a Kamelet).\n    82\u2192     */\n    83\u2192    boolean isCreatedByRouteTemplate();\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192    /**\n    86\u2192     * Whether this route was created from a Kamelet.\n    87\u2192     */\n    88\u2192    boolean isCreatedByKamelet();\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192    /**\n    91\u2192     * Gets the route group\n    92\u2192     *\n    93\u2192     * @return the route group\n    94\u2192     */\n    95\u2192    String getGroup();\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192    /**\n    98\u2192     * Gets the uptime in a human-readable format\n    99\u2192     *\n   100\u2192     * @return the uptime in days/hours/minutes\n   101\u2192     */\n   102\u2192    String getUptime();\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192    /**\n   105\u2192     * Gets the uptime in milliseconds\n   106\u2192     *\n   107\u2192     * @return the uptime in milliseconds\n   108\u2192     */\n   109\u2192    long getUptimeMillis();\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192    /**\n   112\u2192     * Gets the inbound {@link Consumer}\n   113\u2192     *\n   114\u2192     * @return the inbound consumer\n   115\u2192     */\n   116\u2192    Consumer getConsumer();\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192    /**\n   119\u2192     * Gets the {@link Processor}\n   120\u2192     *\n   121\u2192     * @return the processor\n   122\u2192     */\n   123\u2192    Processor getProcessor();\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192    /**\n   126\u2192     * Sets the {@link Processor}\n   127\u2192     */\n   128\u2192    void setProcessor(Processor processor);\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192    /**\n   131\u2192     * Whether or not the route supports suspension (suspend and resume)\n   132\u2192     *\n   133\u2192     * @return <tt>true</tt> if this route supports suspension\n   134\u2192     */\n   135\u2192    boolean supportsSuspension();\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192    /**\n   138\u2192     * This property map is used to associate information about the route.\n   139\u2192     *\n   140\u2192     * @return properties\n   141\u2192     */\n   142\u2192    Map<String, Object> getProperties();\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192    /**\n   145\u2192     * Gets the route description (if any has been configured).\n   146\u2192     * <p/>\n   147\u2192     * The description is configured using the {@link #DESCRIPTION_PROPERTY} as key in the {@link #getProperties()}.\n   148\u2192     *\n   149\u2192     * @return the description, or <tt>null</tt> if no description has been configured.\n   150\u2192     */\n   151\u2192    String getDescription();\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192    /**\n   154\u2192     * Gets the route note (if any has been configured).\n   155\u2192     * <p/>\n   156\u2192     * The note is configured using the {@link #NOTE_PROPERTY} as key in the {@link #getProperties()}.\n   157\u2192     *\n   158\u2192     * @return the note, or <tt>null</tt> if no note has been configured.\n   159\u2192     */\n   160\u2192    String getNote();\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192    /**\n   163\u2192     * Gets the route configuration id(s) the route has been applied with. Multiple ids is separated by comma.\n   164\u2192     * <p/>\n   165\u2192     * The configuration ids is configured using the {@link #CONFIGURATION_ID_PROPERTY} as key in the\n   166\u2192     * {@link #getProperties()}.\n   167\u2192     *\n   168\u2192     * @return the configuration, or <tt>null</tt> if no configuration has been configured.\n   169\u2192     */\n   170\u2192    String getConfigurationId();\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192    /**\n   173\u2192     * Gets the source resource that this route is located from\n   174\u2192     *\n   175\u2192     * @return the source, or null if this route is not loaded from a resource\n   176\u2192     */\n   177\u2192    Resource getSourceResource();\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192    /**\n   180\u2192     * The source:line-number where the route input is located in the source code\n   181\u2192     */\n   182\u2192    String getSourceLocation();\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192    /**\n   185\u2192     * The source:line-number in short format that can be used for logging or summary purposes.\n   186\u2192     */\n   187\u2192    String getSourceLocationShort();\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192    /**\n   190\u2192     * Gets the camel context\n   191\u2192     *\n   192\u2192     * @return the camel context\n   193\u2192     */\n   194\u2192    CamelContext getCamelContext();\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192    /**\n   197\u2192     * Gets the input endpoint for this route.\n   198\u2192     *\n   199\u2192     * @return the endpoint\n   200\u2192     */\n   201\u2192    Endpoint getEndpoint();\n   202\u2192\n   203\u2192    /**\n   204\u2192     * A strategy callback allowing special initialization when services are initializing.\n   205\u2192     *\n   206\u2192     * @throws Exception is thrown in case of error\n   207\u2192     */\n   208\u2192    void initializeServices() throws Exception;\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192    /**\n   211\u2192     * Returns the services for this particular route\n   212\u2192     *\n   213\u2192     * @return the services\n   214\u2192     */\n   215\u2192    List<Service> getServices();\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192    /**\n   218\u2192     * Adds a service to this route\n   219\u2192     *\n   220\u2192     * @param service the service\n   221\u2192     */\n   222\u2192    void addService(Service service);\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192    /**\n   225\u2192     * Adds a service to this route\n   226\u2192     *\n   227\u2192     * @param service   the service\n   228\u2192     * @param forceStop whether to force stopping the service when the route stops\n   229\u2192     */\n   230\u2192    void addService(Service service, boolean forceStop);\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192    /**\n   233\u2192     * Returns a navigator to navigate this route by navigating all the {@link Processor}s.\n   234\u2192     *\n   235\u2192     * @return a navigator for {@link Processor}.\n   236\u2192     */\n   237\u2192    Navigate<Processor> navigate();\n   238\u2192\n   239\u2192    /**\n   240\u2192     * Returns a list of all the {@link Processor}s from this route that has id's matching the pattern\n   241\u2192     *\n   242\u2192     * @param  pattern the pattern to match by ids\n   243\u2192     * @return         a list of {@link Processor}, is never <tt>null</tt>.\n   244\u2192     */\n   245\u2192    List<Processor> filter(String pattern);\n   246\u2192\n   247\u2192    /**\n   248\u2192     * Callback preparing the route to be started, by warming up the route.\n   249\u2192     */\n   250\u2192    void warmUp();\n   251\u2192\n   252\u2192    /**\n   253\u2192     * Gets the last error that happened during changing the route lifecycle, i.e. such as when an exception was thrown\n   254\u2192     * during starting the route.\n   255\u2192     * <p/>\n   256\u2192     * This is only errors for route lifecycle changes, it is not exceptions thrown during routing exchanges by the\n   257\u2192     * Camel routing engine.\n   258\u2192     *\n   259\u2192     * @return the error or <tt>null</tt> if no error\n   260\u2192     */\n   261\u2192    RouteError getLastError();\n   262\u2192\n   263\u2192    /**\n   264\u2192     * Sets the last error that happened during changing the route lifecycle, i.e. such as when an exception was thrown\n   265\u2192     * during starting the route.\n   266\u2192     * <p/>\n   267\u2192     * This is only errors for route lifecycle changes, it is not exceptions thrown during routing exchanges by the\n   268\u2192     * Camel routing engine.\n   269\u2192     *\n   270\u2192     * @param error the error\n   271\u2192     */\n   272\u2192    void setLastError(RouteError error);\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192    /**\n   275\u2192     * Gets the route startup order\n   276\u2192     */\n   277\u2192    Integer getStartupOrder();\n   278\u2192\n   279\u2192    /**\n   280\u2192     * Sets the route startup order\n   281\u2192     */\n   282\u2192    void setStartupOrder(Integer startupOrder);\n   283\u2192\n   284\u2192    /**\n   285\u2192     * Gets the {@link RouteController} for this route.\n   286\u2192     *\n   287\u2192     * @return the route controller,\n   288\u2192     */\n   289\u2192    RouteController getRouteController();\n   290\u2192\n   291\u2192    /**\n   292\u2192     * Sets the {@link RouteController} for this route.\n   293\u2192     *\n   294\u2192     * @param controller the RouteController\n   295\u2192     */\n   296\u2192    void setRouteController(RouteController controller);\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192    /**\n   299\u2192     * Sets whether the route should automatically start when Camel starts.\n   300\u2192     * <p/>\n   301\u2192     * Default is <tt>true</tt> to always start up.\n   302\u2192     *\n   303\u2192     * @param autoStartup whether to start up automatically.\n   304\u2192     */\n   305\u2192    void setAutoStartup(Boolean autoStartup);\n   306\u2192\n   307\u2192    /**\n   308\u2192     * Gets whether the route should automatically start when Camel starts.\n   309\u2192     * <p/>\n   310\u2192     * Default is <tt>true</tt> to always start up.\n   311\u2192     *\n   312\u2192     * @return <tt>true</tt> if route should automatically start\n   313\u2192     */\n   314\u2192    Boolean isAutoStartup();\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192    /**\n   317\u2192     * Gets the route id\n   318\u2192     */\n   319\u2192    String getRouteId();\n   320\u2192\n   321\u2192    /**\n   322\u2192     * Gets the route description\n   323\u2192     */\n   324\u2192    String getRouteDescription();\n   325\u2192\n   326\u2192    /**\n   327\u2192     * Gets the route note\n   328\u2192     */\n   329\u2192    String getRouteNote();\n   330\u2192\n   331\u2192    /**\n   332\u2192     * Get the route type.\n   333\u2192     *\n   334\u2192     * Important: is null after the route has been created.\n   335\u2192     *\n   336\u2192     * @return the route type during creation of the route, is null after the route has been created.\n   337\u2192     */\n   338\u2192    NamedNode getRoute();\n   339\u2192\n   340\u2192    /**\n   341\u2192     * Clears the route model when its no longer needed.\n   342\u2192     */\n   343\u2192    void clearRouteModel();\n   344\u2192\n   345\u2192    //\n   346\u2192    // CREATION TIME\n   347\u2192    //\n   348\u2192\n   349\u2192    /**\n   350\u2192     * This method retrieves the event driven Processors on this route context.\n   351\u2192     */\n   352\u2192    List<Processor> getEventDrivenProcessors();\n   353\u2192\n   354\u2192    /**\n   355\u2192     * This method retrieves the InterceptStrategy instances this route context.\n   356\u2192     *\n   357\u2192     * @return the strategy\n   358\u2192     */\n   359\u2192    List<InterceptStrategy> getInterceptStrategies();\n   360\u2192\n   361\u2192    /**\n   362\u2192     * Sets a special intercept strategy for management.\n   363\u2192     * <p/>\n   364\u2192     * Is by default used to correlate managed performance counters with processors when the runtime route is being\n   365\u2192     * constructed\n   366\u2192     *\n   367\u2192     * @param interceptStrategy the managed intercept strategy\n   368\u2192     */\n   369\u2192    void setManagementInterceptStrategy(ManagementInterceptStrategy interceptStrategy);\n   370\u2192\n   371\u2192    /**\n   372\u2192     * Gets the special managed intercept strategy if any\n   373\u2192     *\n   374\u2192     * @return the managed intercept strategy, or <tt>null</tt> if not managed\n   375\u2192     */\n   376\u2192    ManagementInterceptStrategy getManagementInterceptStrategy();\n   377\u2192\n   378\u2192    /**\n   379\u2192     * Gets the route policy List\n   380\u2192     *\n   381\u2192     * @return the route policy list if any\n   382\u2192     */\n   383\u2192    List<RoutePolicy> getRoutePolicyList();\n   384\u2192\n   385\u2192    // called at completion time\n   386\u2192    void setErrorHandlerFactory(ErrorHandlerFactory errorHandlerFactory);\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192    // called at runtime\n   389\u2192    ErrorHandlerFactory getErrorHandlerFactory();\n   390\u2192\n   391\u2192    // called at runtime\n   392\u2192    Collection<Processor> getOnCompletions();\n   393\u2192\n   394\u2192    // called at completion time\n   395\u2192    void setOnCompletion(String onCompletionId, Processor processor);\n   396\u2192\n   397\u2192    // called at runtime\n   398\u2192    Collection<Processor> getOnExceptions();\n   399\u2192\n   400\u2192    // called at runtime\n   401\u2192    Processor getOnException(String onExceptionId);\n   402\u2192\n   403\u2192    // called at completion time\n   404\u2192    void setOnException(String onExceptionId, Processor processor);\n   405\u2192\n   406\u2192    /**\n   407\u2192     * Adds error handler for the given exception type\n   408\u2192     *\n   409\u2192     * @param factory   the error handler factory\n   410\u2192     * @param exception the exception to handle\n   411\u2192     */\n   412\u2192    void addErrorHandler(ErrorHandlerFactory factory, NamedNode exception);\n   413\u2192\n   414\u2192    /**\n   415\u2192     * Gets the error handlers\n   416\u2192     *\n   417\u2192     * @param factory the error handler factory\n   418\u2192     */\n   419\u2192    Set<NamedNode> getErrorHandlers(ErrorHandlerFactory factory);\n   420\u2192\n   421\u2192    /**\n   422\u2192     * Link the error handlers from a factory to another\n   423\u2192     *\n   424\u2192     * @param source the source factory\n   425\u2192     * @param target the target factory\n   426\u2192     */\n   427\u2192    void addErrorHandlerFactoryReference(ErrorHandlerFactory source, ErrorHandlerFactory target);\n   428\u2192\n   429\u2192    /**\n   430\u2192     * Sets the resume strategy for the route\n   431\u2192     */\n   432\u2192    void setResumeStrategy(ResumeStrategy resumeStrategy);\n   433\u2192\n   434\u2192    /**\n   435\u2192     * Sets the consumer listener for the route\n   436\u2192     */\n   437\u2192    void setConsumerListener(ConsumerListener<?, ?> consumerListener);\n   438\u2192\n   439\u2192}\n   440\u2192"
