org.apache.camel.spi.ValidatorRegistry;\n    57\u2192import org.apache.camel.support.jsse.SSLContextParameters;\n    58\u2192import org.apache.camel.vault.VaultConfiguration;\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192/**\n    61\u2192 * Interface used to represent the CamelContext used to configure routes and the policies to use during message\n    62\u2192 * exchanges between endpoints.\n    63\u2192 * <p/>\n    64\u2192 * The CamelContext offers the following methods {@link CamelContextLifecycle} to control the lifecycle:\n    65\u2192 * <ul>\n    66\u2192 * <li>{@link #start()} - to start</li>\n    67\u2192 * <li>{@link #stop()} - to shutdown (will stop all routes/components/endpoints etc and clear internal state/cache)</li>\n    68\u2192 * <li>{@link #suspend()} - to pause routing messages</li>\n    69\u2192 * <li>{@link #resume()} - to resume after a suspend</li>\n    70\u2192 * </ul>\n    71\u2192 * <p/>\n    72\u2192 * <b>Notice:</b> {@link #stop()} and {@link #suspend()} will gracefully stop/suspend routes ensuring any messages in\n    73\u2192 * progress will be given time to complete. See more details at {@link org.apache.camel.spi.ShutdownStrategy}.\n    74\u2192 * <p/>\n    75\u2192 * If you are doing a hot restart then it's advised to use the suspend/resume methods which ensure a faster restart but\n    76\u2192 * also allows any internal state to be kept as is. The stop/start approach will do a <i>cold</i> restart of Camel,\n    77\u2192 * where all internal state is reset.\n    78\u2192 * <p/>\n    79\u2192 * End users are advised to use suspend/resume. Using stop is for shutting down Camel and it's not guaranteed that when\n    80\u2192 * it's being started again using the start method that Camel will operate consistently.\n    81\u2192 * <p/>\n    82\u2192 * You can use the {@link CamelContext#getCamelContextExtension()} to obtain the extension point for the\n    83\u2192 * {@link CamelContext}. This extension point exposes internal APIs via {@link ExtendedCamelContext}.\n    84\u2192 */\n    85\u2192public interface CamelContext extends CamelContextLifecycle, RuntimeConfiguration {\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192    /**\n    88\u2192     * Gets the {@link ExtendedCamelContext} that contains the extension points for internal context APIs. These APIs\n    89\u2192     * are intended for internal usage within Camel and end-users should avoid using them.\n    90\u2192     *\n    91\u2192     * @return this {@link ExtendedCamelContext} extension point for this context.\n    92\u2192     */\n    93\u2192    ExtendedCamelContext getCamelContextExtension();\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192    /**\n    96\u2192     * If CamelContext during the start procedure was vetoed, and therefore causing Camel to not start.\n    97\u2192     */\n    98\u2192    boolean isVetoStarted();\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192    /**\n   101\u2192     * Gets the name (id) of this CamelContext.\n   102\u2192     *\n   103\u2192     * @return the name\n   104\u2192     */\n   105\u2192    String getName();\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192    /**\n   108\u2192     * Gets the description of this CamelContext.\n   109\u2192     *\n   110\u2192     * @return the description, or null if no description has been set.\n   111\u2192     */\n   112\u2192    String getDescription();\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192    /**\n   115\u2192     * Gets the current name strategy\n   116\u2192     *\n   117\u2192     * @return name strategy\n   118\u2192     */\n   119\u2192    CamelContextNameStrategy getNameStrategy();\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192    /**\n   122\u2192     * Sets a custom name strategy\n   123\u2192     *\n   124\u2192     * @param nameStrategy name strategy\n   125\u2192     */\n   126\u2192    void setNameStrategy(CamelContextNameStrategy nameStrategy);\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192    /**\n   129\u2192     * Gets the current management name strategy\n   130\u2192     *\n   131\u2192     * @return management name strategy\n   132\u2192     */\n   133\u2192    ManagementNameStrategy getManagementNameStrategy();\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192    /**\n   136\u2192     * Sets a custom management name strategy\n   137\u2192     *\n   138\u2192     * @param nameStrategy name strategy\n   139\u2192     */\n   140\u2192    void setManagementNameStrategy(ManagementNameStrategy nameStrategy);\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192    /**\n   143\u2192     * Gets the name this {@link CamelContext} was registered in JMX.\n   144\u2192     * <p/>\n   145\u2192     * The reason that a {@link CamelContext} can have a different name in JMX is the fact to remedy for name clash in\n   146\u2192     * JMX when having multiple {@link CamelContext}s in the same JVM. Camel will automatic reassign and use a free name\n   147\u2192     * to avoid failing to start.\n   148\u2192     *\n   149\u2192     * @return the management name\n   150\u2192     */\n   151\u2192    String getManagementName();\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192    /**\n   154\u2192     * Sets the name this {@link CamelContext} will be registered in JMX.\n   155\u2192     */\n   156\u2192    void setManagementName(String name);\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192    /**\n   159\u2192     * Gets the version of this CamelContext.\n   160\u2192     *\n   161\u2192     * @return the version\n   162\u2192     */\n   163\u2192    String getVersion();\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192    /**\n   166\u2192     * Gets the uptime for this context.\n   167\u2192     *\n   168\u2192     * @return the uptime\n   169\u2192     */\n   170\u2192    Duration getUptime();\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192    /**\n   173\u2192     * Gets a clock instance that keeps track of time for relevant CamelContext events\n   174\u2192     *\n   175\u2192     * @return A clock instance\n   176\u2192     */\n   177\u2192    EventClock<ContextEvents> getClock();\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192    // Service Methods\n   180\u2192    //-----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   181\u2192\n   182\u2192    /**\n   183\u2192     * Adds a service to this CamelContext, which allows this CamelContext to control the lifecycle, ensuring the\n   184\u2192     * service is stopped when the CamelContext stops.\n   185\u2192     * <p/>\n   186\u2192     * The service will also have {@link CamelContext} injected if its {@link CamelContextAware}. The service will also\n   187\u2192     * be enlisted in JMX for management (if JMX is enabled). The service will be started, if its not already started.\n   188\u2192     *\n   189\u2192     * @param  object    the service\n   190\u2192     * @throws Exception can be thrown when starting the service\n   191\u2192     */\n   192\u2192    void addService(Object object) throws Exception;\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192    /**\n   195\u2192     * Adds a service to this CamelContext.\n   196\u2192     * <p/>\n   197\u2192     * The service will also have {@link CamelContext} injected if its {@link CamelContextAware}. The service will also\n   198\u2192     * be enlisted in JMX for management (if JMX is enabled). The service will be started, if its not already started.\n   199\u2192     * <p/>\n   200\u2192     * If the option <tt>closeOnShutdown</tt> is <tt>true</tt> then this CamelContext will control the lifecycle,\n   201\u2192     * ensuring the service is stopped when the CamelContext stops. If the option <tt>closeOnShutdown</tt> is\n   202\u2192     * <tt>false</tt> then this CamelContext will not stop the service when the CamelContext stops.\n   203\u2192     *\n   204\u2192     * @param  object         the service\n   205\u2192     * @param  stopOnShutdown whether to stop the service when this CamelContext shutdown.\n   206\u2192     * @throws Exception      can be thrown when starting the service\n   207\u2192     */\n   208\u2192    void addService(Object object, boolean stopOnShutdown) throws Exception;\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192    /**\n   211\u2192     * Adds a service to this CamelContext.\n   212\u2192     * <p/>\n   213\u2192     * The service will also have {@link CamelContext} injected if its {@link CamelContextAware}. The service will also\n   214\u2192     * be enlisted in JMX for management (if JMX is enabled). The service will be started, if its not already started.\n   215\u2192     * <p/>\n   216\u2192     * If the option <tt>closeOnShutdown</tt> is <tt>true</tt> then this CamelContext will control the lifecycle,\n   217\u2192     * ensuring the service is stopped when the CamelContext stops. If the option <tt>closeOnShutdown</tt> is\n   218\u2192     * <tt>false</tt> then this CamelContext will not stop the service when the CamelContext stops.\n   219\u2192     *\n   220\u2192     * @param  object         the service\n   221\u2192     * @param  stopOnShutdown whether to stop the service when this CamelContext shutdown.\n   222\u2192     * @param  forceStart     whether to force starting the service right now, as otherwise the service may be deferred\n   223\u2192     *                        being started to later using {@link #deferStartService(Object, boolean)}\n   224\u2192     * @throws Exception      can be thrown when starting the service\n   225\u2192     */\n   226\u2192    void addService(Object object, boolean stopOnShutdown, boolean forceStart) throws Exception;\n   227\u2192\n   228\u2192    /**\n   229\u2192     * Adds a service to this CamelContext (prototype scope).\n   230\u2192     * <p/>\n   231\u2192     * The service will also have {@link CamelContext} injected if its {@link CamelContextAware}. The service will be\n   232\u2192     * started, if its not already started.\n   233\u2192     *\n   234\u2192     * @param  object    the service\n   235\u2192     * @throws Exception can be thrown when starting the service\n   236\u2192     */\n   237\u2192    void addPrototypeService(Object object) throws Exception;\n   238\u2192\n   239\u2192    /**\n   240\u2192     * Removes a service from this CamelContext.\n   241\u2192     * <p/>\n   242\u2192     * The service is assumed to have been previously added using {@link #addService(Object)} method. This method will\n   243\u2192     * <b>not</b> change the service lifecycle.\n   244\u2192     *\n   245\u2192     * @param  object    the service\n   246\u2192     * @throws Exception can be thrown if error removing the service\n   247\u2192     * @return           <tt>true</tt> if the service was removed, <tt>false</tt> if no service existed\n   248\u2192     */\n   249\u2192    boolean removeService(Object object) throws Exception;\n   250\u2192\n   251\u2192    /**\n   252\u2192     * Has the given service already been added to this CamelContext?\n   253\u2192     *\n   254\u2192     * @param  object the service\n   255\u2192     * @return        <tt>true</tt> if already added, <tt>false</tt> if not.\n   256\u2192     */\n   257\u2192    boolean hasService(Object object);\n   258\u2192\n   259\u2192    /**\n   260\u2192     * Finds the first service matching the filter\n   261\u2192     *\n   262\u2192     * @param  filter the filter\n   263\u2192     * @return        the service if found or null if none found\n   264\u2192     */\n   265\u2192    Service hasService(java.util.function.Predicate<Service> filter);\n   266\u2192\n   267\u2192    /**\n   268\u2192     * Has the given service type already been added to this CamelContext?\n   269\u2192     *\n   270\u2192     * @param  type the class type\n   271\u2192     * @return      the service instance or <tt>null</tt> if not already added.\n   272\u2192     */\n   273\u2192    <T> T hasService(Class<T> type);\n   274\u2192\n   275\u2192    /**\n   276\u2192     * Has the given service type already been added to this CamelContext?\n   277\u2192     *\n   278\u2192     * @param  type the class type\n   279\u2192     * @return      the services instance or empty set.\n   280\u2192     */\n   281\u2192    <T> Set<T> hasServices(Class<T> type);\n   282\u2192\n   283\u2192    /**\n   284\u2192     * Defers starting the service until {@link CamelContext} is (almost started) or started and has initialized all its\n   285\u2192     * prior services and routes.\n   286\u2192     * <p/>\n   287\u2192     * If {@link CamelContext} is already started then the service is started immediately.\n   288\u2192     *\n   289\u2192     * @param  object         the service\n   290\u2192     * @param  stopOnShutdown whether to stop the service when this CamelContext shutdown. Setting this to <tt>true</tt>\n   291\u2192     *                        will keep a reference to the service in this {@link CamelContext} until the CamelContext\n   292\u2192     *                        is stopped. So do not use it for short lived services.\n   293\u2192     * @throws Exception      can be thrown when starting the service, which is only attempted if {@link CamelContext}\n   294\u2192     *                        has already been started when calling this method.\n   295\u2192     */\n   296\u2192    void deferStartService(Object object, boolean stopOnShutdown) throws Exception;\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192    /**\n   299\u2192     * Adds the given listener to be invoked when {@link CamelContext} have just been started.\n   300\u2192     * <p/>\n   301\u2192     * This allows listeners to do any custom work after the routes and other services have been started and are\n   302\u2192     * running.\n   303\u2192     * <p/>\n   304\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> The listener will always be invoked, also if the {@link CamelContext} has already been started,\n   305\u2192     * see the {@link org.apache.camel.StartupListener#onCamelContextStarted(CamelContext, boolean)} method.\n   306\u2192     *\n   307\u2192     * @param  listener  the listener\n   308\u2192     * @throws Exception can be thrown if {@link CamelContext} is already started and the listener is invoked and cause\n   309\u2192     *                   an exception to be thrown\n   310\u2192     */\n   311\u2192    void addStartupListener(StartupListener listener) throws Exception;\n   312\u2192\n   313\u2192    // Component Management Methods\n   314\u2192    //-----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192    /**\n   317\u2192     * Adds a component to the context.\n   318\u2192     *\n   319\u2192     * Notice the component will be auto-started if Camel is already started.\n   320\u2192     *\n   321\u2192     * @param componentName the name the component is registered as\n   322\u2192     * @param component     the component\n   323\u2192     */\n   324\u2192    void addComponent(String componentName, Component component);\n   325\u2192\n   326\u2192    /**\n   327\u2192     * Is the given component already registered?\n   328\u2192     *\n   329\u2192     * @param  componentName the name of the component\n   330\u2192     * @return               the registered Component or <tt>null</tt> if not registered\n   331\u2192     */\n   332\u2192    Component hasComponent(String componentName);\n   333\u2192\n   334\u2192    /**\n   335\u2192     * Gets a component from the CamelContext by name.\n   336\u2192     * <p/>\n   337\u2192     * Notice the returned component will be auto-started. If you do not intend to do that then use\n   338\u2192     * {@link #getComponent(String, boolean, boolean)}.\n   339\u2192     *\n   340\u2192     * @param  componentName the name of the component\n   341\u2192     * @return               the component\n   342\u2192     */\n   343\u2192    Component getComponent(String componentName);\n   344\u2192\n   345\u2192    /**\n   346\u2192     * Gets a component from the CamelContext by name.\n   347\u2192     * <p/>\n   348\u2192     * Notice the returned component will be auto-started. If you do not intend to do that then use\n   349\u2192     * {@link #getComponent(String, boolean, boolean)}.\n   350\u2192     *\n   351\u2192     * @param  name                 the name of the component\n   352\u2192     * @param  autoCreateComponents whether or not the component should be lazily created if it does not already exist\n   353\u2192     * @return                      the component\n   354\u2192     */\n   355\u2192    Component getComponent(String name, boolean autoCreateComponents);\n   356\u2192\n   357\u2192    /**\n   358\u2192     * Gets a component from the CamelContext by name.\n   359\u2192     *\n   360\u2192     * @param  name                 the name of the component\n   361\u2192     * @param  autoCreateComponents whether or not the component should be lazily created if it does not already exist\n   362\u2192     * @param  autoStart            whether to auto start the component if {@link CamelContext} is already started.\n   363\u2192     * @return                      the component\n   364\u2192     */\n   365\u2192    Component getComponent(String name, boolean autoCreateComponents, boolean autoStart);\n   366\u2192\n   367\u2192    /**\n   368\u2192     * Gets a component from the CamelContext by name and specifying the expected type of component.\n   369\u2192     *\n   370\u2192     * @param  name          the name to lookup\n   371\u2192     * @param  componentType the expected type\n   372\u2192     * @return               the component\n   373\u2192     */\n   374\u2192    <T extends Component> T getComponent(String name, Class<T> componentType);\n   375\u2192\n   376\u2192    /**\n   377\u2192     * Gets a readonly list of names of the components currently registered\n   378\u2192     *\n   379\u2192     * @return a readonly list with the names of the components\n   380\u2192     */\n   381\u2192    Set<String> getComponentNames();\n   382\u2192\n   383\u2192    /**\n   384\u2192     * Removes a previously added component.\n   385\u2192     * <p/>\n   386\u2192     * The component being removed will be stopped first.\n   387\u2192     *\n   388\u2192     * @param  componentName the component name to remove\n   389\u2192     * @return               the previously added component or null if it had not been previously added.\n   390\u2192     */\n   391\u2192    Component removeComponent(String componentName);\n   392\u2192\n   393\u2192    // Endpoint Management Methods\n   394\u2192    //-----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   395\u2192\n   396\u2192    /**\n   397\u2192     * Gets the {@link org.apache.camel.spi.EndpointRegistry}\n   398\u2192     */\n   399\u2192    EndpointRegistry getEndpointRegistry();\n   400\u2192\n   401\u2192    /**\n   402\u2192     * Resolves the given name to an {@link Endpoint} of the specified type. If the name has a singleton endpoint\n   403\u2192     * registered, then the singleton is returned. Otherwise, a new {@link Endpoint} is created and registered in the\n   404\u2192     * {@link org.apache.camel.spi.EndpointRegistry}.\n   405\u2192     *\n   406\u2192     * @param  uri the URI of the endpoint\n   407\u2192     * @return     the endpoint\n   408\u2192     */\n   409\u2192    Endpoint getEndpoint(String uri);\n   410\u2192\n   411\u2192    /**\n   412\u2192     * Resolves the given name to an {@link Endpoint} of the specified type. If the name has a singleton endpoint\n   413\u2192     * registered, then the singleton is returned. Otherwise, a new {@link Endpoint} is created and registered in the\n   414\u2192     * {@link org.apache.camel.spi.EndpointRegistry}.\n   415\u2192     *\n   416\u2192     * @param  uri        the URI of the endpoint\n   417\u2192     * @param  parameters the parameters to customize the endpoint\n   418\u2192     * @return            the endpoint\n   419\u2192     */\n   420\u2192    Endpoint getEndpoint(String uri, Map<String, Object> parameters);\n   421\u2192\n   422\u2192    /**\n   423\u2192     * Resolves the given name to an {@link Endpoint} of the specified type. If the name has a singleton endpoint\n   424\u2192     * registered, then the singleton is returned. Otherwise, a new {@link Endpoint} is created and registered in the\n   425\u2192     * {@link org.apache.camel.spi.EndpointRegistry}.\n   426\u2192     *\n   427\u2192     * @param  name         the name of the endpoint\n   428\u2192     * @param  endpointType the expected type\n   429\u2192     * @return              the endpoint\n   430\u2192     */\n   431\u2192    <T extends Endpoint> T getEndpoint(String name, Class<T> endpointType);\n   432\u2192\n   433\u2192    /**\n   434\u2192     * Returns a read-only {@link Collection} of all of the endpoints from the\n   435\u2192     * {@link org.apache.camel.spi.EndpointRegistry}\n   436\u2192     *\n   437\u2192     * @return all endpoints\n   438\u2192     */\n   439\u2192    Collection<Endpoint> getEndpoints();\n   440\u2192\n   441\u2192    /**\n   442\u2192     * Is the given endpoint already registered in the {@link org.apache.camel.spi.EndpointRegistry}\n   443\u2192     *\n   444\u2192     * @param  uri the URI of the endpoint\n   445\u2192     * @return     the registered endpoint or <tt>null</tt> if not registered\n   446\u2192     */\n   447\u2192    Endpoint hasEndpoint(String uri);\n   448\u2192\n   449\u2192    /**\n   450\u2192     * Adds and starts the endpoint to the {@link org.apache.camel.spi.EndpointRegistry} using the given URI.\n   451\u2192     *\n   452\u2192     * @param  uri       the URI to be used to resolve this endpoint\n   453\u2192     * @param  endpoint  the endpoint to be started and added to the registry\n   454\u2192     * @return           the old endpoint that was previously registered or <tt>null</tt> if none was registered\n   455\u2192     * @throws Exception if the new endpoint could not be started or the old endpoint could not be stopped\n   456\u2192     */\n   457\u2192    Endpoint addEndpoint(String uri, Endpoint endpoint) throws Exception;\n   458\u2192\n   459\u2192    /**\n   460\u2192     * Removes the endpoint from the {@link org.apache.camel.spi.EndpointRegistry}.\n   461\u2192     * <p/>\n   462\u2192     * The endpoint being removed will be stopped first.\n   463\u2192     *\n   464\u2192     * @param  endpoint  the endpoint\n   465\u2192     * @throws Exception if the endpoint could not be stopped\n   466\u2192     */\n   467\u2192    void removeEndpoint(Endpoint endpoint) throws Exception;\n   468\u2192\n   469\u2192    /**\n   470\u2192     * Removes all endpoints with the given URI from the {@link org.apache.camel.spi.EndpointRegistry}.\n   471\u2192     * <p/>\n   472\u2192     * The endpoints being removed will be stopped first.\n   473\u2192     *\n   474\u2192     * @param  pattern   an uri or pattern to match\n   475\u2192     * @return           a collection of endpoints removed which could be empty if there are no endpoints found for the\n   476\u2192     *                   given <tt>pattern</tt>\n   477\u2192     * @throws Exception if at least one endpoint could not be stopped\n   478\u2192     * @see              org.apache.camel.support.EndpointHelper#matchEndpoint(CamelContext, String, String) for pattern\n   479\u2192     */\n   480\u2192    Collection<Endpoint> removeEndpoints(String pattern) throws Exception;\n   481\u2192\n   482\u2192    /**\n   483\u2192     * Gets the global endpoint configuration, where you can configure common endpoint options.\n   484\u2192     */\n   485\u2192    GlobalEndpointConfiguration getGlobalEndpointConfiguration();\n   486\u2192\n   487\u2192    // Route Management Methods\n   488\u2192    //-----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   489\u2192\n   490\u2192    /**\n   491\u2192     * Sets a custom {@link RouteController} to use\n   492\u2192     *\n   493\u2192     * @param routeController the route controller\n   494\u2192     */\n   495\u2192    void setRouteController(RouteController routeController);\n   496\u2192\n   497\u2192    /**\n   498\u2192     * Gets the {@link RouteController}\n   499\u2192     *\n   500\u2192     * @return the route controller.\n   501\u2192     */\n   502\u2192    RouteController getRouteController();\n   503\u2192\n   504\u2192    /**\n   505\u2192     * Returns the current routes in this CamelContext\n   506\u2192     *\n   507\u2192     * @return the current routes\n   508\u2192     */\n   509\u2192    List<Route> getRoutes();\n   510\u2192\n   511\u2192    /**\n   512\u2192     * Returns all the ids of all the routes (sorted)\n   513\u2192     *\n   514\u2192     * @return ids of all the current routes\n   515\u2192     */\n   516\u2192    Set<String> getRouteIds();\n   517\u2192\n   518\u2192    /**\n   519\u2192     * Returns all the names of the route groups (sorted). Is empty if groups are not assigned to any routes\n   520\u2192     *\n   521\u2192     * @return names of all the current route groups (is empty if groups are not assigned to any routes)\n   522\u2192     */\n   523\u2192    Set<String> getRouteGroupIds();\n   524\u2192\n   525\u2192    /**\n   526\u2192     * To get all the routes that matches the filter.\n   527\u2192     *\n   528\u2192     * @param  filter to filter to include only accepted routes\n   529\u2192     * @return        the routes that matched the filter\n   530\u2192     */\n   531\u2192    List<Route> getRoutes(Predicate<Route> filter);\n   532\u2192\n   533\u2192    /**\n   534\u2192     * Gets the routes for the given group\n   535\u2192     *\n   536\u2192     * @param  groupId the id of the group\n   537\u2192     * @return         the routes or an empty list if no routes exists for the given group id\n   538\u2192     */\n   539\u2192    List<Route> getRoutesByGroup(String groupId);\n   540\u2192\n   541\u2192    /**\n   542\u2192     * Returns the total number of routes in this CamelContext\n   543\u2192     */\n   544\u2192    int getRoutesSize();\n   545\u2192\n   546\u2192    /**\n   547\u2192     * Gets the route with the given id\n   548\u2192     *\n   549\u2192     * @param  id id of the route\n   550\u2192     * @return    the route or <tt>null</tt> if not found\n   551\u2192     */\n   552\u2192    Route getRoute(String id);\n   553\u2192\n   554\u2192    /**\n   555\u2192     * Gets the processor from any of the routes which with the given id\n   556\u2192     *\n   557\u2192     * @param  id id of the processor\n   558\u2192     * @return    the processor or <tt>null</tt> if not found\n   559\u2192     */\n   560\u2192    Processor getProcessor(String id);\n   561\u2192\n   562\u2192    /**\n   563\u2192     * Gets the processor from any of the routes which with the given id\n   564\u2192     *\n   565\u2192     * @param  id                           id of the processor\n   566\u2192     * @param  type                         the processor type\n   567\u2192     * @return                              the processor or <tt>null</tt> if not found\n   568\u2192     * @throws java.lang.ClassCastException is thrown if the type is not correct type\n   569\u2192     */\n   570\u2192    <T extends Processor> T getProcessor(String id, Class<T> type);\n   571\u2192\n   572\u2192    /**\n   573\u2192     * Adds a collection of routes to this CamelContext using the given builder to build them.\n   574\u2192     * <p/>\n   575\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> The added routes will <b>only</b> be started, if {@link CamelContext} is already started. You\n   576\u2192     * may want to check the state of {@link CamelContext} before adding the routes, using the\n   577\u2192     * {@link org.apache.camel.CamelContext#getStatus()} method.\n   578\u2192     * <p/>\n   579\u2192     * <b>Important: </b> Each route in the same {@link org.apache.camel.CamelContext} must have an <b>unique</b> route\n   580\u2192     * id. If you use the API from {@link org.apache.camel.CamelContext} or\n   581\u2192     * {@link org.apache.camel.model.ModelCamelContext} to add routes, then any new routes which has a route id that\n   582\u2192     * matches an old route, then the old route is replaced by the new route.\n   583\u2192     *\n   584\u2192     * @param  builder   the builder which will create the routes and add them to this CamelContext\n   585\u2192     * @throws Exception if the routes could not be created for whatever reason\n   586\u2192     */\n   587\u2192    void addRoutes(RoutesBuilder builder) throws Exception;\n   588\u2192\n   589\u2192    /**\n   590\u2192     * Adds the templated routes from the routes builder. For example in Java DSL you can use\n   591\u2192     * {@link org.apache.camel.builder.TemplatedRouteBuilder}.\n   592\u2192     *\n   593\u2192     * @param  builder   the builder which has templated routes\n   594\u2192     * @throws Exception if the routes could not be created for whatever reason\n   595\u2192     */\n   596\u2192    void addTemplatedRoutes(RoutesBuilder builder) throws Exception;\n   597\u2192\n   598\u2192    /**\n   599\u2192     * Adds the routes configurations (global configuration for all routes) from the routes builder.\n   600\u2192     *\n   601\u2192     * @param  builder   the builder which has routes configurations\n   602\u2192     * @throws Exception if the routes configurations could not be created for whatever reason\n   603\u2192     */\n   604\u2192    void addRoutesConfigurations(RouteConfigurationsBuilder builder) throws Exception;\n   605\u2192\n   606\u2192    /**\n   607\u2192     * Removes the given route (the route <b>must</b> be stopped before it can be removed).\n   608\u2192     * <p/>\n   609\u2192     * A route which is removed will be unregistered from JMX, have its services stopped/shutdown and the route\n   610\u2192     * definition etc. will also be removed. All the resources related to the route will be stopped and cleared.\n   611\u2192     * <p/>\n   612\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> When removing a route, the {@link Endpoint}s which are in the static cache of\n   613\u2192     * {@link org.apache.camel.spi.EndpointRegistry} and are <b>only</b> used by the route (not used by other routes)\n   614\u2192     * will also be removed. But {@link Endpoint}s which may have been created as part of routing messages by the route,\n   615\u2192     * and those endpoints are enlisted in the dynamic cache of {@link org.apache.camel.spi.EndpointRegistry} are\n   616\u2192     * <b>not</b> removed. To remove those dynamic kind of endpoints, use the {@link #removeEndpoints(String)} method.\n   617\u2192     * If not removing those endpoints, they will be kept in the dynamic cache of\n   618\u2192     * {@link org.apache.camel.spi.EndpointRegistry}, but my eventually be removed (evicted) when they have not been in\n   619\u2192     * use for a longer period of time; and the dynamic cache upper limit is hit, and it evicts the least used\n   620\u2192     * endpoints.\n   621\u2192     * <p/>\n   622\u2192     * End users can use this method to remove unwanted routes or temporary routes which no longer is in demand.\n   623\u2192     *\n   624\u2192     * @param  routeId   the route id\n   625\u2192     * @return           <tt>true</tt> if the route was removed, <tt>false</tt> if the route could not be removed\n   626\u2192     *                   because it's not stopped\n   627\u2192     * @throws Exception is thrown if the route could not be shutdown for whatever reason\n   628\u2192     */\n   629\u2192    boolean removeRoute(String routeId) throws Exception;\n   630\u2192\n   631\u2192    /**\n   632\u2192     * Adds a new route from a given route template.\n   633\u2192     *\n   634\u2192     * Camel end users should favour using {@link org.apache.camel.builder.TemplatedRouteBuilder} which is a fluent\n   635\u2192     * builder with more functionality than this API.\n   636\u2192     *\n   637\u2192     * @param  routeId         the id of the new route to add (optional)\n   638\u2192     * @param  routeTemplateId the id of the route template (mandatory)\n   639\u2192     * @param  parameters      parameters to use for the route template when creating the new route\n   640\u2192     * @return                 the id of the route added (for example when an id was auto assigned)\n   641\u2192     * @throws Exception       is thrown if error creating and adding the new route\n   642\u2192     */\n   643\u2192    String addRouteFromTemplate(String routeId, String routeTemplateId, Map<String, Object> parameters) throws Exception;\n   644\u2192\n   645\u2192    /**\n   646\u2192     * Adds a new route from a given route template.\n   647\u2192     *\n   648\u2192     * Camel end users should favour using {@link org.apache.camel.builder.TemplatedRouteBuilder} which is a fluent\n   649\u2192     * builder with more functionality than this API.\n   650\u2192     *\n   651\u2192     * @param  routeId         the id of the new route to add (optional)\n   652\u2192     * @param  routeTemplateId the id of the route template (mandatory)\n   653\u2192     * @param  prefixId        prefix to use for all node ids (not route id). Use null for no prefix. (optional)\n   654\u2192     * @param  parameters      parameters to use for the route template when creating the new route\n   655\u2192     * @return                 the id of the route added (for example when an id was auto assigned)\n   656\u2192     * @throws Exception       is thrown if error creating and adding the new route\n   657\u2192     */\n   658\u2192    @Deprecated(since = \"4.14.0\")\n   659\u2192    String addRouteFromTemplate(\n   660\u2192            String routeId, String routeTemplateId, String prefixId,\n   661\u2192            Map<String, Object> parameters)\n   662\u2192            throws Exception;\n   663\u2192\n   664\u2192    /**\n   665\u2192     * Adds a new route from a given route template.\n   666\u2192     *\n   667\u2192     * Camel end users should favour using {@link org.apache.camel.builder.TemplatedRouteBuilder} which is a fluent\n   668\u2192     * builder with more functionality than this API.\n   669\u2192     *\n   670\u2192     * @param  routeId         the id of the new route to add (optional)\n   671\u2192     * @param  routeTemplateId the id of the route template (mandatory)\n   672\u2192     * @param  prefixId        prefix to use for all node ids (not route id). Use null for no prefix. (optional)\n   673\u2192     * @param  group           route group name (optional)\n   674\u2192     * @param  parameters      parameters to use for the route template when creating the new route\n   675\u2192     * @return                 the id of the route added (for example when an id was auto assigned)\n   676\u2192     * @throws Exception       is thrown if error creating and adding the new route\n   677\u2192     */\n   678\u2192    String addRouteFromTemplate(\n   679\u2192            String routeId, String routeTemplateId, String prefixId, String group,\n   680\u2192            Map<String, Object> parameters)\n   681\u2192            throws Exception;\n   682\u2192\n   683\u2192    /**\n   684\u2192     * Adds a new route from a given route template.\n   685\u2192     *\n   686\u2192     * Camel end users should favour using {@link org.apache.camel.builder.TemplatedRouteBuilder} which is a fluent\n   687\u2192     * builder with more functionality than this API.\n   688\u2192     *\n   689\u2192     * @param  routeId              the id of the new route to add (optional)\n   690\u2192     * @param  routeTemplateId      the id of the route template (mandatory)\n   691\u2192     * @param  prefixId             prefix to use for all node ids (not route id). Use null for no prefix. (optional)\n   692\u2192     * @param  routeTemplateContext the route template context (mandatory)\n   693\u2192     * @return                      the id of the route added (for example when an id was auto assigned)\n   694\u2192     * @throws Exception            is thrown if error creating and adding the new route\n   695\u2192     */\n   696\u2192    @Deprecated(since = \"4.14.0\")\n   697\u2192    String addRouteFromTemplate(\n   698\u2192            String routeId, String routeTemplateId, String prefixId, RouteTemplateContext routeTemplateContext)\n   699\u2192            throws Exception;\n   700\u2192\n   701\u2192    /**\n   702\u2192     * Adds a new route from a given route template.\n   703\u2192     *\n   704\u2192     * Camel end users should favour using {@link org.apache.camel.builder.TemplatedRouteBuilder} which is a fluent\n   705\u2192     * builder with more functionality than this API.\n   706\u2192     *\n   707\u2192     * @param  routeId              the id of the new route to add (optional)\n   708\u2192     * @param  routeTemplateId      the id of the route template (mandatory)\n   709\u2192     * @param  prefixId             prefix to use for all node ids (not route id). Use null for no prefix. (optional)\n   710\u2192     * @param  group                route group name (optional)\n   711\u2192     * @param  routeTemplateContext the route template context (mandatory)\n   712\u2192     * @return                      the id of the route added (for example when an id was auto assigned)\n   713\u2192     * @throws Exception            is thrown if error creating and adding the new route\n   714\u2192     */\n   715\u2192    String addRouteFromTemplate(\n   716\u2192            String routeId, String routeTemplateId, String prefixId, String group, RouteTemplateContext routeTemplateContext)\n   717\u2192            throws Exception;\n   718\u2192\n   719\u2192    /**\n   720\u2192     * Adds a new route from a given kamelet\n   721\u2192     *\n   722\u2192     * @param  routeId           the id of the new route to add (optional)\n   723\u2192     * @param  routeTemplateId   the id of the kamelet route template (mandatory)\n   724\u2192     * @param  prefixId          prefix to use for all node ids (not route id). Use null for no prefix. (optional)\n   725\u2192     * @param  parentRouteId     the id of the route which is using the kamelet (such as from / to)\n   726\u2192     * @param  parentProcessorId the id of the processor which is using the kamelet (such as to)\n   727\u2192     * @param  parameters        parameters to use for the route template when creating the new route\n   728\u2192     * @return                   the id of the route added (for example when an id was auto assigned)\n   729\u2192     * @throws Exception         is thrown if error creating and adding the new route\n   730\u2192     */\n   731\u2192    @Deprecated(since = \"4.14.0\")\n   732\u2192    String addRouteFromKamelet(\n   733\u2192            String routeId, String routeTemplateId, String prefixId,\n   734\u2192            String parentRouteId, String parentProcessorId,\n   735\u2192            Map<String, Object> parameters)\n   736\u2192            throws Exception;\n   737\u2192\n   738\u2192    /**\n   739\u2192     * Adds a new route from a given kamelet\n   740\u2192     *\n   741\u2192     * @param  routeId           the id of the new route to add (optional)\n   742\u2192     * @param  routeTemplateId   the id of the kamelet route template (mandatory)\n   743\u2192     * @param  prefixId          prefix to use for all node ids (not route id). Use null for no prefix. (optional)\n   744\u2192     * @param  group             route group name (optional)\n   745\u2192     * @param  parentRouteId     the id of the route which is using the kamelet (such as from / to)\n   746\u2192     * @param  parentProcessorId the id of the processor which is using the kamelet (such as to)\n   747\u2192     * @param  parameters        parameters to use for the route template when creating the new route\n   748\u2192     * @return                   the id of the route added (for example when an id was auto assigned)\n   749\u2192     * @throws Exception         is thrown if error creating and adding the new route\n   750\u2192     */\n   751\u2192    String addRouteFromKamelet(\n   752\u2192            String routeId, String routeTemplateId, String prefixId, String group,\n   753\u2192            String parentRouteId, String parentProcessorId,\n   754\u2192            Map<String, Object> parameters)\n   755\u2192            throws Exception;\n   756\u2192\n   757\u2192    /**\n   758\u2192     * Removes the route templates matching the pattern\n   759\u2192     *\n   760\u2192     * @param  pattern   pattern, such as * for all, or foo* to remove all foo templates\n   761\u2192     * @throws Exception is thrown if error during removing route templates\n   762\u2192     */\n   763\u2192    void removeRouteTemplates(String pattern) throws Exception;\n   764\u2192\n   765\u2192    /**\n   766\u2192     * Adds the given route policy factory\n   767\u2192     *\n   768\u2192     * @param routePolicyFactory the factory\n   769\u2192     */\n   770\u2192    void addRoutePolicyFactory(RoutePolicyFactory routePolicyFactory);\n   771\u2192\n   772\u2192    /**\n   773\u2192     * Gets the route policy factories\n   774\u2192     *\n   775\u2192     * @return the list of current route policy factories\n   776\u2192     */\n   777\u2192    List<RoutePolicyFactory> getRoutePolicyFactories();\n   778\u2192\n   779\u2192    // Rest Methods\n   780\u2192    //-----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   781\u2192\n   782\u2192    /**\n   783\u2192     * Sets a custom {@link org.apache.camel.spi.RestConfiguration}\n   784\u2192     *\n   785\u2192     * @param restConfiguration the REST configuration\n   786\u2192     */\n   787\u2192    void setRestConfiguration(RestConfiguration restConfiguration);\n   788\u2192\n   789\u2192    /**\n   790\u2192     * Gets the default REST configuration\n   791\u2192     *\n   792\u2192     * @return the configuration, or <tt>null</tt> if none has been configured.\n   793\u2192     */\n   794\u2192    RestConfiguration getRestConfiguration();\n   795\u2192\n   796\u2192    /**\n   797\u2192     * Sets a custom {@link VaultConfiguration}\n   798\u2192     *\n   799\u2192     * @param vaultConfiguration the vault configuration\n   800\u2192     */\n   801\u2192    void setVaultConfiguration(VaultConfiguration vaultConfiguration);\n   802\u2192\n   803\u2192    /**\n   804\u2192     * Gets the vault configuration\n   805\u2192     *\n   806\u2192     * @return the configuration, or <tt>null</tt> if none has been configured.\n   807\u2192     */\n   808\u2192    VaultConfiguration getVaultConfiguration();\n   809\u2192\n   810\u2192    /**\n   811\u2192     * Gets the {@link org.apache.camel.spi.RestRegistry} to use\n   812\u2192     *\n   813\u2192     * @deprecated use PluginHelper\n   814\u2192     */\n   815\u2192    @Deprecated\n   816\u2192    RestRegistry getRestRegistry();\n   817\u2192\n   818\u2192    /**\n   819\u2192     * Sets a custom {@link org.apache.camel.spi.RestRegistry} to use.\n   820\u2192     */\n   821\u2192    @Deprecated\n   822\u2192    void setRestRegistry(RestRegistry restRegistry);\n   823\u2192\n   824\u2192    // Properties\n   825\u2192    //-----------------------------------------------------------------------\n   826\u2192\n   827\u2192    /**\n   828\u2192     * Returns the type converter used to coerce types from one type to another.\n   829\u2192     * <p/>\n   830\u2192     * Notice that this {@link CamelContext} should be at least initialized before you can get the type converter.\n   831\u2192     *\n   832\u2192     * @return the converter\n   833\u2192     */\n   834\u2192    TypeConverter getTypeConverter();\n   835\u2192\n   836\u2192    /**\n   837\u2192     * Returns the type converter registry where type converters can be added or looked up\n   838\u2192     *\n   839\u2192     * @return the type converter registry\n   840\u2192     */\n   841\u2192    TypeConverterRegistry getTypeConverterRegistry();\n   842\u2192\n   843\u2192    /**\n   844\u2192     * Configures the type converter registry to use, where type converters can be added or looked up.\n   845\u2192     *\n   846\u2192     * @param typeConverterRegistry the registry to use\n   847\u2192     */\n   848\u2192    void setTypeConverterRegistry(TypeConverterRegistry typeConverterRegistry);\n   849\u2192\n   850\u2192    /**\n   851\u2192     * Returns the registry used to lookup components by name and type such as SimpleRegistry, Spring\n   852\u2192     * ApplicationContext, JNDI, or the OSGi Service Registry.\n   853\u2192     *\n   854\u2192     * @return the registry\n   855\u2192     */\n   856\u2192    Registry getRegistry();\n   857\u2192\n   858\u2192    /**\n   859\u2192     * Returns the registry used to lookup components by name and as the given type\n   860\u2192     *\n   861\u2192     * @param  type the registry type such as org.apache.camel.impl.JndiRegistry\n   862\u2192     * @return      the registry, or <tt>null</tt> if the given type was not found as a registry implementation\n   863\u2192     */\n   864\u2192    <T> T getRegistry(Class<T> type);\n   865\u2192\n   866\u2192    /**\n   867\u2192     * Returns the injector used to instantiate objects by type\n   868\u2192     *\n   869\u2192     * @return the injector\n   870\u2192     */\n   871\u2192    Injector getInjector();\n   872\u2192\n   873\u2192    /**\n   874\u2192     * Sets the injector to use\n   875\u2192     */\n   876\u2192    void setInjector(Injector injector);\n   877\u2192\n   878\u2192    /**\n   879\u2192     * Returns the lifecycle strategies used to handle lifecycle notifications\n   880\u2192     *\n   881\u2192     * @return the lifecycle strategies\n   882\u2192     */\n   883\u2192    List<LifecycleStrategy> getLifecycleStrategies();\n   884\u2192\n   885\u2192    /**\n   886\u2192     * Adds the given lifecycle strategy to be used.\n   887\u2192     *\n   888\u2192     * @param lifecycleStrategy the strategy\n   889\u2192     */\n   890\u2192    void addLifecycleStrategy(LifecycleStrategy lifecycleStrategy);\n   891\u2192\n   892\u2192    /**\n   893\u2192     * Resolves a language for creating expressions\n   894\u2192     *\n   895\u2192     * @param  language                name of the language\n   896\u2192     * @return                         the resolved language\n   897\u2192     * @throws NoSuchLanguageException is thrown if language could not be resolved\n   898\u2192     */\n   899\u2192    Language resolveLanguage(String language) throws NoSuchLanguageException;\n   900\u2192\n   901\u2192    /**\n   902\u2192     * Parses the given text and resolve any property placeholders - using {{key}}.\n   903\u2192     * <p/>\n   904\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> If resolving placeholders on an endpoint uri, then you SHOULD use\n   905\u2192     * EndpointHelper#resolveEndpointUriPropertyPlaceholders instead.\n   906\u2192     *\n   907\u2192     * @param  text                     the text such as an endpoint uri or the likes\n   908\u2192     * @return                          the text with resolved property placeholders\n   909\u2192     * @throws IllegalArgumentException is thrown if property placeholders was used and there was an error resolving\n   910\u2192     *                                  them\n   911\u2192     */\n   912\u2192    String resolvePropertyPlaceholders(String text);\n   913\u2192\n   914\u2192    /**\n   915\u2192     * To get a variable by name.\n   916\u2192     *\n   917\u2192     * @param  name the variable name. Can be prefixed with repo-id:name to lookup the variable from a specific\n   918\u2192     *              repository. If no repo-id is provided, then global repository will be used.\n   919\u2192     * @return      the variable, or <tt>null</tt> if not found.\n   920\u2192     */\n   921\u2192    Object getVariable(String name);\n   922\u2192\n   923\u2192    /**\n   924\u2192     * To get a variable by name and covert to the given type.\n   925\u2192     *\n   926\u2192     * @param  name the variable name. Can be prefixed with repo-id:name to lookup the variable from a specific\n   927\u2192     *              repository. If no repo-id is provided, then global repository will be used.\n   928\u2192     * @param  type the type to convert the variable to\n   929\u2192     * @return      the variable, or <tt>null</tt> if not found.\n   930\u2192     */\n   931\u2192    <T> T getVariable(String name, Class<T> type);\n   932\u2192\n   933\u2192    /**\n   934\u2192     * Sets a variable\n   935\u2192     *\n   936\u2192     * @param name  the variable name. Can be prefixed with repo-id:name to store the variable in a specific repository.\n   937\u2192     *              If no repo-id is provided, then global repository will be used.\n   938\u2192     * @param value the value of the variable\n   939\u2192     */\n   940\u2192    void setVariable(String name, Object value);\n   941\u2192\n   942\u2192    /**\n   943\u2192     * Returns the configured properties component or create one if none has been configured.\n   944\u2192     *\n   945\u2192     * @return the properties component\n   946\u2192     */\n   947\u2192    PropertiesComponent getPropertiesComponent();\n   948\u2192\n   949\u2192    /**\n   950\u2192     * Sets a custom properties component to be used.\n   951\u2192     */\n   952\u2192    void setPropertiesComponent(PropertiesComponent propertiesComponent);\n   953\u2192\n   954\u2192    /**\n   955\u2192     * Gets a readonly list with the names of the languages currently registered.\n   956\u2192     *\n   957\u2192     * @return a readonly list with the names of the languages\n   958\u2192     */\n   959\u2192    Set<String> getLanguageNames();\n   960\u2192\n   961\u2192    /**\n   962\u2192     * Creates a new {@link ProducerTemplate} which is <b>started</b> and therefore ready to use right away.\n   963\u2192     * <p/>\n   964\u2192     * See this FAQ before use:\n   965\u2192     * <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/why-does-camel-use-too-many-threads-with-producertemplate.html\"> Why does Camel\n   966\u2192     * use too many threads with ProducerTemplate?</a>\n   967\u2192     * <p/>\n   968\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> Make sure to call {@link org.apache.camel.ProducerTemplate#stop()} when you are done using the\n   969\u2192     * template, to clean up any resources.\n   970\u2192     * <p/>\n   971\u2192     * Will use cache size defined in Camel property with key {@link Exchange#MAXIMUM_CACHE_POOL_SIZE}. If no key was\n   972\u2192     * defined then it will fallback to a default size of 1000. You can also use the\n   973\u2192     * {@link org.apache.camel.ProducerTemplate#setMaximumCacheSize(int)} method to use a custom value before starting\n   974\u2192     * the template.\n   975\u2192     *\n   976\u2192     * @return                       the template\n   977\u2192     * @throws RuntimeCamelException is thrown if error starting the template\n   978\u2192     */\n   979\u2192    ProducerTemplate createProducerTemplate();\n   980\u2192\n   981\u2192    /**\n   982\u2192     * Creates a new {@link ProducerTemplate} which is <b>started</b> and therefore ready to use right away.\n   983\u2192     * <p/>\n   984\u2192     * See this FAQ before use:\n   985\u2192     * <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/why-does-camel-use-too-many-threads-with-producertemplate.html\"> Why does Camel\n   986\u2192     * use too many threads with ProducerTemplate?</a>\n   987\u2192     * <p/>\n   988\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> Make sure to call {@link ProducerTemplate#stop()} when you are done using the template, to\n   989\u2192     * clean up any resources.\n   990\u2192     *\n   991\u2192     * @param  maximumCacheSize      the maximum cache size\n   992\u2192     * @return                       the template\n   993\u2192     * @throws RuntimeCamelException is thrown if error starting the template\n   994\u2192     */\n   995\u2192    ProducerTemplate createProducerTemplate(int maximumCacheSize);\n   996\u2192\n   997\u2192    /**\n   998\u2192     * Creates a new {@link FluentProducerTemplate} which is <b>started</b> and therefore ready to use right away.\n   999\u2192     * <p/>\n  1000\u2192     * See this FAQ before use:\n  1001\u2192     * <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/why-does-camel-use-too-many-threads-with-producertemplate.html\"> Why does Camel\n  1002\u2192     * use too many threads with ProducerTemplate?</a>\n  1003\u2192     * <p/>\n  1004\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> Make sure to call {@link org.apache.camel.FluentProducerTemplate#stop()} when you are done\n  1005\u2192     * using the template, to clean up any resources.\n  1006\u2192     * <p/>\n  1007\u2192     * Will use cache size defined in Camel property with key {@link Exchange#MAXIMUM_CACHE_POOL_SIZE}. If no key was\n  1008\u2192     * defined then it will fallback to a default size of 1000. You can also use the\n  1009\u2192     * {@link org.apache.camel.FluentProducerTemplate#setMaximumCacheSize(int)} method to use a custom value before\n  1010\u2192     * starting the template.\n  1011\u2192     *\n  1012\u2192     * @return                       the template\n  1013\u2192     * @throws RuntimeCamelException is thrown if error starting the template\n  1014\u2192     */\n  1015\u2192    FluentProducerTemplate createFluentProducerTemplate();\n  1016\u2192\n  1017\u2192    /**\n  1018\u2192     * Creates a new {@link FluentProducerTemplate} which is <b>started</b> and therefore ready to use right away.\n  1019\u2192     * <p/>\n  1020\u2192     * See this FAQ before use:\n  1021\u2192     * <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/why-does-camel-use-too-many-threads-with-producertemplate.html\"> Why does Camel\n  1022\u2192     * use too many threads with ProducerTemplate?</a>\n  1023\u2192     * <p/>\n  1024\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> Make sure to call {@link FluentProducerTemplate#stop()} when you are done using the template,\n  1025\u2192     * to clean up any resources.\n  1026\u2192     *\n  1027\u2192     * @param  maximumCacheSize      the maximum cache size\n  1028\u2192     * @return                       the template\n  1029\u2192     * @throws RuntimeCamelException is thrown if error starting the template\n  1030\u2192     */\n  1031\u2192    FluentProducerTemplate createFluentProducerTemplate(int maximumCacheSize);\n  1032\u2192\n  1033\u2192    /**\n  1034\u2192     * Creates a new {@link ConsumerTemplate} which is <b>started</b> and therefore ready to use right away.\n  1035\u2192     * <p/>\n  1036\u2192     * See this FAQ before use:\n  1037\u2192     * <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/why-does-camel-use-too-many-threads-with-producertemplate.html\"> Why does Camel\n  1038\u2192     * use too many threads with ProducerTemplate?</a> as it also applies for ConsumerTemplate.\n  1039\u2192     * <p/>\n  1040\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> Make sure to call {@link ConsumerTemplate#stop()} when you are done using the template, to\n  1041\u2192     * clean up any resources.\n  1042\u2192     * <p/>\n  1043\u2192     * Will use cache size defined in Camel property with key {@link Exchange#MAXIMUM_CACHE_POOL_SIZE}. If no key was\n  1044\u2192     * defined then it will fallback to a default size of 1000. You can also use the\n  1045\u2192     * {@link org.apache.camel.ConsumerTemplate#setMaximumCacheSize(int)} method to use a custom value before starting\n  1046\u2192     * the template.\n  1047\u2192     *\n  1048\u2192     * @return                       the template\n  1049\u2192     * @throws RuntimeCamelException is thrown if error starting the template\n  1050\u2192     */\n  1051\u2192    ConsumerTemplate createConsumerTemplate();\n  1052\u2192\n  1053\u2192    /**\n  1054\u2192     * Creates a new {@link ConsumerTemplate} which is <b>started</b> and therefore ready to use right away.\n  1055\u2192     * <p/>\n  1056\u2192     * See this FAQ before use:\n  1057\u2192     * <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/why-does-camel-use-too-many-threads-with-producertemplate.html\"> Why does Camel\n  1058\u2192     * use too many threads with ProducerTemplate?</a> as it also applies for ConsumerTemplate.\n  1059\u2192     * <p/>\n  1060\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> Make sure to call {@link ConsumerTemplate#stop()} when you are done using the template, to\n  1061\u2192     * clean up any resources.\n  1062\u2192     *\n  1063\u2192     * @param  maximumCacheSize      the maximum cache size\n  1064\u2192     * @return                       the template\n  1065\u2192     * @throws RuntimeCamelException is thrown if error starting the template\n  1066\u2192     */\n  1067\u2192 1071\u2192     *\n  1072\u2192     * @param  name the data format name or a reference to it in the {@link Registry}\n  1073\u2192     * @return      the resolved data format, or <tt>null</tt> if not found\n  1074\u2192     */\n  1075\u2192    DataFormat resolveDataFormat(String name);\n  1076\u2192\n  1077\u2192    /**\n  1078\u2192     * Creates a new instance of the given data format given its name.\n  1079\u2192     *\n  1080\u2192     * @param  name the data format name or a reference to a data format factory in the {@link Registry}\n  1081\u2192     * @return      the created data format, or <tt>null</tt> if not found\n  1082\u2192     */\n  1083\u2192    DataFormat createDataFormat(String name);\n  1084\u2192\n  1085\u2192    /**\n  1086\u2192     * Gets a readonly list of names of the data formats currently registered\n  1087\u2192     *\n  1088\u2192     * @return a readonly list with the names of the data formats\n  1089\u2192     */\n  1090\u2192    Set<String> getDataFormatNames();\n  1091\u2192\n  1092\u2192    /**\n  1093\u2192     * Resolve a transformer given a scheme\n  1094\u2192     *\n  1095\u2192     * @param  name the transformer name, usually a combination of some scheme and name.\n  1096\u2192     * @return      the resolved transformer, or <tt>null</tt> if not found\n  1097\u2192     */\n  1098\u2192    Transformer resolveTransformer(String name);\n  1099\u2192\n  1100\u2192    /**\n  1101\u2192     * Resolve a transformer given from/to data type.\n  1102\u2192     *\n  1103\u2192     * @param  from from data type\n  1104\u2192     * @param  to   to data type\n  1105\u2192     * @return      the resolved transformer, or <tt>null</tt> if not found\n  1106\u2192     */\n  1107\u2192    Transformer resolveTransformer(DataType from, DataType to);\n  1108\u2192\n  1109\u2192    /**\n  1110\u2192     * Gets the {@link org.apache.camel.spi.TransformerRegistry}\n  1111\u2192     *\n  1112\u2192     * @return the TransformerRegistry\n  1113\u2192     */\n  1114\u2192    TransformerRegistry getTransformerRegistry();\n  1115\u2192\n  1116\u2192    /**\n  1117\u2192     * Resolve a validator given from/to data type.\n  1118\u2192     *\n  1119\u2192     * @param  type the data type\n  1120\u2192     * @return      the resolved validator, or <tt>null</tt> if not found\n  1121\u2192     */\n  1122\u2192    Validator resolveValidator(DataType type);\n  1123\u2192\n  1124\u2192    /**\n  1125\u2192     * Gets the {@link org.apache.camel.spi.ValidatorRegistry}\n  1126\u2192     *\n  1127\u2192     * @return the ValidatorRegistry\n  1128\u2192     */\n  1129\u2192    ValidatorRegistry getValidatorRegistry();\n  1130\u2192\n  1131\u2192    /**\n  1132\u2192     * Sets global options that can be referenced in the camel context\n  1133\u2192     * <p/>\n  1134\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> This has nothing to do with property placeholders, and is just a plain set of key/value pairs\n  1135\u2192     * which are used to configure global options on CamelContext, such as a maximum debug logging length etc. For\n  1136\u2192     * property placeholders use {@link #resolvePropertyPlaceholders(String)} method and see more details at the\n  1137\u2192     * <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/using-propertyplaceholder.html\">property placeholder</a> documentation.\n  1138\u2192     *\n  1139\u2192     * @param globalOptions global options that can be referenced in the camel context\n  1140\u2192     */\n  1141\u2192    void setGlobalOptions(Map<String, String> globalOptions);\n  1142\u2192\n  1143\u2192    /**\n  1144\u2192     * Gets global options that can be referenced in the camel context.\n  1145\u2192     * <p/>\n CamelContext, such as a maximum debug logging length etc. For\n  1148\u2192     * property placeholders use {@link #resolvePropertyPlaceholders(String)} method and see more details at the\n context\n  1152\u2192     */\n  1153\u2192    Map<String, String> getGlobalOptions();\n  1154\u2192\n  1155\u2192    /**\n  1156\u2192     * Gets the global option value that can be referenced in the see more details at the\n  1161\u2192     * <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/using-propertyplaceholder.html\">property placeholder</a> documentation.\n  1162\u2192     *\n  1163\u2192     * @return the string value of the global option\n  1164\u2192     */\n  1165\u2192    String getGlobalOption(String key);\n  1166\u2192\n  1167\u2192    /**\n  1168\u2192     * Returns the class resolver to be resolver);\n  1180\u2192\n  1181\u2192    /**\n  1182\u2192     * Gets the management strategy\n  1183\u2192     *\n  1184\u2192     * @return the management strategy\n  1185\u2192     */\n Instead use\n  1236\u2192     * {@link RouteController} via {@link CamelContext}.\n  1237\u2192     *\n  1238\u2192     * @return the strategy\n  1239\u2192     */\n  1240\u2192    ShutdownStrategy Turning this on increases\n  1348\u2192     * the verbosity of tracing by including events from internal routes in the templates or kamelets.\n  1349\u2192     */\n  1350\u2192    boolean isBacklogTracingTemplates();\n  1351\u2192\n  1352\u2192    /**\n  1353\u2192     * Whether backlog tracing should trace details from rest-dsl.\n  1354\u2192     */\n  1355\u2192    void setBacklogTracingRests(boolean backlogTracingRests);\n  1356\u2192\n  1357\u2192    /**\n  1358\u2192     * Whether backlog tracing should trace details from rest-dsl.\n  1359\u2192     */\n  1360\u2192    boolean isBacklogTracingRests();\n  1361\u2192\n  1362\u2192    /**\n  1363\u2192     * Gets the current {@link UuidGenerator}\n  1364\u2192     *\n  1365\u2192     * @return the uuidGenerator\n  1366\u2192     */\n  1367\u2192    UuidGenerator getUuidGenerator();\n  1368\u2192\n  1369\u2192    /**\n  1370\u2192     * Sets a custom {@link UuidGenerator} (should only be set once)\n  1371\u2192     *\n  1372\u2192     * @param uuidGenerator the UUID Generator\n  1373\u2192     */\n  1374\u2192    void setUuidGenerator(UuidGenerator uuidGenerator);\n  1375\u2192\n  1376\u2192    /**\n  1377\u2192     * Whether to load custom type converters by scanning classpath. Its recommended to migrate to use fast type converter loading by setting <tt>@Converter(loader = true)</tt>\n  1379\u2192     * on your custom type converter classes.\n  1380\u2192     */\n  1381\u2192    Boolean isLoadTypeConverters();\n  1382\u2192\n  1383\u2192    /**\n  1384\u2192     * Whether to load custom type converters by scanning classpath. 1401\u2192\n  1402\u2192    /**\n  1403\u2192     * Used for exclusive filtering of routes to not automatically start with Camel starts.\n  1404\u2192     *\n  1405\u2192     * The pattern support matching by route id or endpoint urls.\n  1406\u2192     *\n  1407\u2192     * Multiple patterns can be specified separated by comma, as example, to exclude all the routes starting from kafka\n  1408\u2192     * or jms use: kafka,jms.\n  1409\u2192     */\n  1410\u2192    void setAutoStartupExcludePattern(String autoStartupExcludePattern);\n  1411\u2192\n  1412\u2192    /**\n  1413\u2192     * Used for exclusive filtering of routes to not automatically start with Camel starts.\n  1414\u2192     *\n  1415\u2192     * The pattern support matching by route id or endpoint urls.\n  1416\u2192     *\n  1417\u2192     * Multiple patterns can be specified separated by comma, as example, to exclude all the routes starting from kafka\n  1418\u2192     * or jms use: kafka,jms.\n  1419\u2192     */\n  1420\u2192    String getAutoStartupExcludePattern();\n  1421\u2192\n  1422\u2192    /**\n  1423\u2192     * Whether to capture precise source location:line-number for all EIPs in Camel routes.\n  1424\u2192     *\n  1425\u2192     * Enabling this will impact parsing Java based routes (also Groovy, etc.) on startup as this uses\n  1426\u2192     * {@link StackTraceElement} to calculate the location from the Camel route, which comes with a performance cost.\n  1427\u2192     * This only impact startup, not the performance of the routes at runtime.\n  1428\u2192     */\n  1429\u2192    Boolean isSourceLocationEnabled();\n  1430\u2192\n  1431\u2192    /**\n  1432\u2192     * Whether to capture precise source location:line-number for all EIPs in Camel routes.\n  1433\u2192     *\n route, which comes with a performance cost.\n  1436\u2192     * This only impact startup, not the performance of the routes at runtime.\n  1437\u2192     */\n  1438\u2192    void setSourceLocationEnabled(Boolean sourceLocationEnabled);\n  1439\u2192\n  1440\u2192    /**\n  1441\u2192     * Whether to support JBang style //DEPS to specify additional dependencies when running Camel JBang\n  1442\u2192     */\n  1443\u2192    Boolean isModeline();\n  1444\u2192\n  1445\u2192    /**\n  1446\u2192     * Whether to support JBang style //DEPS to specify additional dependencies when running Camel JBang\n  1447\u2192     */\n  1448\u2192    void setModeline(Boolean modeline);\n  1449\u2192\n  1450\u2192    /**\n  1451\u2192     * Whether to enable developer console (requires camel-console on classpath).\n  1452\u2192     *\n  1453\u2192     * The developer console is only for assisting during development. This is NOT for production usage.\n  1454\u2192     */\n  1455\u2192    Boolean isDevConsole();\n  1456\u2192\n  1457\u2192    /**\n  1458\u2192     * Whether to enable developer console (requires camel-console on classpath)\n  1459\u2192     *\n  1460\u2192     * The developer console is only for assisting during development. This is NOT for production usage.\n  1461\u2192     */\n  1462\u2192    void setDevConsole(Boolean loadDevConsoles);\n  1463\u2192\n  1464\u2192    /**\n  1465\u2192     * Whether or not type converter statistics is enabled.\n  1466\u2192     * <p/>\n  1467\u2192     * By default the type converter utilization statistics is disabled. <b>Notice:</b> If enabled then there is a\n  1468\u2192     * slight performance impact under very heavy load.\n  1469\u2192     *\n  1470\u2192     * @return <tt>true</tt> if enabled, <tt>false</tt> if disabled (default).\n  1471\u2192     */\n  1472\u2192    Boolean isTypeConverterStatisticsEnabled();\n  1473\u2192\n  1474\u2192    /**\n  1475\u2192     * Sets whether or not type converter statistics is enabled.\n  1476\u2192     * <p/>\n  1477\u2192     * By default the type converter utilization statistics is disabled. <b>Notice:</b> If enabled then there is a\n  1478\u2192     * slight performance impact under very heavy load.\n  1479\u2192     * <p/>\n  1480\u2192     * You can enable/disable the statistics at runtime using the\n  1481\u2192     * {@link org.apache.camel.spi.TypeConverterRegistry#getStatistics()#setTypeConverterStatisticsEnabled(Boolean)}\n  1482\u2192     * method, or from JMX on the {@link org.apache.camel.api.management.mbean.ManagedTypeConverterRegistryMBean} mbean.\n  1483\u2192     *\n  1484\u2192     * @param typeConverterStatisticsEnabled <tt>true</tt> to enable, <tt>false</tt> to disable\n  1485\u2192     */\n  1486\u2192    void setTypeConverterStatisticsEnabled(Boolean typeConverterStatisticsEnabled);\n  1487\u2192\n  1488\u2192    /**\n  1489\u2192     * Whether or not <a href=\"http://www.slf4j.org/api/org/slf4j/MDC.html\">MDC</a> logging is being enabled.\n  1490\u2192     *\n  1491\u2192     * @return <tt>true</tt> if MDC logging is enabled\n  1492\u2192     */\n  1493\u2192    Boolean isUseMDCLogging();\n  1494\u2192\n  1495\u2192    /**\n  1496\u2192     * Set whether <a href=\"http://www.slf4j.org/api/org/slf4j/MDC.html\">MDC</a> is enabled.\n  1497\u2192     *\n  1498\u2192     * @param useMDCLogging <tt>true</tt> to enable MDC logging, <tt>false</tt> to disable\n  1499\u2192     */\n  1500\u2192    void setUseMDCLogging(Boolean useMDCLogging);\n  1501\u2192\n  1502\u2192    /**\n  1503\u2192     * Gets the pattern used for determine which custom MDC keys to propagate during message routing when the routing\n  1504\u2192     * engine continues routing asynchronously for the given message. Setting this pattern to <tt>*</tt> will propagate\n  1505\u2192     * all custom keys. Or setting the pattern to <tt>foo*,bar*</tt> will propagate any keys starting with either foo or\n  1506\u2192     * bar. Notice that a set of standard Camel MDC keys are always propagated which starts with <tt>camel.</tt> as key\n  1507\u2192     * name.\n  1508\u2192     * <p/>\n  1509\u2192     * The match rules are applied in this order (case insensitive):\n  1510\u2192     * <ul>\n  1511\u2192     * <li>exact match, returns true</li>\n  1512\u2192     * <li>wildcard match (pattern ends with a * and the name starts with the pattern), returns true</li>\n  1513\u2192     * <li>regular expression match, returns true</li>\n  1514\u2192     * <li>otherwise returns false</li>\n  1515\u2192     * </ul>\n  1516\u2192     */\n  1517\u2192    String getMDCLoggingKeysPattern();\n  1518\u2192\n  1519\u2192    /**\n  1520\u2192     * Sets the pattern used for determine which custom MDC keys to propagate during message routing when the routing\n  1521\u2192     * engine continues routing asynchronously for the given message. Setting this pattern to <tt>*</tt> will propagate\n  1522\u2192     * all custom keys. Or setting the pattern to <tt>foo*,bar*</tt> will propagate any keys starting with either foo or\n  1523\u2192     * bar. Notice that a set of standard Camel MDC keys are always propagated which starts with <tt>camel.</tt> as key\n  1524\u2192     * name.\n  1525\u2192     * <p/>\n  1526\u2192     * The match rules are applied in this order (case insensitive):\n  1527\u2192     * <ul>\n  1528\u2192     * <li>exact match, returns true</li>\n  1529\u2192     * <li>wildcard match (pattern ends with a * and the name starts with the pattern), returns true</li>\n  1530\u2192     * <li>regular expression match, returns true</li>\n  1531\u2192     * <li>otherwise returns false</li>\n  1532\u2192     * </ul>\n  1533\u2192     *\n  1534\u2192     * @param pattern the pattern\n  1535\u2192     */\n  1536\u2192    void setMDCLoggingKeysPattern(String pattern);\n  1537\u2192\n  1538\u2192    /**\n  1539\u2192     * To use a custom tracing logging format.\n  1540\u2192     *\n  1541\u2192     * The default format (arrow, routeId, label) is: %-4.4s [%-12.12s] [%-33.33s]\n  1542\u2192     */\n  1543\u2192    String getTracingLoggingFormat();\n  1544\u2192\n  1545\u2192    /**\n  1546\u2192     * To use a custom tracing logging format.\n  1547\u2192     *\n  1548\u2192     * The default format (arrow, routeId, label) is: %-4.4s [%-12.12s] [%-33.33s]\n  1549\u2192     *\n  1550\u2192     * @param format the logging format\n  1551\u2192     */\n  1552\u2192    void setTracingLoggingFormat(String format);\n  1553\u2192\n  1554\u2192    /**\n  1555\u2192     * Whether tracing should trace inner details from route templates (or kamelets). Turning this on increases the\n  1556\u2192     * verbosity of tracing by including events from internal routes in the templates or kamelets.\n  1557\u2192     */\n  1558\u2192    void setTracingTemplates(boolean tracingTemplates);\n  1559\u2192\n  1560\u2192    /**\n  1561\u2192     * Whether tracing should trace inner details from route templates (or kamelets). Turning this off can reduce the\n  1562\u2192     * verbosity of tracing when using many route templates, and allow to focus on tracing your own Camel routes only.\n  1563\u2192     */\n  1564\u2192    boolean isTracingTemplates();\n  1565\u2192\n  1566\u2192    /**\n  1567\u2192     * Whether tracing should trace details from rest-dsl.\n  1568\u2192     */\n  1569\u2192    void setTracingRests(boolean tracingRests);\n  1570\u2192\n  1571\u2192    /**\n  1572\u2192     * Whether tracing should trace details from rest-dsl.\n  1573\u2192     */\n  1574\u2192    boolean isTracingRests();\n  1575\u2192\n  1576\u2192    /**\n  1577\u2192     * If dumping is enabled then Camel will during startup dump all loaded routes (incl rests and route templates)\n  1578\u2192     * represented as XML DSL into the log. This is intended for trouble shooting or to assist during development.\n  1579\u2192     *\n  1580\u2192     * Sensitive information that may be configured in the route endpoints could potentially be included in the dump\n  1581\u2192     * output and is therefore not recommended to be used for production usage.\n  1582\u2192     *\n  1583\u2192     * This requires to have camel-xml-jaxb on the classpath to be able to dump the routes as XML.\n  1584\u2192     *\n  1585\u2192     * @return <tt>xml</tt>, or <tt>yaml</tt> if dumping is enabled\n  1586\u2192     */\n  1587\u2192    String getDumpRoutes();\n  1588\u2192\n  1589\u2192    /**\n  1590\u2192     * If dumping is enabled then Camel will during startup dump all loaded routes (incl rests and route templates)\n  1591\u2192     * represented as XML/YAML DSL into the log. This is intended for trouble shooting or to assist during development.\n  1592\u2192     *\n  1593\u2192     * Sensitive information that may be configured in the route endpoints could potentially be included in the dump\n  1594\u2192     * output and is therefore not recommended being used for production usage.\n  1595\u2192     *\n  1596\u2192     * This requires to have camel-xml-io/camel-yaml-io on the classpath to be able to dump the routes as XML/YAML.\n  1597\u2192     *\n  1598\u2192     * @param format xml or yaml (additional configuration can be specified using query parameters, eg\n  1599\u2192     *               ?include=all&uriAsParameters=true)\n  1600\u2192     */\n  1601\u2192    void setDumpRoutes(String format);\n  1602\u2192\n  1603\u2192    /**\n  1604\u2192     * Whether to enable using data type on Camel messages.\n  1605\u2192     * <p/>\n  1606\u2192     * Data type are automatic turned on if one or more routes has been explicit configured with input and output types.\n  1607\u2192     * Otherwise, data type is default off.\n  1608\u2192     *\n  1609\u2192     * @return <tt>true</tt> if data type is enabled\n  1610\u2192 Data type are automatic turned on if one or more routes has been explicit configured with input and output types.\n  1617\u2192     * Otherwise, data type is default off.\n  1618\u2192     *\n
