org.apache.camel.util.function.Suppliers;\n    48\u2192import org.slf4j.Logger;\n    49\u2192import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192/**\n    52\u2192 * Default component to use for base for components implementations.\n    53\u2192 */\n    54\u2192public abstract class DefaultComponent extends ServiceSupport implements Component {\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(DefaultComponent.class);\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192    /**\n    59\u2192     * Simple RAW() pattern used only for validating URI in this class\n    60\u2192     */\n    61\u2192    private static final Pattern RAW_PATTERN = Pattern.compile(\"RAW[({].*&&.*[)}]\");\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192    private volatile PropertyConfigurer componentPropertyConfigurer;\n    64\u2192    private volatile PropertyConfigurer endpointPropertyConfigurer;\n    65\u2192    private volatile String defaultName;\n    66\u2192    private final List<Supplier<ComponentExtension>> extensions = new ArrayList<>();\n    67\u2192    private CamelContext camelContext;\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"advanced\", defaultValue = \"true\",\n    70\u2192              description = \"Whether autowiring is enabled. This is used for automatic autowiring options (the option must be marked as autowired)\"\n    71\u2192                            + \" by looking up in the registry to find if there is a single instance of matching type, which then gets configured on the component.\"\n    72\u2192                            + \" This can be used for automatic configuring JDBC data sources, JMS connection factories, AWS Clients, etc.\")\n    73\u2192    private boolean autowiredEnabled = true;\n    74\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"consumer\",\n    75\u2192              description = \"Allows for bridging the consumer to the Camel routing Error Handler, which mean any exceptions (if possible) occurred while\"\n    76\u2192                            + \" the Camel consumer is trying to pickup incoming messages, or the likes, will now be processed as a message and handled by the routing Error Handler.\"\n    77\u2192                            + \" Important: This is only possible if the 3rd party component allows Camel to be alerted if an exception was thrown. Some components handle this internally only,\"\n    78\u2192                            + \" and therefore bridgeErrorHandler is not possible. In other situations we may improve the Camel component to hook into the 3rd party component\"\n    79\u2192                            + \" and make this possible for future releases.\"\n    80\u2192                            + \" By default the consumer will use the org.apache.camel.spi.ExceptionHandler to deal with exceptions, that will be logged at WARN or ERROR level and ignored.\")\n    81\u2192    private boolean bridgeErrorHandler;\n    82\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"producer\",\n    83\u2192              description = \"Whether the producer should be started lazy (on the first message). By starting lazy you can use this to allow CamelContext and routes to startup\"\n    84\u2192                            + \" in situations where a producer may otherwise fail during starting and cause the route to fail being started. By deferring this startup to be lazy then\"\n    85\u2192                            + \" the startup failure can be handled during routing messages via Camel's routing error handlers. Beware that when the first message is processed\"\n    86\u2192                            + \" then creating and starting the producer may take a little time and prolong the total processing time of the processing.\")\n    87\u2192    private boolean lazyStartProducer;\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192    public DefaultComponent() {\n    90\u2192    }\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192    public DefaultComponent(CamelContext context) {\n    93\u2192        this.camelContext = context;\n    94\u2192    }\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192    @Override\n    97\u2192    public Endpoint createEndpoint(String uri, Map<String, Object> properties) throws Exception {\n    98\u2192        // need to encode before its safe to parse with java.net.Uri\n    99\u2192        String encodedUri = UnsafeUriCharactersEncoder.encode(uri);\n   100\u2192        URI u = new URI(encodedUri);\n   101\u2192        String path;\n   102\u2192        if (u.getScheme() != null) {\n   103\u2192            // if there is a scheme then there is also a path\n   104\u2192            path = URISupport.extractRemainderPath(u, useRawUri());\n   105\u2192        } else {\n   106\u2192            // this uri has no context-path as the leading text is the component name (scheme)\n   107\u2192            path = null;\n   108\u2192        }\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192        // use encoded or raw uri?\n   111\u2192        Map<String, Object> parameters;\n   112\u2192        if (useRawUri()) {\n   113\u2192            // when using raw uri then the query is taking from the uri as is\n   114\u2192            String query = StringHelper.after(uri, \"?\");\n   115\u2192            if (query == null) {\n   116\u2192                query = u.getRawQuery();\n   117\u2192            }\n   118\u2192            // and use method parseQuery\n   119\u2192            parameters = URISupport.parseQuery(query, true);\n   120\u2192        } else {\n   121\u2192            // however when using the encoded (default mode) uri then the query,\n   122\u2192            // is taken from the URI (ensures values is URI encoded)\n   123\u2192            // and use method parseParameters\n   124\u2192            parameters = URISupport.parseParameters(u);\n   125\u2192        }\n   126\u2192        if (properties != null) {\n   127\u2192            parameters.putAll(properties);\n   128\u2192        }\n   129\u2192        // This special property is only to identify endpoints in a unique manner\n   130\u2192        parameters.remove(\"hash\");\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192        if (resolveRawParameterValues()) {\n   133\u2192            // parameters using raw syntax: RAW(value)\n   134\u2192            // should have the token removed, so its only the value we have in parameters, as we are about to create\n   135\u2192            // an endpoint and want to have the parameter values without the RAW tokens\n   136\u2192            RawParameterHelper.resolveRawParameterValues(camelContext, parameters);\n   137\u2192        }\n   138\u2192\n   139\u2192        // use encoded or raw uri?\n   140\u2192        uri = useRawUri() ? uri : encodedUri;\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192        validateURI(uri, path, parameters);\n   143\u2192        if (LOG.isTraceEnabled()) {\n   144\u2192            // at trace level its okay to have parameters logged, that may contain passwords\n   145\u2192            LOG.trace(\"Creating endpoint uri=[{}], path=[{}], parameters=[{}]\", URISupport.sanitizeUri(uri),\n   146\u2192                    URISupport.sanitizePath(path), parameters);\n   147\u2192        } else if (LOG.isDebugEnabled()) {\n   148\u2192            // but at debug level only output sanitized uris\n   149\u2192            LOG.debug(\"Creating endpoint uri=[{}], path=[{}]\", URISupport.sanitizeUri(uri), URISupport.sanitizePath(path));\n   150\u2192        }\n   151\u2192\n   152\u2192        boolean bridge = bridgeErrorHandler || getCamelContext().getGlobalEndpointConfiguration().isBridgeErrorHandler();\n   153\u2192        Boolean bool = getAndRemoveParameter(parameters, \"bridgeErrorHandler\", Boolean.class);\n   154\u2192        if (bool != null) {\n   155\u2192            bridge = bool;\n   156\u2192        }\n   157\u2192        boolean lazy = lazyStartProducer || getCamelContext().getGlobalEndpointConfiguration().isLazyStartProducer();\n   158\u2192        bool = getAndRemoveParameter(parameters, \"lazyStartProducer\", Boolean.class);\n   159\u2192        if (bool != null) {\n   160\u2192            lazy = bool;\n   161\u2192        }\n   162\u2192        // camel context can turn off autowire globally unless there is a uri parameter\n   163\u2192        boolean autowire = camelContext.isAutowiredEnabled()\n   164\u2192                && (autowiredEnabled || getCamelContext().getGlobalEndpointConfiguration().isAutowiredEnabled());\n   165\u2192        bool = getAndRemoveParameter(parameters, \"autowiredEnabled\", Boolean.class);\n   166\u2192        if (bool != null) {\n   167\u2192            autowire = bool;\n   168\u2192        }\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192        // create endpoint\n   171\u2192        Endpoint endpoint = createEndpoint(uri, path, parameters);\n   172\u2192        if (endpoint == null) {\n   173\u2192            return null;\n   174\u2192        }\n   175\u2192        // inject camel context\n   176\u2192        endpoint.setCamelContext(getCamelContext());\n   177\u2192\n   178\u2192        // and setup those global options afterwards\n   179\u2192        if (endpoint instanceof DefaultEndpoint de) {\n   180\u2192            de.setBridgeErrorHandler(bridge);\n   181\u2192            de.setLazyStartProducer(lazy);\n   182\u2192            de.setAutowiredEnabled(autowire);\n   183\u2192        }\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192        // configure remainder of the parameters\n   186\u2192        setProperties(endpoint, parameters);\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192        // if endpoint is strict (not lenient) and we have unknown parameters configured then\n   189\u2192        // fail if there are parameters that could not be set, then they are probably misspell or not supported at all\n   190\u2192        if (!endpoint.isLenientProperties()) {\n   191\u2192            validateParameters(uri, parameters, null);\n   192\u2192        }\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192        // allow custom configuration after properties has been configured\n   195\u2192        if (endpoint instanceof AfterPropertiesConfigured afterPropertiesConfigured) {\n   196\u2192            afterPropertiesConfigured.afterPropertiesConfigured(getCamelContext());\n   197\u2192        }\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192        afterConfiguration(uri, path, endpoint, parameters);\n   200\u2192        return endpoint;\n   201\u2192    }\n   202\u2192\n   203\u2192    @Override\n   204\u2192    public Endpoint createEndpoint(String uri) throws Exception {\n   205\u2192        return createEndpoint(uri, null);\n   206\u2192    }\n   207\u2192\n   208\u2192    @Override\n   209\u2192    public boolean useRawUri() {\n   210\u2192        // should use encoded uri by default\n   211\u2192        return false;\n   212\u2192    }\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192    public boolean isLazyStartProducer() {\n   215\u2192        return lazyStartProducer;\n   216\u2192    }\n   217\u2192\n   218\u2192    /**\n   219\u2192     * Whether the producer should be started lazy (on the first message). By starting lazy you can use this to allow\n   220\u2192     * CamelContext and routes to startup in situations where a producer may otherwise fail during starting and cause\n   221\u2192     * the route to fail being started. By deferring this startup to be lazy then the startup failure can be handled\n   222\u2192     * during routing messages via Camel's routing error handlers. Beware that when the first message is processed then\n   223\u2192     * creating and starting the producer may take a little time and prolong the total processing time of the\n   224\u2192     * processing.\n   225\u2192     */\n   226\u2192    public void setLazyStartProducer(boolean lazyStartProducer) {\n   227\u2192        this.lazyStartProducer = lazyStartProducer;\n   228\u2192    }\n   229\u2192\n   230\u2192    public boolean isBridgeErrorHandler() {\n   231\u2192        return bridgeErrorHandler;\n   232\u2192    }\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192    /**\n   235\u2192     * Allows for bridging the consumer to the Camel routing Error Handler, which mean any exceptions (if possible)\n   236\u2192     * occurred while the Camel consumer is trying to pickup incoming messages, or the likes, will now be processed as a\n   237\u2192     * message and handled by the routing Error Handler. Important: This is only possible if the 3rd party component\n   238\u2192     * allows Camel to be alerted if an exception was thrown. Some components handle this internally only, and therefore\n   239\u2192     * bridgeErrorHandler is not possible. In other situations we may improve the Camel component to hook into the 3rd\n   240\u2192     * party component and make this possible for future releases.\n   241\u2192     * <p/>\n   242\u2192     * By default the consumer will use the org.apache.camel.spi.ExceptionHandler to deal with exceptions, that will be\n   243\u2192     * logged at WARN/ERROR level and ignored.\n   244\u2192     */\n   245\u2192    public void setBridgeErrorHandler(boolean bridgeErrorHandler) {\n   246\u2192        this.bridgeErrorHandler = bridgeErrorHandler;\n   247\u2192    }\n   248\u2192\n   249\u2192    @Override\n   250\u2192    public boolean isAutowiredEnabled() {\n   251\u2192        return autowiredEnabled;\n   252\u2192    }\n   253\u2192\n   254\u2192    /**\n   255\u2192     * Whether autowiring is enabled. This is used for automatic autowiring options (the option must be marked as\n   256\u2192     * autowired) by looking up in the registry to find if there is a single instance of matching type, which then gets\n   257\u2192     * configured on the component. This can be used for automatic configuring JDBC data sources, JMS connection\n   258\u2192     * factories, AWS Clients, etc.\n   259\u2192     */\n   260\u2192    public void setAutowiredEnabled(boolean autowiredEnabled) {\n   261\u2192        this.autowiredEnabled = autowiredEnabled;\n   262\u2192    }\n   263\u2192\n   264\u2192    /**\n   265\u2192     * Strategy to do post configuration logic.\n   266\u2192     * <p/>\n   267\u2192     * Can be used to construct an URI based on the remaining parameters. For example the parameters that configures the\n   268\u2192     * endpoint have been removed from the parameters which leaves only the additional parameters left.\n   269\u2192     *\n   270\u2192     * @param  uri        the uri\n   271\u2192     * @param  remaining  the remaining part of the URI without the query parameters or component prefix\n   272\u2192     * @param  endpoint   the created endpoint\n   273\u2192     * @param  parameters the remaining parameters after the endpoint has been created and parsed the parameters\n   274\u2192     * @throws Exception  can be thrown to indicate error creating the endpoint\n   275\u2192     */\n   276\u2192    protected void afterConfiguration(String uri, String remaining, Endpoint endpoint, Map<String, Object> parameters)\n   277\u2192            throws Exception {\n   278\u2192        // noop\n   279\u2192    }\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192    /**\n   282\u2192     * Strategy for validation of parameters, that was not able to be resolved to any endpoint options.\n   283\u2192     *\n   284\u2192     * @param  uri                            the uri\n   285\u2192     * @param  parameters                     the parameters, an empty map if no parameters given\n   286\u2192     * @param  optionPrefix                   optional prefix to filter the parameters for validation. Use <tt>null</tt>\n   287\u2192     *                                        for validate all.\n   288\u2192     * @throws ResolveEndpointFailedException should be thrown if the URI validation failed\n   289\u2192     */\n   290\u2192    protected void validateParameters(String uri, Map<String, Object> parameters, String optionPrefix) {\n   291\u2192        if (parameters == null || parameters.isEmpty()) {\n   292\u2192            return;\n   293\u2192        }\n   294\u2192\n   295\u2192        Map<String, Object> param = parameters;\n   296\u2192        if (optionPrefix != null) {\n   297\u2192            param = PropertiesHelper.extractProperties(parameters, optionPrefix);\n   298\u2192        }\n   299\u2192\n   300\u2192        if (!param.isEmpty()) {\n   301\u2192            throw new ResolveEndpointFailedException(\n   302\u2192                    uri, \"There are \" + param.size()\n   303\u2192                         + \" parameters that couldn't be set on the endpoint.\"\n   304\u2192                         + \" Check the uri if the parameters are spelt correctly and that they are properties of the endpoint.\"\n   305\u2192                         + \" Unknown parameters=[\" + param + \"]\");\n   306\u2192        }\n   307\u2192    }\n   308\u2192\n   309\u2192    /**\n   310\u2192     * Strategy for validation of the uri when creating the endpoint.\n   311\u2192     *\n   312\u2192     * @param  uri                            the uri\n   313\u2192     * @param  path                           the path - part after the scheme\n   314\u2192     * @param  parameters                     the parameters, an empty map if no parameters given\n   315\u2192     * @throws ResolveEndpointFailedException should be thrown if the URI validation failed\n   316\u2192     */\n   317\u2192    protected void validateURI(String uri, String path, Map<String, Object> parameters) {\n   318\u2192        // check for uri containing double && markers without include by RAW\n   319\u2192        if (uri.contains(\"&&\")) {\n   320\u2192            Matcher m = RAW_PATTERN.matcher(uri);\n   321\u2192            // we should skip the RAW part\n   322\u2192            if (!m.find()) {\n   323\u2192                throw new ResolveEndpointFailedException(\n   324\u2192                        uri, \"Invalid uri syntax: Double && marker found. \"\n   325\u2192                             + \"Check the uri and remove the duplicate & marker.\");\n   326\u2192            }\n   327\u2192        }\n   328\u2192\n   329\u2192        // if we have a trailing & then that is invalid as well\n   330\u2192        if (uri.endsWith(\"&\")) {\n   331\u2192            throw new ResolveEndpointFailedException(\n   332\u2192                    uri, \"Invalid uri syntax: Trailing & marker found. \"\n   333\u2192                         + \"Check the uri and remove the trailing & marker.\");\n   334\u2192        }\n   335\u2192    }\n   336\u2192\n   337\u2192    /**\n   338\u2192     * Configure if the parameters using the RAW token syntax need to be resolved before being consumed by\n   339\u2192     * {@link #createEndpoint(String, Map)}.\n   340\u2192     * <p/>\n   341\u2192     * As the parameters are used to create an endpoint, by default they should have the token removed so its only the\n   342\u2192     * value we have in parameters however there are some cases where the endpoint may act as a proxy for another\n   343\u2192     * endpoint and you need to preserve the values as they are.\n   344\u2192     */\n   345\u2192    protected boolean resolveRawParameterValues() {\n   346\u2192        // should resolve raw parameters by default\n   347\u2192        return true;\n   348\u2192    }\n   349\u2192\n   350\u2192    @Override\n   351\u2192    public CamelContext getCamelContext() {\n   352\u2192        return camelContext;\n   353\u2192    }\n   354\u2192\n   355\u2192    @Override\n   356\u2192    public void setCamelContext(CamelContext context) {\n   357\u2192        this.camelContext = context;\n   358\u2192    }\n   359\u2192\n   360\u2192    @Override\n   361\u2192    public String getDefaultName() {\n   362\u2192        return defaultName;\n   363\u2192    }\n   364\u2192\n   365\u2192    @Override\n   366\u2192    protected void doBuild() throws Exception {\n   367\u2192        if (defaultName == null) {\n   368\u2192            org.apache.camel.spi.annotations.Component ann\n   369\u2192                    = ObjectHelper.getAnnotation(this, org.apache.camel.spi.annotations.Component.class);\n   370\u2192            if (ann != null) {\n   371\u2192                String name = ann.value();\n   372\u2192                // just grab first scheme name if the component has scheme alias (eg http,https)\n   373\u2192                if (name.contains(\",\")) {\n   374\u2192                    name = StringHelper.before(name, \",\");\n   375\u2192                }\n   376\u2192                defaultName = name;\n   377\u2192            }\n   378\u2192        }\n   379\u2192        if (defaultName != null) {\n   380\u2192            if (componentPropertyConfigurer == null) {\n   381\u2192                componentPropertyConfigurer = PluginHelper.getConfigurerResolver(getCamelContext())\n   382\u2192                        .resolvePropertyConfigurer(defaultName + \"-component-configurer\", getCamelContext());\n   383\u2192            }\n   384\u2192            if (endpointPropertyConfigurer == null) {\n   385\u2192                endpointPropertyConfigurer = PluginHelper.getConfigurerResolver(getCamelContext())\n   386\u2192                        .resolvePropertyConfigurer(defaultName + \"-endpoint-configurer\", getCamelContext());\n   387\u2192            }\n   388\u2192        }\n   389\u2192    }\n   390\u2192\n   391\u2192    @Override\n   392\u2192    protected void doInit() throws Exception {\n   393\u2192        ObjectHelper.notNull(getCamelContext(), \"camelContext\");\n   394\u2192    }\n   395\u2192\n   396\u2192    /**\n   397\u2192     * A factory method allowing derived components to create a new endpoint from the given URI, remaining path and\n   398\u2192     * optional parameters\n   399\u2192     *\n   400\u2192     * @param  uri        the full URI of the endpoint\n   401\u2192     * @param  remaining  the remaining part of the URI without the query parameters or component prefix\n   402\u2192     * @param  parameters the optional parameters passed in\n   403\u2192     * @return            a newly created endpoint or null if the endpoint cannot be created based on the inputs\n   404\u2192     * @throws Exception  is thrown if error creating the endpoint\n   405\u2192     */\n   406\u2192    protected abstract Endpoint createEndpoint(String uri, String remaining, Map<String, Object> parameters)\n   407\u2192            throws Exception;\n   408\u2192\n   409\u2192    /**\n   410\u2192     * Configure an endpoint using the given parameters. In the usual cases, this is the only call needed after having\n   411\u2192     * created the endpoint in the {@link #createEndpoint(String, String, Map)} method's implementation.\n   412\u2192     *\n   413\u2192     * This method will call the {@link Endpoint#configureProperties(Map)} method which should delegate the the\n   414\u2192     * endpoint's {@link PropertyConfigurer} instance. In some rare cases, you need to override this method to\n   415\u2192     * explicitely set parameters in case a simple generated configurer can not be used.\n   416\u2192     *\n   417\u2192     * @param endpoint   the endpoint\n   418\u2192     * @param parameters properties to set\n   419\u2192     */\n   420\u2192    protected void setProperties(Endpoint endpoint, Map<String, Object> parameters) throws Exception {\n   421\u2192        endpoint.configureProperties(parameters);\n   422\u2192    }\n   423\u2192\n   424\u2192    /**\n   425\u2192     * Sets the bean properties on the given bean\n   426\u2192     *\n   427\u2192     * @param bean       the bean\n   428\u2192     * @param parameters properties to set\n   429\u2192     */\n   430\u2192    protected void setProperties(Object bean, Map<String, Object> parameters) throws Exception {\n   431\u2192        setProperties(getCamelContext(), bean, parameters);\n   432\u2192    }\n   433\u2192\n   434\u2192    /**\n   435\u2192     * Sets the bean properties on the given bean using the given {@link CamelContext}.\n   436\u2192     *\n   437\u2192     * @param camelContext the {@link CamelContext} to use\n   438\u2192     * @param bean         the bean\n   439\u2192     * @param parameters   properties to set\n   440\u2192     */\n   441\u2192    protected void setProperties(CamelContext camelContext, Object bean, Map<String, Object> parameters) throws Exception {\n   442\u2192        if (parameters == null || parameters.isEmpty()) {\n   443\u2192            return;\n   444\u2192        }\n   445\u2192\n   446\u2192        PropertyConfigurer configurer;\n   447\u2192        if (bean instanceof Component) {\n   448\u2192            configurer = getComponentPropertyConfigurer();\n   449\u2192        } else if (bean instanceof Endpoint) {\n   450\u2192            configurer = getEndpointPropertyConfigurer();\n   451\u2192        } else if (bean instanceof PropertyConfigurerAware propertyConfigurerAware) {\n   452\u2192            configurer = propertyConfigurerAware.getPropertyConfigurer(bean);\n   453\u2192        } else {\n   454\u2192            configurer = null;\n   455\u2192        }\n   456\u2192        // use configurer and ignore case as end users may type an option name with mixed case\n   457\u2192        PropertyBindingSupport.build().withConfigurer(configurer).withIgnoreCase(true).bind(camelContext, bean, parameters);\n   458\u2192    }\n   459\u2192\n   460\u2192    @Override\n   461\u2192    public PropertyConfigurer getComponentPropertyConfigurer() {\n   462\u2192        return componentPropertyConfigurer;\n   463\u2192    }\n   464\u2192\n   465\u2192    @Override\n   466\u2192    public PropertyConfigurer getEndpointPropertyConfigurer() {\n   467\u2192        return endpointPropertyConfigurer;\n   468\u2192    }\n   469\u2192\n   470\u2192    /**\n   471\u2192     * Derived classes may wish to overload this to prevent the default introspection of URI parameters on the created\n   472\u2192     * {@link Endpoint} instance.\n   473\u2192     */\n   474\u2192    protected boolean useIntrospectionOnEndpoint() {\n   475\u2192        return true;\n   476\u2192    }\n   477\u2192\n   478\u2192    /**\n   479\u2192     * Gets the parameter and remove it from the parameter map. This method doesn't resolve reference parameters in the\n   480\u2192     * registry.\n   481\u2192     *\n   482\u2192     * @param  parameters the parameters\n   483\u2192     * @param  key        the key\n   484\u2192     * @param  type       the requested type to convert the value from the parameter\n   485\u2192     * @return            the converted value parameter, <tt>null</tt> if parameter does not exist.\n   486\u2192     * @see               #resolveAndRemoveReferenceParameter(Map, String, Class)\n   487\u2192     */\n   488\u2192    public <T> T getAndRemoveParameter(Map<String, Object> parameters, String key, Class<T> type) {\n   489\u2192        return getAndRemoveParameter(parameters, key, type, null);\n   490\u2192    }\n   491\u2192\n   492\u2192    /**\n   493\u2192     * Gets the parameter and remove it from the parameter map. This method doesn't resolve reference parameters in the\n   494\u2192     * registry.\n   495\u2192     *\n   496\u2192     * @param  parameters   the parameters\n   497\u2192     * @param  key          the key\n   498\u2192     * @param  type         the requested type to convert the value from the parameter\n   499\u2192     * @param  defaultValue use this default value if the parameter does not contain the key\n   500\u2192     * @return              the converted value parameter\n   501\u2192     * @see                 #resolveAndRemoveReferenceParameter(Map, String, Class, Object)\n   502\u2192     */\n   503\u2192    public <T> T getAndRemoveParameter(Map<String, Object> parameters, String key, Class<T> type, T defaultValue) {\n   504\u2192        Object value = parameters.remove(key);\n   505\u2192        if (value != null) {\n   506\u2192            // if we have a value then convert it\n   507\u2192            return CamelContextHelper.mandatoryConvertTo(getCamelContext(), type, value);\n   508\u2192        } else {\n   509\u2192            value = defaultValue;\n   510\u2192        }\n   511\u2192        if (value == null) {\n   512\u2192            return null;\n   513\u2192        }\n   514\u2192\n   515\u2192        return CamelContextHelper.mandatoryConvertTo(getCamelContext(), type, value);\n   516\u2192    }\n   517\u2192\n   518\u2192    /**\n   519\u2192     * Gets the parameter and remove it from the parameter map. This method resolves reference parameters in the\n   520\u2192     * registry as well.\n   521\u2192     *\n   522\u2192     * @param  parameters the parameters\n   523\u2192     * @param  key        the key\n   524\u2192     * @param  type       the requested type to convert the value from the parameter\n   525\u2192     * @return            the converted value parameter\n   526\u2192     */\n   527\u2192    public <T> T getAndRemoveOrResolveReferenceParameter(Map<String, Object> parameters, String key, Class<T> type) {\n   528\u2192        return getAndRemoveOrResolveReferenceParameter(parameters, key, type, null);\n   529\u2192    }\n   530\u2192\n   531\u2192    /**\n   532\u2192     * Gets the parameter and remove it from the parameter map. This method resolves reference parameters in the\n   533\u2192     * registry as well.\n   534\u2192     *\n   535\u2192     * @param  parameters   the parameters\n   536\u2192     * @param  key          the key\n   537\u2192     * @param  type         the requested type to convert the value from the parameter\n   538\u2192     * @param  defaultValue use this default value if the parameter does not contain the key\n   539\u2192     * @return              the converted value parameter\n   540\u2192     */\n   541\u2192    public <T> T getAndRemoveOrResolveReferenceParameter(\n   542\u2192            Map<String, Object> parameters, String key, Class<T> type, T defaultValue) {\n   543\u2192        // the parameter may be the the type already (such as from endpoint-dsl)\n   544\u2192        Object value = parameters.remove(key);\n   545\u2192        if (value instanceof String str) {\n   546\u2192            if (EndpointHelper.isReferenceParameter(str)) {\n   547\u2192                return EndpointHelper.resolveReferenceParameter(getCamelContext(), str, type);\n   548\u2192            }\n   549\u2192        }\n   550\u2192        if (type.isInstance(value)) {\n   551\u2192            // special for string references\n   552\u2192            if (String.class == type) {\n   553\u2192                String str = value.toString();\n   554\u2192                if (EndpointHelper.isReferenceParameter(str)) {\n   555\u2192                    value = EndpointHelper.resolveReferenceParameter(getCamelContext(), str, type);\n   556\u2192                }\n   557\u2192            }\n   558\u2192            return type.cast(value);\n   559\u2192        } else if (value == null) {\n   560\u2192            return defaultValue;\n   561\u2192        } else {\n   562\u2192            // okay so it may be a reference so value should be string\n   563\u2192            String str = getCamelContext().getTypeConverter().tryConvertTo(String.class, value);\n   564\u2192            if (EndpointHelper.isReferenceParameter(str)) {\n   565\u2192                return EndpointHelper.resolveReferenceParameter(getCamelContext(), str, type);\n   566\u2192            } else {\n   567\u2192                return getCamelContext().getTypeConverter().convertTo(type, value);\n   568\u2192            }\n   569\u2192        }\n   570\u2192    }\n   571\u2192\n   572\u2192    /**\n   573\u2192     * Resolves a reference parameter in the registry and removes it from the map.\n   574\u2192     *\n   575\u2192     * @param  <T>                      type of object to lookup in the registry.\n   576\u2192     * @param  parameters               parameter map.\n   577\u2192     * @param  key                      parameter map key.\n   578\u2192     * @param  type                     type of object to lookup in the registry.\n   579\u2192     * @return                          the referenced object or <code>null</code> if the parameter map doesn't contain\n   580\u2192     *                                  the key.\n   581\u2192     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if a non-null reference was not found in registry.\n   582\u2192     */\n   583\u2192    public <T> T resolveAndRemoveReferenceParameter(Map<String, Object> parameters, String key, Class<T> type) {\n   584\u2192        return resolveAndRemoveReferenceParameter(parameters, key, type, null);\n   585\u2192    }\n   586\u2192\n   587\u2192    /**\n   588\u2192     * Resolves a reference parameter in the registry and removes it from the map.\n   589\u2192     *\n   590\u2192     * @param  <T>                      type of object to lookup in the registry.\n   591\u2192     * @param  parameters               parameter map.\n   592\u2192     * @param  key                      parameter map key.\n   593\u2192     * @param  type                     type of object to lookup in the registry.\n   594\u2192     * @param  defaultValue             default value to use if the parameter map doesn't contain the key.\n   595\u2192     * @return                          the referenced object or the default value.\n   596\u2192     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if referenced object was not found in registry.\n   597\u2192     */\n   598\u2192    public <T> T resolveAndRemoveReferenceParameter(Map<String, Object> parameters, String key, Class<T> type, T defaultValue) {\n   599\u2192        // the parameter may be the the type already (such as from endpoint-dsl)\n   600\u2192        Object value = parameters.remove(key);\n   601\u2192        if (value instanceof String str) {\n   602\u2192            if (EndpointHelper.isReferenceParameter(str)) {\n   603\u2192                return EndpointHelper.resolveReferenceParameter(getCamelContext(), str, type);\n   604\u2192            }\n   605\u2192        }\n   606\u2192        if (type.isInstance(value)) {\n   607\u2192            // special for string references\n   608\u2192            if (String.class == type) {\n   609\u2192                String str = value.toString();\n   610\u2192                if (EndpointHelper.isReferenceParameter(str)) {\n   611\u2192                    value = EndpointHelper.resolveReferenceParameter(getCamelContext(), str, type);\n   612\u2192                }\n   613\u2192            }\n   614\u2192            return type.cast(value);\n   615\u2192        } else if (value == null) {\n   616\u2192            return defaultValue;\n   617\u2192        } else {\n   618\u2192            // okay so it may be a reference so value should be string\n   619\u2192            String str = getCamelContext().getTypeConverter().tryConvertTo(String.class, value);\n   620\u2192            return EndpointHelper.resolveReferenceParameter(getCamelContext(), str, type);\n   621\u2192        }\n   622\u2192    }\n   623\u2192\n   624\u2192    /**\n   625\u2192     * Resolves a reference list parameter in the registry and removes it from the map.\n   626\u2192     *\n   627\u2192     * @param  parameters               parameter map.\n   628\u2192     * @param  key                      parameter map key.\n   629\u2192     * @param  elementType              result list element type.\n   630\u2192     * @return                          the list of referenced objects or an empty list if the parameter map doesn't\n   631\u2192     *                                  contain the key.\n   632\u2192     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any of the referenced objects was not found in registry.\n   633\u2192     * @see                             EndpointHelper#resolveReferenceListParameter(CamelContext, String, Class)\n   634\u2192     */\n   635\u2192    public <T> List<T> resolveAndRemoveReferenceListParameter(\n   636\u2192            Map<String, Object> parameters, String key, Class<T> elementType) {\n   637\u2192        return resolveAndRemoveReferenceListParameter(parameters, key, elementType, new ArrayList<>(0));\n   638\u2192    }\n   639\u2192\n   640\u2192    /**\n   641\u2192     * Resolves a reference list parameter in the registry and removes it from the map.\n   642\u2192     *\n   643\u2192     * @param  parameters               parameter map.\n   644\u2192     * @param  key                      parameter map key.\n   645\u2192     * @param  elementType              result list element type.\n   646\u2192     * @param  defaultValue             default value to use if the parameter map doesn't contain the key.\n   647\u2192     * @return                          the list of referenced objects or the default value.\n   648\u2192     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if any of the referenced objects was not found in registry.\n   649\u2192     * @see                             EndpointHelper#resolveReferenceListParameter(CamelContext, String, Class)\n   650\u2192     */\n   651\u2192    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n   652\u2192    public <T> List<T> resolveAndRemoveReferenceListParameter(\n   653\u2192            Map<String, Object> parameters, String key, Class<T> elementType, List<T> defaultValue) {\n   654\u2192        // the value may already be a list such as when using endpoint-dsl\n   655\u2192        Object value = getAndRemoveParameter(parameters, key, Object.class);\n   656\u2192        if (value instanceof List) {\n   657\u2192            return (List<T>) value;\n   658\u2192        }\n   659\u2192        if (value == null) {\n   660\u2192            return defaultValue;\n   661\u2192        } else {\n   662\u2192            // okay so it may be a reference so value should be string\n   663\u2192            String str = getCamelContext().getTypeConverter().tryConvertTo(String.class, value);\n   664\u2192            return EndpointHelper.resolveReferenceListParameter(getCamelContext(), str, elementType);\n   665\u2192        }\n   666\u2192    }\n   667\u2192\n   668\u2192    /**\n   669\u2192     * Returns the reminder of the text if it starts with the prefix.\n   670\u2192     * <p/>\n   671\u2192     * Is useable for string parameters that contains commands.\n   672\u2192     *\n   673\u2192     * @param  prefix the prefix\n   674\u2192     * @param  text   the text\n   675\u2192     * @return        the reminder, or null if no reminder\n   676\u2192     */\n   677\u2192    protected String ifStartsWithReturnRemainder(String prefix, String text) {\n   678\u2192        if (text.startsWith(prefix)) {\n   679\u2192            String remainder = text.substring(prefix.length());\n   680\u2192            if (!remainder.isEmpty()) {\n   681\u2192                return remainder;\n   682\u2192            }\n   683\u2192        }\n   684\u2192        return null;\n   685\u2192    }\n   686\u2192\n   687\u2192    protected void registerExtension(ComponentExtension extension) {\n   688\u2192        extensions.add(() -> extension);\n   689\u2192    }\n   690\u2192\n   691\u2192    protected void registerExtension(Supplier<ComponentExtension> supplier) {\n   692\u2192        extensions.add(Suppliers.memorize(supplier));\n   693\u2192    }\n   694\u2192\n   695\u2192    @Override\n   696\u2192    public Collection<Class<? extends ComponentExtension>> getSupportedExtensions() {\n   697\u2192        return extensions.stream()\n   698\u2192                .map(Supplier::get)\n   699\u2192                .map(ComponentExtension::getClass)\n   700\u2192                .collect(Collectors.toList());\n   701\u2192    }\n   702\u2192\n   703\u2192    @Override\n   704\u2192    public <T extends ComponentExtension> Optional<T> getExtension(Class<T> extensionType) {\n   705\u2192        return extensions.stream()\n   706\u2192                .map(Supplier::get)\n   707\u2192                .filter(extensionType::isInstance)\n   708\u2192                .findFirst()\n   709\u2192                .map(extensionType::cast)\n   710\u2192                .map(e -> Component.trySetComponent(e, this))\n   711\u2192                .map(e -> CamelContextAware.trySetCamelContext(e, getCamelContext()));\n   712\u2192    }\n   713\u2192}\n   714\u2192"
15. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/core/camel-support/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/support/DefaultEndpoint.java"
}
Output

in writing, software\n    12\u2192 * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192package org.apache.camel.support;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import java.util.Map;\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192import org.apache.camel.AsyncProducer;\n    22\u2192import org.apache.camel.CamelContext;\n    23\u2192import org.apache.camel.CamelContextAware;\n    24\u2192import org.apache.camel.Component;\n    25\u2192import org.apache.camel.Consumer;\n    26\u2192import org.apache.camel.Endpoint;\n    27\u2192import org.apache.camel.Exchange;\n    28\u2192import org.apache.camel.ExchangePattern;\n    29\u2192import org.apache.camel.PollingConsumer;\n    30\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.ExceptionHandler;\n    31\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.HasId;\n    32\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.Metadata;\n    33\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.PropertyConfigurer;\n    34\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.PropertyConfigurerAware;\n    35\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.PropertyConfigurerGetter;\n    36\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.UriParam;\n    37\u2192import org.apache.camel.support.service.ServiceSupport;\n    38\u2192import org.apache.camel.util.ObjectHelper;\n    39\u2192import org.apache.camel.util.StringHelper;\n    40\u2192import org.apache.camel.util.URISupport;\n    41\u2192import org.slf4j.Logger;\n    42\u2192import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192/**\n    45\u2192 * Default {@link Endpoint}.\n    46\u2192 */\n    47\u2192public abstract class DefaultEndpoint extends ServiceSupport implements Endpoint, HasId, CamelContextAware {\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(DefaultEndpoint.class);\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192    private final String id = EndpointHelper.createEndpointId();\n    52\u2192    private transient String endpointUriToString;\n    53\u2192    private volatile String endpointUri;\n    54\u2192    private CamelContext camelContext;\n    55\u2192    private Component component;\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192    @Metadata(label = \"advanced\", defaultValue = \"true\",\n    58\u2192              description = \"Whether autowiring is enabled. This is used for automatic autowiring options (the option must be marked as autowired)\"\n    59\u2192                            + \" by looking up in the registry to find if there is a single instance of matching type, which then gets configured on the component.\"\n    60\u2192                            + \" This can be used for automatic configuring JDBC data sources, JMS connection factories, AWS Clients, etc.\"\n    61\u2192                            + \" Important: If a component has the same option defined on both component and endpoint level, then disabling\"\n    62\u2192                            + \" autowiring on endpoint level would not affect that the component will still be autowired, and therefore the endpoint\"\n    63\u2192                            + \" will be configured with option from the component level. In other words turning off autowiring would then require to turn it off on the component level.\")\n    64\u2192    private boolean autowiredEnabled = true;\n    65\u2192    @UriParam(label = \"producer,advanced\",\n    66\u2192              description = \"Whether the producer should be started lazy (on the first message). By starting lazy you can use this to allow CamelContext and routes to startup\"\n    67\u2192                            + \" in situations where a producer may otherwise fail during starting and cause the route to fail being started. By deferring this startup to be lazy then\"\n    68\u2192                            + \" the startup failure can be handled during routing messages via Camel's routing error handlers. Beware that when the first message is processed\"\n    69\u2192                            + \" then creating and starting the producer may take a little time and prolong the total processing time of the processing.\")\n    70\u2192    private boolean lazyStartProducer;\n    71\u2192    @UriParam(label = \"consumer,advanced\",\n    72\u2192              description = \"Allows for bridging the consumer to the Camel routing Error Handler, which mean any exceptions (if possible) occurred while\"\n    73\u2192                            + \" the Camel consumer is trying to pickup incoming messages, or the likes, will now be processed as a message and handled by the routing Error Handler.\"\n    74\u2192                            + \" Important: This is only possible if the 3rd party component allows Camel to be alerted if an exception was thrown. Some components handle this internally only,\"\n    75\u2192                            + \" and therefore bridgeErrorHandler is not possible. In other situations we may improve the Camel component to hook into the 3rd party component\"\n    76\u2192                            + \" and make this possible for future releases.\"\n    77\u2192                            + \" By default the consumer will use the org.apache.camel.spi.ExceptionHandler to deal with exceptions, that will be logged at WARN or ERROR level and ignored.\")\n    78\u2192    private boolean bridgeErrorHandler;\n    79\u2192    @UriParam(label = \"consumer,advanced\", optionalPrefix = \"consumer.\",\n    80\u2192              description = \"To let the consumer use a custom ExceptionHandler.\"\n    81\u2192                            + \" Notice if the option bridgeErrorHandler is enabled then this option is not in use.\"\n    82\u2192                            + \" By default the consumer will deal with exceptions, that will be logged at WARN or ERROR level and ignored.\")\n    83\u2192    private ExceptionHandler exceptionHandler;\n    84\u2192    @UriParam(label = \"consumer,advanced\",\n    85\u2192              description = \"Sets the exchange pattern when the consumer creates an exchange.\")\n    86\u2192    // no default value set on @UriParam as the MEP is sometimes InOnly or InOut depending on the component in use\n    87\u2192    private ExchangePattern exchangePattern = ExchangePattern.InOnly;\n    88\u2192    // pooling consumer options only related to EventDrivenPollingConsumer which are very seldom in use\n    89\u2192    // so lets not expose them in the component docs as it will be included in every component\n    90\u2192    private int pollingConsumerQueueSize = 1000;\n    91\u2192    private boolean pollingConsumerBlockWhenFull = true;\n    92\u2192    private long pollingConsumerBlockTimeout;\n    93\u2192    private boolean pollingConsumerCopy;\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192    /**\n    96\u2192     * Constructs a fully-initialized DefaultEndpoint instance. This is the preferred method of constructing an object\n    97\u2192     * from Java code (as opposed to Spring beans, etc.).\n    98\u2192     *\n    99\u2192     * @param endpointUri the full URI used to create this endpoint\n   100\u2192     * @param component   the component that created this endpoint\n   101\u2192     */\n   102\u2192    protected DefaultEndpoint(String endpointUri, Component component) {\n   103\u2192        this.component = component;\n   104\u2192        this.setEndpointUri(endpointUri);\n   105\u2192        if (component != null) {\n   106\u2192            this.camelContext = component.getCamelContext();\n   107\u2192        }\n   108\u2192    }\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192    /**\n   111\u2192     * Constructs a partially-initialized DefaultEndpoint instance. Useful when creating endpoints manually (e.g., as\n   112\u2192     * beans in Spring).\n   113\u2192     * <p/>\n   114\u2192     * Please note that the endpoint URI must be set through properties (or overriding {@link #createEndpointUri()} if\n   115\u2192     * one uses this constructor.\n   116\u2192     * <p/>\n   117\u2192     * <b>Note:</b> It is preferred to create endpoints using the associated component.\n   118\u2192     */\n   119\u2192    protected DefaultEndpoint() {\n   120\u2192    }\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192    @Override\n   123\u2192    public int hashCode() {\n   124\u2192        return getEndpointUri().hashCode() * 37 + 1;\n   125\u2192    }\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192    @Override\n   128\u2192    public boolean equals(Object object) {\n   129\u2192        if (object instanceof DefaultEndpoint that) {\n   130\u2192            // must also match the same CamelContext in case we compare endpoints from different contexts\n   131\u2192            String thisContextName = this.getCamelContext() != null ? this.getCamelContext().getName() : null;\n   132\u2192            String thatContextName = that.getCamelContext() != null ? that.getCamelContext().getName() : null;\n   133\u2192            return ObjectHelper.equal(this.getEndpointUri(), that.getEndpointUri())\n   134\u2192                    && ObjectHelper.equal(thisContextName, thatContextName);\n   135\u2192        }\n   136\u2192        return false;\n   137\u2192    }\n   138\u2192\n   139\u2192    @Override\n   140\u2192    public String toString() {\n   141\u2192        if (endpointUriToString == null) {\n   142\u2192            String value = null;\n   143\u2192            try {\n   144\u2192                value = getEndpointUri();\n   145\u2192            } catch (RuntimeException e) {\n   146\u2192                // ignore any exception and use null for building the string value\n   147\u2192            }\n   148\u2192            // ensure to sanitize uri so we do not show sensitive information such as passwords\n   149\u2192            endpointUriToString = URISupport.sanitizeUri(value);\n   150\u2192        }\n   151\u2192        return endpointUriToString;\n   152\u2192    }\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192    /**\n   155\u2192     * Returns a unique String ID which can be used for aliasing without having to use the whole URI which is not unique\n   156\u2192     */\n   157\u2192    @Override\n   158\u2192    public String getId() {\n   159\u2192        return id;\n   160\u2192    }\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192    @Override\n   163\u2192    public String getEndpointUri() {\n   164\u2192        if (endpointUri == null) {\n   165\u2192            endpointUri = createEndpointUri();\n   166\u2192            if (endpointUri == null) {\n   167\u2192                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\n   168\u2192                        \"endpointUri is not specified and \" + getClass().getName()\n   169\u2192                                                   + \" does not implement createEndpointUri() to create a default value\");\n   170\u2192            }\n   171\u2192        }\n   172\u2192        return endpointUri;\n   173\u2192    }\n   174\u2192\n   175\u2192    @Override\n   176\u2192    public String getEndpointKey() {\n   177\u2192        if (isLenientProperties()) {\n   178\u2192            // only use the endpoint uri without parameters as the properties are lenient\n   179\u2192            String uri = getEndpointUri();\n   180\u2192            return StringHelper.before(uri, \"?\", uri);\n   181\u2192        } else {\n   182\u2192            // use the full endpoint uri\n   183\u2192            return getEndpointUri();\n   184\u2192        }\n   185\u2192    }\n   186\u2192\n   187\u2192    @Override\n   188\u2192    public CamelContext getCamelContext() {\n   189\u2192        return camelContext;\n   190\u2192    }\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192    @Override\n   193\u2192    public AsyncProducer createAsyncProducer() throws Exception {\n   194\u2192        if (isLazyStartProducer()) {\n   195\u2192            return new LazyStartProducer(this);\n   196\u2192        } else {\n   197\u2192            return AsyncProcessorConverterHelper.convert(createProducer());\n   198\u2192        }\n   199\u2192    }\n   200\u2192\n   201\u2192    /**\n   202\u2192     * Returns the component that created this endpoint, or <tt>null</tt> if none set.\n   203\u2192     */\n   204\u2192    public Component getComponent() {\n   205\u2192        return component;\n   206\u2192    }\n   207\u2192\n   208\u2192    public void setComponent(Component component) {\n   209\u2192        this.component = component;\n   210\u2192    }\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192    @Override\n   213\u2192    public void setCamelContext(CamelContext camelContext) {\n   214\u2192        this.camelContext = camelContext;\n   215\u2192    }\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192    @Override\n   218\u2192    public PollingConsumer createPollingConsumer() throws Exception {\n   219\u2192        // should not call configurePollingConsumer when its EventDrivenPollingConsumer\n   220\u2192        if (LOG.isDebugEnabled()) {\n   221\u2192            LOG.debug(\"Creating EventDrivenPollingConsumer with queueSize: {} blockWhenFull: {} blockTimeout: {} copy: {}\",\n   222\u2192                    getPollingConsumerQueueSize(), isPollingConsumerBlockWhenFull(), getPollingConsumerBlockTimeout(),\n   223\u2192                    isPollingConsumerCopy());\n   224\u2192        }\n   225\u2192        EventDrivenPollingConsumer consumer = new EventDrivenPollingConsumer(this, getPollingConsumerQueueSize());\n   226\u2192        consumer.setBlockWhenFull(isPollingConsumerBlockWhenFull());\n   227\u2192        consumer.setBlockTimeout(getPollingConsumerBlockTimeout());\n   228\u2192        consumer.setCopy(isPollingConsumerCopy());\n   229\u2192        return consumer;\n   230\u2192    }\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192    @Override\n   233\u2192    public Exchange createExchange() {\n   234\u2192        return createExchange(exchangePattern);\n   235\u2192    }\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192    @Override\n   238\u2192    public Exchange createExchange(ExchangePattern pattern) {\n   239\u2192        Exchange answer = DefaultExchange.newFromEndpoint(this, pattern);\n   240\u2192        configureExchange(answer);\n   241\u2192        return answer;\n   242\u2192    }\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192    @Override\n   245\u2192    public void configureExchange(Exchange exchange) {\n   246\u2192        // noop\n   247\u2192    }\n   248\u2192\n   249\u2192    @Override\n   250\u2192    public ExchangePattern getExchangePattern() {\n   251\u2192        return exchangePattern;\n   252\u2192    }\n   253\u2192\n   254\u2192    /**\n   255\u2192     * Sets the default exchange pattern when creating an exchange.\n   256\u2192     */\n   257\u2192    public void setExchangePattern(ExchangePattern exchangePattern) {\n   258\u2192        this.exchangePattern = exchangePattern;\n   259\u2192    }\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192    public boolean isAutowiredEnabled() {\n   262\u2192        return autowiredEnabled;\n   263\u2192    }\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192    /**\n   266\u2192     * Whether autowiring is enabled. This is used for automatic autowiring options (the option must be marked as\n   267\u2192     * autowired) by looking up in the registry to find if there is a single instance of matching type, which then gets\n   268\u2192     * configured on the component. This can be used for automatic configuring JDBC data sources, JMS connection\n   269\u2192     * factories, AWS Clients, etc. Important: If a component has the same option defined on both component and endpoint\n   270\u2192     * level, then disabling autowiring on endpoint level would not affect that the component will still be autowired,\n   271\u2192     * and therefore the endpoint will be configured with option from the component level. In other words turning off\n   272\u2192     * autowiring would then require to turn it off on the component level.\n   273\u2192     */\n   274\u2192    public void setAutowiredEnabled(boolean autowiredEnabled) {\n   275\u2192        this.autowiredEnabled = autowiredEnabled;\n   276\u2192    }\n   277\u2192\n   278\u2192    public boolean isLazyStartProducer() {\n   279\u2192        return lazyStartProducer;\n   280\u2192    }\n   281\u2192\n   282\u2192    /**\n   283\u2192     * Whether the producer should be started lazy (on the first message). By starting lazy you can use this to allow\n   284\u2192     * CamelContext and routes to startup in situations where a producer may otherwise fail during starting and cause\n   285\u2192     * the route to fail being started. By deferring this startup to be lazy then the startup failure can be handled\n   286\u2192     * during routing messages via Camel's routing error handlers. Beware that when the first message is processed then\n   287\u2192     * creating and starting the producer may take a little time and prolong the total processing time of the\n   288\u2192     * processing.\n   289\u2192     */\n   290\u2192    public void setLazyStartProducer(boolean lazyStartProducer) {\n   291\u2192        this.lazyStartProducer = lazyStartProducer;\n   292\u2192    }\n   293\u2192\n   294\u2192    public boolean isBridgeErrorHandler() {\n   295\u2192        return bridgeErrorHandler;\n   296\u2192    }\n   297\u2192\n   298\u2192    /**\n   299\u2192     * Allows for bridging the consumer to the Camel routing Error Handler, which mean any exceptions (if possible)\n   300\u2192     * occurred while the Camel consumer is trying to pickup incoming messages, or the likes, will now be processed as a\n   301\u2192     * message and handled by the routing Error Handler. Important: This is only possible if the 3rd party component\n   302\u2192     * allows Camel to be alerted if an exception was thrown. Some components handle this internally only, and therefore\n   303\u2192     * bridgeErrorHandler is not possible. In other situations we may improve the Camel component to hook into the 3rd\n   304\u2192     * party component and make this possible for future releases.\n   305\u2192     * <p/>\n   306\u2192     * By default the consumer will use the org.apache.camel.spi.ExceptionHandler to deal with exceptions, that will be\n   307\u2192     * logged at WARN/ERROR level and ignored.\n   308\u2192     */\n   309\u2192    public void setBridgeErrorHandler(boolean bridgeErrorHandler) {\n   310\u2192        this.bridgeErrorHandler = bridgeErrorHandler;\n   311\u2192    }\n   312\u2192\n   313\u2192    public ExceptionHandler getExceptionHandler() {\n   314\u2192        return exceptionHandler;\n   315\u2192    }\n   316\u2192\n   317\u2192    /**\n   318\u2192     * To let the consumer use a custom ExceptionHandler. Notice if the option bridgeErrorHandler is enabled then this\n   319\u2192     * options is not in use. By default the consumer will deal with exceptions, that will be logged at WARN/ERROR level\n   320\u2192     * and ignored.\n   321\u2192     */\n   322\u2192    public void setExceptionHandler(ExceptionHandler exceptionHandler) {\n   323\u2192        this.exceptionHandler = exceptionHandler;\n   324\u2192    }\n   325\u2192\n   326\u2192    public int getPollingConsumerQueueSize() {\n   327\u2192        return pollingConsumerQueueSize;\n   328\u2192    }\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192    /**\n   331\u2192     * Sets the {@link org.apache.camel.PollingConsumer} queue size, when {@link EventDrivenPollingConsumer} is being\n   332\u2192     * used. Notice some Camel components may have their own implementation of {@link org.apache.camel.PollingConsumer}\n   333\u2192     * and therefore not using the default {@link EventDrivenPollingConsumer} implementation.\n   334\u2192     * <p/>\n   335\u2192     * The default value is <tt>1000</tt>\n   336\u2192     */\n   337\u2192    public void setPollingConsumerQueueSize(int pollingConsumerQueueSize) {\n   338\u2192        this.pollingConsumerQueueSize = pollingConsumerQueueSize;\n   339\u2192    }\n   340\u2192\n   341\u2192    public boolean isPollingConsumerBlockWhenFull() {\n   342\u2192        return pollingConsumerBlockWhenFull;\n   343\u2192    }\n   344\u2192\n   345\u2192    /**\n   346\u2192     * Set whether to block when adding to the internal queue off when {@link EventDrivenPollingConsumer} is being used.\n   347\u2192     * Notice some Camel components may have their own implementation of {@link org.apache.camel.PollingConsumer} and\n   348\u2192     * therefore not using the default {@link EventDrivenPollingConsumer} implementation.\n   349\u2192     * <p/>\n   350\u2192     * Setting this option to <tt>false</tt>, will result in an {@link java.lang.IllegalStateException} being thrown\n   351\u2192     * when trying to add to the queue, and it is full.\n   352\u2192     * <p/>\n   353\u2192     * The default value is <tt>true</tt> which will block the producer queue until the queue has space.\n   354\u2192     */\n   355\u2192    public void setPollingConsumerBlockWhenFull(boolean pollingConsumerBlockWhenFull) {\n   356\u2192        this.pollingConsumerBlockWhenFull = pollingConsumerBlockWhenFull;\n   357\u2192    }\n   358\u2192\n   359\u2192    public long getPollingConsumerBlockTimeout() {\n   360\u2192        return pollingConsumerBlockTimeout;\n   361\u2192    }\n   362\u2192\n   363\u2192    /**\n   364\u2192     * Sets the timeout in millis to use when adding to the internal queue off when {@link EventDrivenPollingConsumer}\n   365\u2192     * is being used.\n   366\u2192     *\n   367\u2192     * @see #setPollingConsumerBlockWhenFull(boolean)\n   368\u2192     */\n   369\u2192    public void setPollingConsumerBlockTimeout(long pollingConsumerBlockTimeout) {\n   370\u2192        this.pollingConsumerBlockTimeout = pollingConsumerBlockTimeout;\n   371\u2192    }\n   372\u2192\n   373\u2192    public boolean isPollingConsumerCopy() {\n   374\u2192        return pollingConsumerCopy;\n   375\u2192    }\n   376\u2192\n   377\u2192    /**\n   378\u2192     * Sets whether to copy the exchange when adding to the internal queue off when {@link EventDrivenPollingConsumer}\n   379\u2192     * is being used.\n   380\u2192     *\n   381\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> When copy is enabled then the unit of work is handed over from the current exchange to the\n   382\u2192     * copied exchange instance. And therefore its the responsible of the {@link PollingConsumer} to done the unit of\n   383\u2192     * work on the received exchanges. When the polled exchange is no longer needed then MUST call\n   384\u2192     * {@link org.apache.camel.spi.UnitOfWork#done(Exchange)}.\n   385\u2192     *\n   386\u2192     * Default is false to not copy.\n   387\u2192     */\n   388\u2192    public void setPollingConsumerCopy(boolean pollingConsumerCopy) {\n   389\u2192        this.pollingConsumerCopy = pollingConsumerCopy;\n   390\u2192    }\n   391\u2192\n   392\u2192    @Override\n   393\u2192    public void configureProperties(Map<String, Object> options) {\n   394\u2192        setProperties(this, options);\n   395\u2192    }\n   396\u2192\n   397\u2192    /**\n   398\u2192     * Sets the bean properties on the given bean.\n   399\u2192     * <p/>\n   400\u2192     * This is the same logical implementation as {@link DefaultComponent#setProperties(Object, java.util.Map)}\n   401\u2192     *\n   402\u2192     * @param bean       the bean\n   403\u2192     * @param parameters properties to set\n   404\u2192     */\n   405\u2192    public void setProperties(Object bean, Map<String, Object> parameters) {\n   406\u2192        if (parameters == null || parameters.isEmpty()) {\n   407\u2192            return;\n   408\u2192        }\n   409\u2192\n   410\u2192        PropertyConfigurer configurer = null;\n   411\u2192        if (bean instanceof Component) {\n   412\u2192            configurer = getComponent().getComponentPropertyConfigurer();\n   413\u2192        } else if (bean instanceof Endpoint) {\n   414\u2192            configurer = getComponent().getEndpointPropertyConfigurer();\n   415\u2192        } else if (bean instanceof PropertyConfigurerAware propertyConfigurerAware) {\n   416\u2192            configurer = propertyConfigurerAware.getPropertyConfigurer(bean);\n   417\u2192        }\n   418\u2192        // use configurer and ignore case as end users may type an option name with mixed case\n   419\u2192        PropertyBindingSupport.build().withConfigurer(configurer).withIgnoreCase(true)\n   420\u2192                // if the endpoint is lenient then use optional\n   421\u2192                .withOptional(isLenientProperties())\n   422\u2192                .bind(camelContext, bean, parameters);\n   423\u2192    }\n   424\u2192\n   425\u2192    /**\n   426\u2192     * A factory method to lazily create the endpointUri if none is specified\n   427\u2192     */\n   428\u2192    protected String createEndpointUri() {\n   429\u2192        return null;\n   430\u2192    }\n   431\u2192\n   432\u2192    /**\n   433\u2192     * Sets the endpointUri if it has not been specified yet via some kind of dependency injection mechanism. This\n   434\u2192     * allows dependency injection frameworks such as Spring to set the default endpoint URI in cases where it has not\n   435\u2192     * been explicitly configured using the name/context in which an Endpoint is created.\n   436\u2192     */\n   437\u2192    public void setEndpointUriIfNotSpecified(String value) {\n   438\u2192        if (endpointUri == null) {\n   439\u2192            setEndpointUri(value);\n   440\u2192        }\n   441\u2192    }\n   442\u2192\n   443\u2192    /**\n   444\u2192     * Sets the URI that created this endpoint.\n   445\u2192     */\n   446\u2192    protected void setEndpointUri(String endpointUri) {\n   447\u2192        this.endpointUri = endpointUri;\n   448\u2192    }\n   449\u2192\n   450\u2192    /**\n   451\u2192     * An endpoint should favour to be singleton by default, only in some rare special cases can an endpoint be\n   452\u2192     * non-singleton. This implementation is singleton and this method returns true.\n   453\u2192     */\n   454\u2192    @Override\n   455\u2192    public boolean isSingleton() {\n   456\u2192        return true;\n   457\u2192    }\n   458\u2192\n   459\u2192    @Override\n   460\u2192    public boolean isLenientProperties() {\n   461\u2192        // default should be false for most components\n   462\u2192        return false;\n   463\u2192    }\n   464\u2192\n   465\u2192    protected void configureConsumer(Consumer consumer) throws Exception {\n   466\u2192        // inject CamelContext\n   467\u2192        CamelContextAware.trySetCamelContext(consumer, camelContext);\n   468\u2192\n   469\u2192        if (bridgeErrorHandler) {\n   470\u2192            if (consumer instanceof DefaultConsumer defaultConsumer) {\n   471\u2192                defaultConsumer.setExceptionHandler(new BridgeExceptionHandlerToErrorHandler(defaultConsumer));\n   472\u2192            } else {\n   473\u2192                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\n   474\u2192                        \"Option bridgeErrorHandler is only supported by endpoints,\"\n   475\u2192                                                   + \" having their consumer extend DefaultConsumer. The consumer is a \"\n   476\u2192                                                   + consumer.getClass().getName() + \" class.\");\n   477\u2192            }\n   478\u2192        }\n   479\u2192        if (exceptionHandler != null) {\n   480\u2192            if (consumer instanceof DefaultConsumer defaultConsumer) {\n   481\u2192                defaultConsumer.setExceptionHandler(exceptionHandler);\n   482\u2192            }\n   483\u2192        }\n   484\u2192    }\n   485\u2192\n   486\u2192    protected void configurePollingConsumer(PollingConsumer consumer) throws Exception {\n   487\u2192        configureConsumer(consumer);\n   488\u2192    }\n   489\u2192\n   490\u2192    @Override\n   491\u2192    protected void doInit() throws Exception {\n   492\u2192        ObjectHelper.notNull(getCamelContext(), \"camelContext\");\n   493\u2192\n   494\u2192        if (autowiredEnabled && getComponent() != null && getComponent().isAutowiredEnabled()) {\n   495\u2192            PropertyConfigurer configurer = getComponent().getEndpointPropertyConfigurer();\n   496\u2192            if (configurer instanceof PropertyConfigurerGetter getter) {\n   497\u2192                String[] names = getter.getAutowiredNames();\n   498\u2192                if (names != null) {\n   499\u2192                    for (String name : names) {\n   500\u2192                        // is there already a configured value?\n   501\u2192                        Object value = getter.getOptionValue(this, name, true);\n   502\u2192                        if (value == null) {\n   503\u2192                            Class<?> type = getter.getOptionType(name, true);\n   504\u2192                            if (type != null) {\n   505\u2192                                value = camelContext.getRegistry().findSingleByType(type);\n   506\u2192                            }\n   507\u2192                            if (value != null) {\n   508\u2192                                boolean hit = configurer.configure(camelContext, this, name, value, true);\n   509\u2192                                if (hit) {\n   510\u2192                                    if (LOG.isDebugEnabled()) {\n   511\u2192                                        LOG.debug(\n   512\u2192                                                \"Autowired property: {} on endpoint: {} as exactly one instance of type: {} ({}) found in the registry\",\n   513\u2192                                                name, this, type.getName(), value.getClass().getName());\n   514\u2192                                    }\n   515\u2192                                }\n   516\u2192                            }\n   517\u2192                        }\n   518\u2192                    }\n   519\u2192                }\n   520\u2192            }\n   521\u2192        }\n   522\u2192    }\n   523\u2192}\n   524\u2192"

18\u2192\n    19\u2192import org.apache.camel.AsyncCallback;\n    20\u2192import org.apache.camel.AsyncProcessor;\n    21\u2192import org.apache.camel.Consumer;\n    22\u2192import org.apache.camel.Endpoint;\n    23\u2192import org.apache.camel.Exchange;\n    24\u2192import org.apache.camel.PooledExchange;\n    25\u2192import org.apache.camel.Processor;\n    26\u2192import
