30\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.ExceptionHandler;\n    31\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.ExchangeFactory;\n    32\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.HostedService;\n    33\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.RouteIdAware;\n    34\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.UnitOfWork;\n    35\u2192import org.apache.camel.support.service.ServiceHelper;\n    36\u2192import org.apache.camel.support.service.ServiceSupport;\n    37\u2192import org.apache.camel.util.URISupport;\n    38\u2192import org.slf4j.Logger;\n    39\u2192import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192/**\n    42\u2192 * A default consumer useful for implementation inheritance.\n    43\u2192 */\n    44\u2192public class DefaultConsumer extends ServiceSupport\n    45\u2192        implements Consumer, RouteAware, RouteIdAware, HealthCheckAware, HostedService {\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(DefaultConsumer.class);\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192    private transient String consumerToString;\n    50\u2192    private final Endpoint endpoint;\n    51\u2192    private final Processor processor;\n    52\u2192    private final AsyncProcessor asyncProcessor;\n    53\u2192    private final ExchangeFactory exchangeFactory;\n    54\u2192    private HealthCheck healthCheck;\n    55\u2192    private ExceptionHandler exceptionHandler;\n    56\u2192    private Route route;\n    57\u2192    private String routeId;\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192    public DefaultConsumer(Endpoint endpoint, Processor processor) {\n    60\u2192        this.endpoint = endpoint;\n    61\u2192        this.processor = processor;\n    62\u2192        this.asyncProcessor = AsyncProcessorConverterHelper.convert(processor);\n    63\u2192        this.exceptionHandler = new LoggingExceptionHandler(endpoint.getCamelContext(), getClass());\n    64\u2192        // create a per consumer exchange factory\n    65\u2192        this.exchangeFactory = endpoint.getCamelContext().getCamelContextExtension()\n    66\u2192                .getExchangeFactory().newExchangeFactory(this);\n    67\u2192    }\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192    @Override\n    70\u2192    public boolean isHostedService() {\n    71\u2192        return false;\n    72\u2192    }\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192    @Override\n    75\u2192    public String toString() {\n    76\u2192        if (consumerToString == null) {\n    77\u2192            consumerToString = \"Consumer[\" + URISupport.sanitizeUri(endpoint.getEndpointUri()) + \"]\";\n    78\u2192        }\n    79\u2192        return consumerToString;\n    80\u2192    }\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192    @Override\n    83\u2192    public Route getRoute() {\n    84\u2192        return route;\n    85\u2192    }\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192    @Override\n    88\u2192    public void setRoute(Route route) {\n    89\u2192        this.route = route;\n    90\u2192    }\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192    @Override\n    93\u2192    public String getRouteId() {\n    94\u2192        return routeId;\n    95\u2192    }\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192    @Override\n    98\u2192    public void setRouteId(String routeId) {\n    99\u2192        this.routeId = routeId;\n   100\u2192    }\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192    /**\n   103\u2192     * If the consumer needs to defer done the {@link org.apache.camel.spi.UnitOfWork} on the processed {@link Exchange}\n   104\u2192     * then this method should be use to create and start the {@link UnitOfWork} on the exchange.\n   105\u2192     *\n   106\u2192     * @param  exchange  the exchange\n   107\u2192     * @return           the created and started unit of work\n   108\u2192     * @throws Exception is thrown if error starting the unit of work\n   109\u2192     * @see              #doneUoW(org.apache.camel.Exchange)\n   110\u2192     */\n   111\u2192    public UnitOfWork createUoW(Exchange exchange) throws Exception {\n   112\u2192        // if the exchange doesn't have from route id set, then set it if it originated\n   113\u2192        // from this unit of work\n   114\u2192        if (route != null && exchange.getFromRouteId() == null) {\n   115\u2192            exchange.getExchangeExtension().setFromRouteId(route.getId());\n   116\u2192        }\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192        // create uow (however for pooled exchanges then the uow is pre-created)\n   119\u2192        UnitOfWork uow = exchange.getUnitOfWork();\n   120\u2192        if (uow == null) {\n   121\u2192            uow = PluginHelper.getUnitOfWorkFactory(endpoint.getCamelContext()).createUnitOfWork(exchange);\n   122\u2192            exchange.getExchangeExtension().setUnitOfWork(uow);\n   123\u2192        }\n   124\u2192        return uow;\n   125\u2192    }\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192    /**\n   128\u2192     * If the consumer needs to defer done the {@link org.apache.camel.spi.UnitOfWork} on the processed {@link Exchange}\n   129\u2192     * then this method should be executed when the consumer is finished processing the message.\n   130\u2192     *\n   131\u2192     * @param exchange the exchange\n   132\u2192     * @see            #createUoW(org.apache.camel.Exchange)\n   133\u2192     */\n   134\u2192    public void doneUoW(Exchange exchange) {\n   135\u2192        UnitOfWorkHelper.doneUow(exchange.getUnitOfWork(), exchange);\n   136\u2192    }\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192    @Override\n   139\u2192    public Exchange createExchange(boolean autoRelease) {\n   140\u2192        Exchange answer = exchangeFactory.create(getEndpoint(), autoRelease);\n   141\u2192        endpoint.configureExchange(answer);\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192        answer.getExchangeExtension().setFromRouteId(routeId);\n   144\u2192        return answer;\n   145\u2192    }\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192    @Override\n   148\u2192    public void releaseExchange(Exchange exchange, boolean autoRelease) {\n   149\u2192        if (exchange != null) {\n   150\u2192            if (!autoRelease && exchange instanceof PooledExchange pooledExchange) {\n   151\u2192                // if not auto release we must manually force done\n   152\u2192                pooledExchange.done();\n   153\u2192            }\n   154\u2192            exchangeFactory.release(exchange);\n   155\u2192        }\n   156\u2192    }\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192    @Override\n   159\u2192    public AsyncCallback defaultConsumerCallback(Exchange exchange, boolean autoRelease) {\n   160\u2192        boolean pooled = exchangeFactory.isPooled();\n   161\u2192        if (pooled) {\n   162\u2192            AsyncCallback answer = exchange.getExchangeExtension().getDefaultConsumerCallback();\n   163\u2192            if (answer == null) {\n   164\u2192                answer = new DefaultConsumerCallback(this, exchange, autoRelease);\n   165\u2192                exchange.getExchangeExtension().setDefaultConsumerCallback(answer);\n   166\u2192            }\n   167\u2192            return answer;\n   168\u2192        } else {\n   169\u2192            return new DefaultConsumerCallback(this, exchange, autoRelease);\n   170\u2192        }\n   171\u2192    }\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192    @Override\n   174\u2192    public Endpoint getEndpoint() {\n   175\u2192        return endpoint;\n   176\u2192    }\n   177\u2192\n   178\u2192    @Override\n   179\u2192    public Processor getProcessor() {\n   180\u2192        return processor;\n   181\u2192    }\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192    /**\n   184\u2192     * Provides an {@link org.apache.camel.AsyncProcessor} interface to the configured processor on the consumer. If the\n   185\u2192     * processor does not implement the interface, it will be adapted so that it does.\n   186\u2192     */\n   187\u2192    public AsyncProcessor getAsyncProcessor() {\n   188\u2192        return asyncProcessor;\n   189\u2192    }\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192    public ExceptionHandler getExceptionHandler() {\n   192\u2192        return exceptionHandler;\n   193\u2192    }\n   194\u2192\n   195\u2192    public void setExceptionHandler(ExceptionHandler exceptionHandler) {\n   196\u2192        this.exceptionHandler = exceptionHandler;\n   197\u2192    }\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192    @Override\n   200\u2192    public void setHealthCheck(HealthCheck healthCheck) {\n   201\u2192        this.healthCheck = healthCheck;\n   202\u2192    }\n   203\u2192\n   204\u2192    @Override\n   205\u2192    public HealthCheck getHealthCheck() {\n   206\u2192        return healthCheck;\n   207\u2192    }\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192    @Override\n   210\u2192    protected void doBuild() throws Exception {\n   211\u2192        LOG.debug(\"Build consumer: {}\", this);\n   212\u2192        ServiceHelper.buildService(exchangeFactory, processor);\n   213\u2192\n   214\u2192        // force creating and load the class during build time so the JVM does not\n   215\u2192        // load the class on first exchange to be created\n   216\u2192        Object dummy = new DefaultConsumerCallback(this, null, false);\n   217\u2192        LOG.trace(\"Warming up DefaultConsumer loaded class: {}\", dummy.getClass().getName());\n   218\u2192    }\n   219\u2192\n   220\u2192    @Override\n   221\u2192    protected void doInit() throws Exception {\n   222\u2192        LOG.debug(\"Init consumer: {}\", this);\n   223\u2192        ServiceHelper.initService(exchangeFactory, processor);\n   224\u2192    }\n   225\u2192\n   226\u2192    @Override\n   227\u2192    protected void doStart() throws Exception {\n   228\u2192        LOG.debug(\"Starting consumer: {}\", this);\n   229\u2192        exchangeFactory.setRouteId(routeId);\n   230\u2192        ServiceHelper.startService(exchangeFactory, processor);\n   231\u2192    }\n   232\u2192\n   233\u2192    @Override\n   234\u2192    protected void doStop() throws Exception {\n   235\u2192        LOG.debug(\"Stopping consumer: {}\", this);\n   236\u2192        ServiceHelper.stopService(exchangeFactory, processor);\n   237\u2192    }\n   238\u2192\n   239\u2192    @Override\n   240\u2192    protected void doShutdown() throws Exception {\n   241\u2192        LOG.debug(\"Shutting down consumer: {}\", this);\n   242\u2192        ServiceHelper.stopAndShutdownServices(exchangeFactory, processor);\n   243\u2192    }\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192    /**\n   246\u2192     * Handles the given exception using the {@link #getExceptionHandler()}\n   247\u2192     *\n   248\u2192     * @param t the exception to handle\n   249\u2192     */\n   250\u2192    protected void handleException(Throwable t) {\n   251\u2192        Throwable newt = (t == null) ? new IllegalArgumentException(\"Handling [null] exception\") : t;\n   252\u2192        getExceptionHandler().handleException(newt);\n   253\u2192    }\n   254\u2192\n   255\u2192    /**\n   256\u2192     * Handles the given exception using the {@link #getExceptionHandler()}\n   257\u2192     *\n   258\u2192     * @param message additional message about the exception\n   259\u2192     * @param t       the exception to handle\n   260\u2192     */\n   261\u2192    protected void handleException(String message, Throwable t) {\n   262\u2192        Throwable newt = (t == null) ? new IllegalArgumentException(\"Handling [null] exception\") : t;\n   263\u2192        getExceptionHandler().handleException(message, newt);\n   264\u2192    }\n   265\u2192\n   266\u2192    /**\n   267\u2192     * Handles the given exception using the {@link #getExceptionHandler()}\n   268\u2192     *\n   269\u2192     * @param message  additional message about the exception\n   270\u2192     * @param exchange exchange which cause the exception\n   271\u2192     * @param t        the exception to handle\n   272\u2192     */\n   273\u2192    protected void handleException(String message, Exchange exchange, Throwable t) {\n   274\u2192        Throwable newt = (t == null) ? new IllegalArgumentException(\"Handling [null] exception\") : t;\n   275\u2192        getExceptionHandler().handleException(message, exchange, newt);\n   276\u2192    }\n   277\u2192\n   278\u2192    private static final class DefaultConsumerCallback implements AsyncCallback {\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192        private final DefaultConsumer consumer;\n   281\u2192        private final Exchange exchange;\n   282\u2192        private final boolean autoRelease;\n   283\u2192\n   284\u2192        public DefaultConsumerCallback(DefaultConsumer consumer, Exchange exchange, boolean autoRelease) {\n   285\u2192            this.consumer = consumer;\n   286\u2192            this.exchange = exchange;\n   287\u2192            this.autoRelease = autoRelease;\n   288\u2192        }\n   289\u2192\n   290\u2192        @Override\n   291\u2192        public void done(boolean doneSync) {\n   292\u2192            try {\n   293\u2192                // handle any thrown exception\n   294\u2192                if (exchange.getException() != null) {\n   295\u2192                    consumer.getExceptionHandler().handleException(\"Error processing exchange\", exchange,\n   296\u2192                            exchange.getException());\n   297\u2192                }\n   298\u2192            } finally {\n   299\u2192                if (!autoRelease) {\n   300\u2192                    // must release if not auto released\n   301\u2192                    consumer.releaseExchange(exchange, autoRelease);\n   302\u2192                }\n   303\u2192            }\n   304\u2192        }\n   305\u2192\n   306\u2192        @Override\n   307\u2192        public String toString() {\n   308\u2192            return \"DefaultConsumerCallback\";\n   309\u2192        }\n   310\u2192    }\n   311\u2192\n   312\u2192}\n   313\u2192"

14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192package org.apache.camel.support;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import org.apache.camel.Endpoint;\n    20\u2192import org.apache.camel.Exchange;\n    21\u2192import org.apache.camel.Producer;\n    22\u2192import org.apache.camel.support.service.ServiceSupport;\n    23\u2192import org.apache.camel.util.URISupport;\n    24\u2192import org.slf4j.Logger;\n    25\u2192import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192/**\n    28\u2192 * A default implementation of {@link Producer} for implementation inheritance.\n    29\u2192 */\n    30\u2192public abstract class DefaultProducer extends ServiceSupport implements Producer {\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(DefaultProducer.class);\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192    private transient String producerToString;\n    35\u2192    private final Endpoint endpoint;\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192    public DefaultProducer(Endpoint endpoint) {\n    38\u2192        this.endpoint = endpoint;\n    39\u2192    }\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192    @Override\n    42\u2192    public String toString() {\n    43\u2192        if (producerToString == null) {\n    44\u2192            producerToString = \"Producer[\" + URISupport.sanitizeUri(endpoint.getEndpointUri()) + \"]\";\n    45\u2192        }\n    46\u2192        return producerToString;\n    47\u2192    }\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192    @Override\n    50\u2192    public Endpoint getEndpoint() {\n    51\u2192        return endpoint;\n    52\u2192    }\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192    public Exchange createExchange() {\n    55\u2192        return endpoint.createExchange();\n    56\u2192    }\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192    /**\n    59\u2192     * This implementation will delegate to the endpoint {@link org.apache.camel.Endpoint#isSingleton()}\n    60\u2192     */\n    61\u2192    @Override\n    62\u2192    public boolean isSingleton() {\n    63\u2192        return endpoint.isSingleton();\n    64\u2192    }\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192    @Override\n    67\u2192    protected void doStart() throws Exception {\n    68\u2192        // log at debug level for singletons, for prototype scoped log at trace level to not spam logs\n    69\u2192        if (isSingleton()) {\n    70\u2192            LOG.debug(\"Starting producer: {}\", this);\n    71\u2192        } else {\n    72\u2192            LOG.trace(\"Starting producer: {}\", this);\n    73\u2192        }\n    74\u2192    }\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192    @Override\n    77\u2192    protected void doStop() throws Exception {\n    78\u2192        // log at debug level for singletons, for prototype scoped log at trace level to not spam logs\n    79\u2192        if (isSingleton()) {\n    80\u2192            LOG.debug(\"Stopping producer: {}\", this);\n    81\u2192        } else {\n    82\u2192            LOG.trace(\"Stopping producer: {}\", this);\n    83\u2192        }\n    84\u2192    }\n    85\u2192}\n    86\u2192"

14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192package org.apache.camel.support;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture;\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192import org.apache.camel.AsyncProducer;\n    22\u2192import org.apache.camel.Endpoint;\n    23\u2192import org.apache.camel.Exchange;\n    24\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.AsyncProcessorAwaitManager;\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192/**\n    27\u2192 * A default implementation of {@link org.apache.camel.Producer} for implementation inheritance, which can process\n    28\u2192 * {@link Exchange}s asynchronously.\n    29\u2192 */\n    30\u2192public abstract class DefaultAsyncProducer extends DefaultProducer implements AsyncProducer {\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192    public DefaultAsyncProducer(Endpoint endpoint) {\n    33\u2192        super(endpoint);\n    34\u2192    }\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192    @Override\n    37\u2192    public void process(Exchange exchange) throws Exception {\n    38\u2192        AsyncProcessorAwaitManager awaitManager\n    39\u2192                = PluginHelper.getAsyncProcessorAwaitManager(exchange.getContext());\n    40\u2192        awaitManager.process(this, exchange);\n    41\u2192    }\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192    @Override\n    44\u2192    public CompletableFuture<Exchange> processAsync(Exchange exchange) {\n    45\u2192        AsyncCallbackToCompletableFutureAdapter<Exchange> callback = new AsyncCallbackToCompletableFutureAdapter<>(exchange);\n    46\u2192        process(exchange, callback);\n    47\u2192        return callback.getFuture();\n    48\u2192    }\n    49\u2192}\n    50\u2192"

in writing, software\n    12\u2192 * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192package org.apache.camel.support;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import org.apache.camel.Endpoint;\n    20\u2192import org.apache.camel.Exchange;\n    21\u2192import org.apache.camel.PollingConsumer;\n    22\u2192import org.apache.camel.Processor;\n    23\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.ExceptionHandler;\n    24\u2192import org.apache.camel.support.service.ServiceSupport;\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192/**\n    27\u2192 * A useful base class for implementations of {@link PollingConsumer}\n    28\u2192 */\n    29\u2192public abstract class PollingConsumerSupport extends ServiceSupport implements PollingConsumer {\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192    private final Endpoint endpoint;\n    32\u2192    private ExceptionHandler exceptionHandler;\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192    public PollingConsumerSupport(Endpoint endpoint) {\n    35\u2192        this.endpoint = endpoint;\n    36\u2192        this.exceptionHandler = new LoggingExceptionHandler(endpoint.getCamelContext(), getClass());\n    37\u2192    }\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192    @Override\n    40\u2192    public String toString() {\n    41\u2192        return \"PollingConsumer on \" + endpoint;\n    42\u2192    }\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192    @Override\n    45\u2192    public Endpoint getEndpoint() {\n    46\u2192        return endpoint;\n    47\u2192    }\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192    @Override\n    50\u2192    public Processor getProcessor() {\n    51\u2192        return null;\n    52\u2192    }\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192    @Override\n    55\u2192    public Exchange createExchange(boolean autoRelease) {\n    56\u2192        throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\"Not supported on PollingConsumer\");\n    57\u2192    }\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192    @Override\n    60\u2192    public void releaseExchange(Exchange exchange, boolean autoRelease) {\n    61\u2192        throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\"Not supported on PollingConsumer\");\n    62\u2192    }\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192    public ExceptionHandler getExceptionHandler() {\n    65\u2192        return exceptionHandler;\n    66\u2192    }\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192    public void setExceptionHandler(ExceptionHandler exceptionHandler) {\n    69\u2192        this.exceptionHandler = exceptionHandler;\n    70\u2192    }\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192    /**\n    73\u2192     * Handles the given exception using the {@link #getExceptionHandler()}\n    74\u2192     *\n    75\u2192     * @param t the exception to handle\n    76\u2192     */\n    77\u2192    protected void handleException(Throwable t) {\n    78\u2192        getExceptionHandler().handleException(t);\n    79\u2192    }\n    80\u2192}\n    81\u2192"

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     3\u2192 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\n     4\u2192 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n     5\u2192 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n     6\u2192 * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n     7\u2192 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     8\u2192 *\n     9\u2192 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    10\u2192 *\n    11\u2192 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    12\u2192 * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192package org.apache.camel.support;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import java.util.HashMap;\n    20\u2192import java.util.LinkedHashMap;\n    21\u2192import java.util.Map;\n    22\u2192import java.util.concurrent.ScheduledExecutorService;\n    23\u2192import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;\n    24\u2192import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicInteger;\n    25\u2192import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicLong;\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192import org.apache.camel.Component;\n    28\u2192import org.apache.camel.Endpoint;\n    29\u2192import org.apache.camel.Exchange;\n    30\u2192import org.apache.camel.FailedToCreateConsumerException;\n    31\u2192import org.apache.camel.LoggingLevel;\n    32\u2192import org.apache.camel.PollingConsumerPollingStrategy;\n    33\u2192import org.apache.camel.Processor;\n    34\u2192import org.apache.camel.Suspendable;\n    35\u2192import org.apache.camel.health.HealthCheck;\n    36\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.HttpResponseAware;\n    37\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.PollingConsumerPollStrategy;\n    38\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.ScheduledPollConsumerScheduler;\n    39\u2192import org.apache.camel.support.service.ServiceHelper;\n    40\u2192import org.apache.camel.util.ObjectHelper;\n    41\u2192import org.apache.camel.util.PropertiesHelper;\n    42\u2192import org.slf4j.Logger;\n    43\u2192import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192/**\n    46\u2192 * A useful base class for any consumer which is polling based\n    47\u2192 */\n    48\u2192public abstract class ScheduledPollConsumer extends DefaultConsumer\n    49\u2192        implements Runnable, Suspendable, PollingConsumerPollingStrategy {\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(ScheduledPollConsumer.class);\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192    private ScheduledPollConsumerScheduler scheduler;\n    54\u2192    private ScheduledExecutorService scheduledExecutorService;\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192    // if adding more options then align with org.apache.camel.support.ScheduledPollEndpoint\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192    private boolean startScheduler = true;\n    59\u2192    private long initialDelay = 1000;\n    60\u2192    private long delay = 500;\n    61\u2192    private TimeUnit timeUnit = TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS;\n    62\u2192    private boolean useFixedDelay = true;\n    63\u2192    private PollingConsumerPollStrategy pollStrategy;\n    64\u2192    private LoggingLevel runLoggingLevel = LoggingLevel.TRACE;\n    65\u2192    private boolean sendEmptyMessageWhenIdle;\n    66\u2192    private boolean greedy;\n    67\u2192    private int backoffMultiplier;\n    68\u2192    private int backoffIdleThreshold;\n    69\u2192    private int backoffErrorThreshold;\n    70\u2192    private long repeatCount;\n    71\u2192    private Map<String, Object> schedulerProperties;\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192    // state during running\n    74\u2192    private final AtomicInteger pollingCounter = new AtomicInteger();\n    75\u2192    private final AtomicInteger backoffCounter = new AtomicInteger();\n    76\u2192    private final AtomicLong idleCounter = new AtomicLong();\n    77\u2192    private final AtomicLong errorCounter = new AtomicLong();\n    78\u2192    private final AtomicLong successCounter = new AtomicLong();\n    79\u2192    private volatile Throwable lastError;\n    80\u2192    private volatile Map<String, Object> lastErrorDetails;\n    81\u2192    private final AtomicLong counter = new AtomicLong();\n    82\u2192    private volatile boolean firstPollDone;\n    83\u2192    private volatile boolean forceReady;\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192    public ScheduledPollConsumer(Endpoint endpoint, Processor processor) {\n    86\u2192        super(endpoint, processor);\n    87\u2192    }\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192    public ScheduledPollConsumer(Endpoint endpoint, Processor processor, ScheduledExecutorService scheduledExecutorService) {\n    90\u2192        super(endpoint, processor);\n    91\u2192        // we have been given an existing thread pool, so we should not manage its lifecycle\n    92\u2192        // so we should keep shutdownExecutor as false\n    93\u2192        this.scheduledExecutorService = scheduledExecutorService;\n    94\u2192        ObjectHelper.notNull(scheduledExecutorService, \"scheduledExecutorService\");\n    95\u2192    }\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192    /**\n    98\u2192     * Invoked whenever we should be polled\n    99\u2192     */\n   100\u2192    @Override\n   101\u2192    public void run() {\n   102\u2192        // avoid this thread to throw exceptions because the thread pool wont re-schedule a new thread\n   103\u2192        try {\n   104\u2192            // log starting\n   105\u2192            if (LoggingLevel.ERROR == runLoggingLevel) {\n   106\u2192                LOG.error(\"Scheduled task started on:   {}\", this.getEndpoint());\n   107\u2192            } else if (LoggingLevel.WARN == runLoggingLevel) {\n   108\u2192                LOG.warn(\"Scheduled task started on:   {}\", this.getEndpoint());\n   109\u2192            } else if (LoggingLevel.INFO == runLoggingLevel) {\n   110\u2192                LOG.info(\"Scheduled task started on:   {}\", this.getEndpoint());\n   111\u2192            } else if (LoggingLevel.DEBUG == runLoggingLevel) {\n   112\u2192                LOG.debug(\"Scheduled task started on:   {}\", this.getEndpoint());\n   113\u2192            } else {\n   114\u2192                LOG.trace(\"Scheduled task started on:   {}\", this.getEndpoint());\n   115\u2192            }\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192            // execute scheduled task\n   118\u2192            doRun();\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192            // log completed\n   121\u2192            if (LoggingLevel.ERROR == runLoggingLevel) {\n   122\u2192                LOG.error(\"Scheduled task completed on: {}\", this.getEndpoint());\n   123\u2192            } else if (LoggingLevel.WARN == runLoggingLevel) {\n   124\u2192                LOG.warn(\"Scheduled task completed on: {}\", this.getEndpoint());\n   125\u2192            } else if (LoggingLevel.INFO == runLoggingLevel) {\n   126\u2192                LOG.info(\"Scheduled task completed on: {}\", this.getEndpoint());\n   127\u2192            } else if (LoggingLevel.DEBUG == runLoggingLevel) {\n   128\u2192                LOG.debug(\"Scheduled task completed on: {}\", this.getEndpoint());\n   129\u2192            } else {\n   130\u2192                LOG.trace(\"Scheduled task completed on: {}\", this.getEndpoint());\n   131\u2192            }\n   132\u2192        } catch (Error e) {\n   133\u2192            // need to log so there is visibility as otherwise the user may not see anything in logs\n   134\u2192            LOG.error(\"Fatal error occurred during running scheduled task on: {}, due: {}.\",\n   135\u2192                    this.getEndpoint(), e.getMessage(), e);\n   136\u2192            throw e;\n   137\u2192        } catch (Throwable e) {\n   138\u2192            LOG.error(\"Error occurred during running scheduled task on: {}, due: {}.\"\n   139\u2192                      + \" This exception is ignored and the task will run again on next poll.\",\n   140\u2192                    this.getEndpoint(), e.getMessage(), e);\n   141\u2192        }\n   142\u2192    }\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192    private void doRun() {\n   145\u2192        if (isSuspended()) {\n   146\u2192            LOG.trace(\"Cannot start to poll: {} as its suspended\", this.getEndpoint());\n   147\u2192            return;\n   148\u2192        }\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192        // should we backoff if its enabled, and either the idle or error counter is > the threshold\n   151\u2192        if (backoffMultiplier > 0\n   152\u2192                // either idle or error threshold could be not in use, so check for that and use MAX_VALUE if not in use\n   153\u2192                && idleCounter.longValue() >= (backoffIdleThreshold > 0 ? backoffIdleThreshold : Integer.MAX_VALUE)\n   154\u2192                || errorCounter.longValue() >= (backoffErrorThreshold > 0 ? backoffErrorThreshold : Integer.MAX_VALUE)) {\n   155\u2192            final int currentBackoffCounter = backoffCounter.incrementAndGet();\n   156\u2192            if (currentBackoffCounter < backoffMultiplier) {\n   157\u2192                // yes we should backoff\n   158\u2192                if (idleCounter.intValue() > 0) {\n   159\u2192                    LOG.debug(\"doRun() backoff due subsequent {} idles (backoff at {}/{})\", idleCounter.longValue(),\n   160\u2192                            backoffCounter.intValue(), backoffMultiplier);\n   161\u2192                } else {\n   162\u2192                    LOG.debug(\"doRun() backoff due subsequent {} errors (backoff at {}/{})\", errorCounter.intValue(),\n   163\u2192                            backoffCounter.intValue(), backoffMultiplier);\n   164\u2192                }\n   165\u2192                return;\n   166\u2192            } else {\n   167\u2192                // we are finished with backoff so reset counters\n   168\u2192                idleCounter.set(0);\n   169\u2192                errorCounter.set(0);\n   170\u2192                backoffCounter.set(0);\n   171\u2192                successCounter.set(0);\n   172\u2192                LOG.trace(\"doRun() backoff finished, resetting counters.\");\n   173\u2192            }\n   174\u2192        }\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192        long count = counter.incrementAndGet();\n   177\u2192        boolean stopFire = repeatCount > 0 && count > repeatCount;\n   178\u2192        if (stopFire) {\n   179\u2192            LOG.debug(\"Cancelling {} scheduler as repeat count limit reached after {} counts.\", getEndpoint(), repeatCount);\n   180\u2192            scheduler.unscheduleTask();\n   181\u2192            return;\n   182\u2192        }\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192        int retryCounter = -1;\n   185\u2192        boolean done = false;\n   186\u2192        Throwable cause = null;\n   187\u2192        int polledMessages = 0;\n   188\u2192\n   189\u2192        while (!done) {\n   190\u2192            try {\n   191\u2192                // eager assume we are done\n   192\u2192                done = true;\n   193\u2192                if (isPollAllowed()) {\n   194\u2192\n   195\u2192                    if (retryCounter == -1) {\n   196\u2192                        LOG.trace(\"Starting to poll: {}\", this.getEndpoint());\n   197\u2192                    } else {\n   198\u2192                        LOG.debug(\"Retrying attempt {} to poll: {}\", retryCounter, this.getEndpoint());\n   199\u2192                    }\n   200\u2192\n   201\u2192                    // mark we are polling which should also include the begin/poll/commit\n   202\u2192                    pollingCounter.incrementAndGet();\n   203\u2192                    try {\n   204\u2192                        boolean begin = pollStrategy.begin(this, getEndpoint());\n   205\u2192                        if (begin) {\n   206\u2192                            retryCounter++;\n   207\u2192                            polledMessages = poll();\n   208\u2192                            LOG.trace(\"Polled {} messages\", polledMessages);\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192                            if (polledMessages == 0 && isSendEmptyMessageWhenIdle()) {\n   211\u2192                                // send an \"empty\" exchange\n   212\u2192                                processEmptyMessage();\n   213\u2192                            }\n   214\u2192\n   215\u2192                            pollStrategy.commit(this, getEndpoint(), polledMessages);\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192                            if (polledMessages > 0 && isGreedy()) {\n   218\u2192                                done = false;\n   219\u2192                                retryCounter = -1;\n   220\u2192                                LOG.trace(\"Greedy polling after processing {} messages\", polledMessages);\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192                                // clear any error that might be since we have successfully polled, otherwise readiness checks might believe the\n   223\u2192                                // consumer to be unhealthy\n   224\u2192                                errorCounter.set(0);\n   225\u2192                                lastError = null;\n   226\u2192                                lastErrorDetails = null;\n   227\u2192\n   228\u2192                                // setting firstPollDone to true if greedy polling is enabled\n   229\u2192                                firstPollDone = true;\n   230\u2192                            }\n   231\u2192                        } else {\n   232\u2192                            LOG.debug(\"Cannot begin polling as pollStrategy returned false: {}\", pollStrategy);\n   233\u2192                        }\n   234\u2192                    } finally {\n   235\u2192                        pollingCounter.decrementAndGet();\n   236\u2192                    }\n   237\u2192                }\n   238\u2192\n   239\u2192                LOG.trace(\"Finished polling: {}\", this.getEndpoint());\n   240\u2192            } catch (Exception e) {\n   241\u2192                try {\n   242\u2192                    boolean retry = pollStrategy.rollback(this, getEndpoint(), retryCounter, e);\n   243\u2192                    if (retry) {\n   244\u2192                        // do not set cause as we retry\n   245\u2192                        done = false;\n   246\u2192                    } else {\n   247\u2192                        cause = e;\n   248\u2192                        done = true;\n   249\u2192                    }\n   250\u2192                } catch (Exception t) {\n   251\u2192                    cause = t;\n   252\u2192                    done = true;\n   253\u2192                }\n   254\u2192            }\n   255\u2192\n   256\u2192            if (cause != null && isRunAllowed()) {\n   257\u2192                // let exception handler deal with the caused exception\n   258\u2192                // but suppress this during shutdown as the logs may get flooded with exceptions during shutdown/forced shutdown\n   259\u2192                try {\n   260\u2192                    getExceptionHandler().handleException(\"Failed polling endpoint: \" + getEndpoint()\n   261\u2192                                                          + \". Will try again at next poll\",\n   262\u2192                            cause);\n   263\u2192                } catch (Exception e) {\n   264\u2192                    LOG.warn(\"Error handling exception. This exception will be ignored.\", e);\n   265\u2192                }\n   266\u2192            }\n   267\u2192        }\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192        if (cause != null) {\n   270\u2192            idleCounter.set(0);\n   271\u2192            successCounter.set(0);\n   272\u2192            errorCounter.incrementAndGet();\n   273\u2192            lastError = cause;\n   274\u2192            // enrich last error with http response code if possible\n   275\u2192            if (cause instanceof HttpResponseAware httpResponseAware) {\n   276\u2192                int code = httpResponseAware.getHttpResponseCode();\n   277\u2192                if (code > 0) {\n   278\u2192                    addLastErrorDetail(HealthCheck.HTTP_RESPONSE_CODE, code);\n   279\u2192                }\n   280\u2192            }\n   281\u2192        } else {\n   282\u2192            if (polledMessages == 0) {\n   283\u2192                idleCounter.incrementAndGet();\n   284\u2192            } else {\n   285\u2192                idleCounter.set(0);\n   286\u2192            }\n   287\u2192            successCounter.incrementAndGet();\n   288\u2192            errorCounter.set(0);\n   289\u2192            lastError = null;\n   290\u2192            lastErrorDetails = null;\n   291\u2192        }\n   292\u2192\n   293\u2192        // now first pool is done after the poll is complete\n   294\u2192        firstPollDone = true;\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192        LOG.trace(\"doRun() done with idleCounter={}, successCounter={}, errorCounter={}\", idleCounter.longValue(),\n   297\u2192                successCounter.longValue(), errorCounter.longValue());\n   298\u2192\n   299\u2192        // avoid this thread to throw exceptions because the thread pool wont re-schedule a new thread\n   300\u2192    }\n   301\u2192\n   302\u2192    /**\n   303\u2192     * No messages to poll so send an empty message instead.\n   304\u2192     *\n   305\u2192     * @throws Exception is thrown if error processing the empty message.\n   306\u2192     */\n   307\u2192    protected void processEmptyMessage() throws Exception {\n   308\u2192        Exchange exchange = getEndpoint().createExchange();\n   309\u2192        LOG.debug(\"Sending empty message as there were no messages from polling: {}\", this.getEndpoint());\n   310\u2192        getProcessor().process(exchange);\n   311\u2192    }\n   312\u2192\n   313\u2192    // Properties\n   314\u2192    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192    protected boolean isPollAllowed() {\n   317\u2192        return isRunAllowed() && !isSuspended();\n   318\u2192    }\n   319\u2192\n   320\u2192    /**\n   321\u2192     * Whether polling is currently in progress\n   322\u2192     */\n   323\u2192    public boolean isPolling() {\n   324\u2192        return pollingCounter.get() > 0;\n   325\u2192    }\n   326\u2192\n   327\u2192    public ScheduledPollConsumerScheduler getScheduler() {\n   328\u2192        return scheduler;\n   329\u2192    }\n   330\u2192\n   331\u2192    public void setScheduler(ScheduledPollConsumerScheduler scheduler) {\n   332\u2192        this.scheduler = scheduler;\n   333\u2192    }\n   334\u2192\n   335\u2192    public Map<String, Object> getSchedulerProperties() {\n   336\u2192        return schedulerProperties;\n   337\u2192    }\n   338\u2192\n   339\u2192    public void setSchedulerProperties(Map<String, Object> schedulerProperties) {\n   340\u2192        this.schedulerProperties = schedulerProperties;\n   341\u2192    }\n   342\u2192\n   343\u2192    public long getInitialDelay() {\n   344\u2192 void setSendEmptyMessageWhenIdle(boolean sendEmptyMessageWhenIdle) {\n   400\u2192        this.sendEmptyMessageWhenIdle = sendEmptyMessageWhenIdle;\n   401\u2192    }\n   402\u2192\n   403\u2192 return greedy;\n   409\u2192    }\n   410\u2192\n   411\u2192    public void setGreedy(boolean greedy) {\n   412\u2192        this.greedy = greedy;\n   413\u2192    }\n   414\u2192\n   415\u2192 isSchedulerStarted() {\n   456\u2192        return scheduler.isSchedulerStarted();\n   457\u2192    }\n   458\u2192\n   459\u2192    public void setScheduledExecutorService(ScheduledExecutorService scheduledExecutorService) {\n   460\u2192        this.scheduledExecutorService = scheduledExecutorService;\n   461\u2192    }\n   462\u2192\n   463\u2192    /**\n   464\u2192     * Gets the error counter. If the counter is > 0 that means the consumer failed polling for the last N number of\n   465\u2192     * times. When the consumer is successfully again, then the error counter resets to zero.\n   466\u2192     *\n   467\u2192     * @see #getSuccessCounter()\n   468\u2192     */\n   469\u2192    public long getErrorCounter() {\n   470\u2192        return errorCounter.longValue();\n   471\u2192    }\n   472\u2192\n   473\u2192    /**\n   474\u2192     * Gets the success counter. If the success is > 0 that means the consumer succeeded polling for the last N number\n   475\u2192     * of times. When the consumer is failing again, then the success counter resets to zero.\n   476\u2192     *\n   477\u2192     * @see #getErrorCounter()\n   478\u2192     */\n   479\u2192    public long getSuccessCounter() {\n   480\u2192        return successCounter.longValue();\n   481\u2192    }\n   482\u2192\n   483\u2192    /**\n   484\u2192     * Gets the total number of polls run.\n   485\u2192     */\n   486\u2192    public long getCounter() {\n   487\u2192        return counter.get();\n   488\u2192    }\n   489\u2192\n   490\u2192    /**\n   491\u2192     * Whether a first pool attempt has been done (also if the consumer has been restarted).\n   492\u2192     */\n   493\u2192    public boolean isFirstPollDone() {\n   494\u2192        return firstPollDone;\n   495\u2192    }\n   496\u2192\n   497\u2192    /**\n   498\u2192     * Whether the consumer is ready and has established connection to its target system, or first poll has been\n   499\u2192     * completed successfully.\n   500\u2192     *\n   501\u2192     * The health-check is using this information to know when the consumer is ready for readiness checks.\n   502\u2192     */\n   503\u2192    public boolean isConsumerReady() {\n   504\u2192        // we regard the consumer as ready if it was explicit forced to be ready (component specific)\n   505\u2192        // or that it has completed its first poll without an exception was thrown\n   506\u2192        // during connecting to target system and accepting data\n   507\u2192        return forceReady || firstPollDone;\n   508\u2192    }\n   509\u2192\n   510\u2192    // Implementation methods\n   511\u2192    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   512\u2192\n   513\u2192    /**\n   514\u2192     * Forces the consumer to be marked as ready. This can be used by components that need to mark this sooner than\n   515\u2192     * usual (default marked as ready after first poll is done). This allows health-checks to be ready before an entire\n   516\u2192     * poll is completed.\n   517\u2192     *\n   518\u2192     * This is for example needed by the FTP component as polling a large file can take long time, causing a\n   519\u2192     * health-check to not be ready within reasonable time.\n   520\u2192     */\n   521\u2192    protected void forceConsumerAsReady() {\n   522\u2192        forceReady = true;\n   523\u2192    }\n   524\u2192\n   525\u2192    /**\n   526\u2192     * Gets the last caused error (exception) for the last poll that failed. When the consumer is successfully again,\n   527\u2192     * then the error resets to null.\n   528\u2192     */\n   529\u2192    protected Throwable getLastError() {\n   530\u2192        return lastError;\n   531\u2192    }\n   532\u2192\n   533\u2192    /**\n   534\u2192     * Gets the last caused error (exception) details for the last poll that failed. When the consumer is successfully\n   535\u2192     * again, then the error resets to null.\n   536\u2192     *\n   537\u2192     * Some consumers can provide additional error details here, besides the caused exception. For example if the\n   538\u2192     * consumer uses HTTP then the {@link org.apache.camel.health.HealthCheck#HTTP_RESPONSE_CODE} can be included.\n   539\u2192     *\n   540\u2192     * @return error details, or null if no details exists.\n   541\u2192     */\n   542\u2192    protected Map<String, Object> getLastErrorDetails() {\n   543\u2192        return lastErrorDetails;\n   544\u2192    }\n   545\u2192\n   546\u2192    /**\n   547\u2192     * Adds a detail to the last caused error (exception) for the last poll that failed. When the consumer is\n   548\u2192     * successfully again, then the error resets to null.\n   549\u2192     *\n   550\u2192     * Some consumers can provide additional error details here, besides the caused exception. For example if the\n   551\u2192     * consumer uses HTTP then the {@link org.apache.camel.health.HealthCheck#HTTP_RESPONSE_CODE} can be included.\n   552\u2192     *\n   553\u2192     * @param key   the key (see {@link org.apache.camel.health.HealthCheck})\n   554\u2192     * @param value the value\n   555\u2192     */\n   556\u2192    protected void addLastErrorDetail(String key, Object value) {\n   557\u2192        if (lastErrorDetails == null) {\n   558\u2192            lastErrorDetails = new HashMap<>();\n   559\u2192        }\n   560\u2192        if (lastErrorDetails != null) {\n   561\u2192            lastErrorDetails.put(key, value);\n   562\u2192        }\n   563\u2192    }\n   564\u2192\n   565\u2192    /**\n   566\u2192     * The polling method which is invoked periodically to poll this consumer\n   567\u2192     *\n   568\u2192     * @return           number of messages polled, will be <tt>0</tt> if no message was polled at all.\n   569\u2192     * @throws Exception can be thrown if an exception occurred during polling\n   570\u2192     */\n   571\u2192    protected abstract int poll() throws Exception;\n   572\u2192\n   573\u2192    /**\n   574\u2192     * The polling method which is invoked periodically to poll this consumer, for components that support\n   575\u2192     * {@link org.apache.camel.DynamicPollingConsumer} such as camel-file.\n   576\u2192     *\n   577\u2192     * @param  dynamic   the current exchange when being used from Poll and PollEnrich EIPs in dynamic mode,\n   578\u2192     * @return           number of messages polled, will be <tt>0</tt> if no message was polled at all.\n   579\u2192     * @throws Exception can be thrown if an exception occurred during polling\n   580\u2192     */\n   581\u2192    protected int poll(Exchange dynamic) throws Exception {\n   582\u2192        return poll();\n   583\u2192    }\n   584\u2192\n   585\u2192    @Override\n   586\u2192    protected void doBuild() throws Exception {\n   587\u2192        if (getHealthCheck() == null) {\n   588\u2192            String id = \"consumer:\" + getRouteId();\n   589\u2192            ScheduledPollConsumerHealthCheck hc = new ScheduledPollConsumerHealthCheck(this, id);\n   590\u2192            // is there a custom initial state the consumer must use\n   591\u2192            HealthCheck.State initialState = initialHealthCheckState();\n   592\u2192            if (initialState != null) {\n   593\u2192                hc.setInitialState(initialState);\n   594\u2192            }\n   595\u2192            setHealthCheck(hc);\n   596\u2192        }\n   597\u2192        super.doBuild();\n   598\u2192    }\n   599\u2192\n   600\u2192    /**\n   601\u2192     * Used to allow special consumers to override the initial state of the health check (readiness check) during\n   602\u2192     * startup.\n   603\u2192     *\n   604\u2192     * Consumers that are internal only such as camel-scheduler uses UP as initial state because the scheduler may be\n   605\u2192     * configured to run only very in-frequently and therefore the overall health-check state would be affected and seen\n   606\u2192     * as DOWN.\n   607\u2192     *\n   608\u2192     * @return null to use the initial state configured, otherwise force using the returned state.\n   609\u2192     */\n   610\u2192    protected HealthCheck.State initialHealthCheckState() {\n   611\u2192        return null;\n   612\u2192    }\n   613\u2192\n   614\u2192    @Override\n   615\u2192    protected void doInit() throws Exception {\n   616\u2192        super.doInit();\n   617\u2192\n   618\u2192        Component component = getEndpoint().getComponent();\n   619\u2192        if (component instanceof HealthCheckComponent hcc) {\n   620\u2192            getHealthCheck().setEnabled(hcc.isHealthCheckConsumerEnabled());\n   621\u2192        }\n   622\u2192\n   623\u2192        // validate that if backoff multiplier is in use, the threshold values is set correctly\n   624\u2192        if (backoffMultiplier > 0) {\n   625\u2192            if (backoffIdleThreshold <= 0 && backoffErrorThreshold <= 0) {\n   626\u2192                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\n   627\u2192                        \"backoffIdleThreshold and/or backoffErrorThreshold must be configured to a positive value when using backoffMultiplier\");\n   628\u2192            }\n   629\u2192            LOG.debug(\"Using backoff[multiplier={}, idleThreshold={}, errorThreshold={}] on {}\", backoffMultiplier,\n   630\u2192                    backoffIdleThreshold, backoffErrorThreshold, getEndpoint());\n   631\u2192        }\n   632\u2192        if (pollStrategy == null) {\n   633\u2192            pollStrategy = new DefaultPollingConsumerPollStrategy();\n   634\u2192        }\n   635\u2192    }\n   636\u2192\n   637\u2192    @Override\n   638\u2192    protected void doStart() throws Exception {\n   639\u2192        super.doStart();\n   640\u2192\n   641\u2192        boolean newScheduler = false;\n   642\u2192        if (scheduler == null) {\n   643\u2192            DefaultScheduledPollConsumerScheduler scheduler\n   644\u2192                    = new DefaultScheduledPollConsumerScheduler(scheduledExecutorService);\n   645\u2192            scheduler.setDelay(delay);\n   646\u2192            scheduler.setInitialDelay(initialDelay);\n   647\u2192            scheduler.setTimeUnit(timeUnit);\n   648\u2192            scheduler.setUseFixedDelay(useFixedDelay);\n   649\u2192            this.scheduler = scheduler;\n   650\u2192            newScheduler = true;\n   651\u2192        }\n   652\u2192        ObjectHelper.notNull(scheduler, \"scheduler\", this);\n   653\u2192        scheduler.setCamelContext(getEndpoint().getCamelContext());\n   654\u2192\n   655\u2192        // configure scheduler with options from this consumer\n   656\u2192        if (schedulerProperties != null && !schedulerProperties.isEmpty()) {\n   657\u2192            // need to use a copy in case the consumer is restarted so we keep the properties\n   658\u2192            Map<String, Object> copy = new LinkedHashMap<>(schedulerProperties);\n   659\u2192            PropertyBindingSupport.build().bind(getEndpoint().getCamelContext(), scheduler, copy);\n   660\u2192            // special for trigger and job parameters\n   661\u2192            Map<String, Object> triggerParameters = PropertiesHelper.extractProperties(copy, \"trigger.\");\n   662\u2192            Map<String, Object> jobParameters = PropertiesHelper.extractProperties(copy, \"job.\");\n   663\u2192            PropertyBindingSupport.build().bind(getEndpoint().getCamelContext(), scheduler, \"triggerParameters\",\n   664\u2192                    triggerParameters);\n   665\u2192            PropertyBindingSupport.build().bind(getEndpoint().getCamelContext(), scheduler, \"jobParameters\", jobParameters);\n   666\u2192            if (!copy.isEmpty()) {\n   667\u2192                throw new FailedToCreateConsumerException(\n   668\u2192                        getEndpoint(), \"There are \" + copy.size()\n   669\u2192                                       + \" scheduler parameters that couldn't be set on the endpoint.\"\n   670\u2192                                       + \" Check the uri if the parameters are spelt correctly and that they are properties of the endpoint.\"\n   671\u2192                                       + \" Unknown parameters=[\" + copy + \"]\");\n   672\u2192            }\n   673\u2192        }\n   674\u2192        afterConfigureScheduler(scheduler, newScheduler);\n   675\u2192\n   676\u2192        scheduler.onInit(this);\n   677\u2192\n   678\u2192        if (scheduler != null) {\n   679\u2192            scheduler.scheduleTask(this);\n   680\u2192            ServiceHelper.startService(scheduler);\n   681\u2192\n   682\u2192            if (isStartScheduler()) {\n   683\u2192                startScheduler();\n   684\u2192            }\n   685\u2192        }\n   686\u2192    }\n   687\u2192\n   688\u2192    /**\n   689\u2192     * After the scheduler has been configured\n   690\u2192     *\n   691\u2192     * @param scheduler    the scheduler\n   692\u2192     * @param newScheduler true if this consumer created a new scheduler, or false if an existing (shared) scheduler is\n   693\u2192     *                     being used\n   694\u2192     */\n   695\u2192    protected void afterConfigureScheduler(ScheduledPollConsumerScheduler scheduler, boolean newScheduler) {\n   696\u2192        // noop\n   697\u2192    }\n   698\u2192\n   699\u2192    /**\n   700\u2192     * Starts the scheduler.\n   701\u2192     * <p/>\n   702\u2192     * If the scheduler is already started, then this is a noop method call.\n   703\u2192     */\n   704\u2192    public void startScheduler() {\n   705\u2192        scheduler.startScheduler();\n   706\u2192    }\n   707\u2192\n   708\u2192    @Override\n   709\u2192    protected void doStop() throws Exception {\n   710\u2192        if (scheduler != null) {\n   711\u2192            scheduler.unscheduleTask();\n   712\u2192            ServiceHelper.stopAndShutdownServices(scheduler);\n   713\u2192        }\n   714\u2192\n   715\u2192        // clear counters\n   716\u2192        backoffCounter.set(0);\n   717\u2192        idleCounter.set(0);\n   718\u2192        errorCounter.set(0);\n   719\u2192        successCounter.set(0);\n   720\u2192        counter.set(0);\n   721\u2192        // clear ready state\n   722\u2192        firstPollDone = false;\n   723\u2192        forceReady = false;\n   724\u2192\n   725\u2192        super.doStop();\n   726\u2192    }\n   727\u2192\n   728\u2192    @Override\n   729\u2192    protected void doShutdown() throws Exception {\n   730\u2192        ServiceHelper.stopAndShutdownServices(scheduler);\n   731\u2192        super.doShutdown();\n   732\u2192    }\n   733\u2192\n   734\u2192    @Override\n   735\u2192    protected void doSuspend() throws Exception {\n   736\u2192        // dont stop/cancel the future task since we just check in the run method\n   737\u2192    }\n   738\u2192\n   739\u2192    @Override\n   740\u2192    public void onInit() throws Exception {\n   741\u2192        // make sure the scheduler is starting\n   742\u2192        startScheduler = true;\n   743\u2192    }\n   744\u2192\n   745\u2192    @Override\n   746\u2192    public long beforePoll(long timeout) throws Exception {\n   747\u2192        LOG.trace(\"Before poll {}\", getEndpoint());\n   748\u2192        // resume or start our self\n   749\u2192        if (!ServiceHelper.resumeService(this)) {\n   750\u2192            ServiceHelper.startService(this);\n   751\u2192        }\n   752\u2192\n   753\u2192        // ensure at least timeout is as long as one poll delay\n   754\u2192        return Math.max(timeout, getDelay());\n   755\u2192    }\n   756\u2192\n   757\u2192    @Override\n   758\u2192    public void afterPoll() throws Exception {\n   759\u2192        LOG.trace(\"After poll {}\", getEndpoint());\n   760\u2192        // suspend or stop our self\n   761\u2192        if (!ServiceHelper.suspendService(this)) {\n   762\u2192            ServiceHelper.stopService(this);\n   763\u2192        }\n   764\u2192    }\n   765\u2192\n   766\u2192}\n   767\u2192"
21. Task @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Explore Pipeline and Channel",
  "prompt": "Explore the Apache Camel Pipeline and Channel architecture in /workspace. These are critical processor chain components.\n\nFind and read thoroughly:\n\n1. `Pipeline` class - likely in core/camel-core-processor. This chains processors together.\n2. `CamelInternalProcessor` or `SharedCamelInternalProcessor` - internal processor wrapper\n3. `Channel` interface - in core/camel-api, wraps processors with interceptors\n4. `DefaultChannel` - the implementation of Channel\n5. Any `Navigate` interface that allows traversing the processor chain\n\nFor each, I need:\n- Exact file path\n- How Pipeline chains processors and calls them in sequence\n- How Channel wraps a processor with interceptors, error handlers\n- The process() method flow - how an Exchange moves through the chain\n- The async processing model (AsyncProcessor interface)\n\nAlso find `AsyncProcessor` interface and `AsyncProcessorSupport` / `AsyncProcessorConverterHelper`.\n\nBe very thorough.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a0a4e47",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the Pipeline and Channel architecture. Let me compile a thorough report.\n\n## Comprehensive Report: Apache Camel Pipeline and Channel Architecture\n\nI've thoroughly explored the critical processor chain components in Apache Camel. Here's the complete analysis:\n\n---\n\n### 1. **AsyncProcessor Interface**\n**File:** `/workspace/core/camel-api/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/AsyncProcessor.java`\n\n**Key Characteristics:**\n- Extends `Processor` interface\n- Defines asynchronous processing contract with callback support\n- Two main methods:\n  - `boolean process(Exchange exchange, AsyncCallback callback)` - Returns `true` for synchronous completion, `false` for asynchronous\n  - `CompletableFuture<Exchange> processAsync(Exchange exchange)` - Modern async API using CompletableFuture\n- Allows processors to signal completion via `AsyncCallback` invocation rather than throwing exceptions\n\n---\n\n### 2. **Pipeline Class - Processor Chain Orchestrator**\n**File:** `/workspace/core/camel-core-processor/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/processor/Pipeline.java`\n\n**Architecture:**\n- Extends `BaseProcessorSupport`, implements `Navigate<Processor>`, `Traceable`\n- **Key Fields:**\n  - `List<AsyncProcessor> processors` - converted to async for uniform handling\n  - `ReactiveExecutor reactiveExecutor` - schedules work on appropriate executor\n  - `PooledExchangeTaskFactory taskFactory` - manages pooled or prototype task instances\n\n**How Pipeline Chains Processors (Lines 154-170):**\n\n```java\npublic boolean process(Exchange exchange, AsyncCallback callback) {\n    // Create task which has state used during routing\n    PooledExchangeTask task = taskFactory.acquire(exchange, callback);\n    \n    if (exchange.isTransacted()) {\n        reactiveExecutor.scheduleQueue(task);  // Transacted: queue executor\n    } else {\n        reactiveExecutor.scheduleMain(task);   // Non-transacted: main executor\n    }\n    return false;  // Always returns false - processing continues asynchronously\n}\n```\n\n**Inner Class PipelineTask (Lines 58-118):**\n- Implements `PooledExchangeTask` and `AsyncCallback`\n- **State Management:** `index` tracks current processor position (0-size)\n- **Core Run Loop (lines 87-117):**\n  1. Checks stop conditions: `exchange.isRouteStop()`, failed state, or past last processor\n  2. Prepares exchange: `ExchangeHelper.prepareOutToIn(exchange)` converts OUT to IN for next processor\n  3. Gets next processor: `AsyncProcessor processor = processors.get(index++)`\n  4. Invokes processor: `processor.process(exchange, this)` - **passes itself as callback**\n  5. When callback invoked (either sync or async), recursively schedules itself again\n  6. On completion, copies results and schedules original callback\n\n**Key Pattern:** Uses callback chaining - each processor invokes the PipelineTask as its callback, which then schedules the next processor\n\n---\n\n### 3. **Channel Interface**\n**File:** `/workspace/core/camel-api/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/Channel.java`\n\n**Purpose:** Acts as a bridge between processors in the route graph, responsible for:\n- Wrapping processors with interceptors\n- Managing error handlers\n- Providing navigation through processor chains\n\n**Key Methods:**\n```java\nvoid initChannel(Route route, NamedNode definition, NamedNode childDefinition,\n                 List<InterceptStrategy> interceptors, Processor nextProcessor,\n                 NamedRoute routeDefinition, boolean first) throws Exception;\n\nvoid postInitChannel() throws Exception;\n\nProcessor getErrorHandler();      // Gets ErrorHandler if configured\nvoid setErrorHandler(Processor);  // Sets ErrorHandler\n\nProcessor getOutput();            // Gets wrapped output (error handler or output)\nProcessor getNextProcessor();     // Gets unwrapped next processor\n\nRoute getRoute();                 // Gets route context\n```\n\n**Extends:** `AsyncProcessor`, `Navigate<Processor>` - allows both async processing and graph traversal\n\n---\n\n### 4. **DefaultChannel Implementation - Wrapping with Interceptors**\n**File:** `/workspace/core/camel-base-engine/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/impl/engine/DefaultChannel.java`\n\n**Class Hierarchy:** `DefaultChannel extends CamelInternalProcessor implements Channel`\n\n**Key Fields:**\n```java\nprivate Processor errorHandler;      // Top-level error handler\nprivate Processor nextProcessor;     // Original next processor (unwrapped)\nprivate Processor output;            // Wrapped output with all interceptors\nprivate Route route;\nprivate ManagementInterceptStrategy.InstrumentationProcessor<?> instrumentationProcessor;\n```\n\n**Critical Method: getOutput() (Lines 76-83):**\n```java\npublic Processor getOutput() {\n    // errorHandler is already decorated with interceptors\n    // so it contains the entire chain - it's our wrapped output\n    return errorHandler != null ? errorHandler : output;\n}\n```\n\n**How Channel Wraps with Interceptors (Lines 149-271):**\n\n1. **Initialization Phase (initChannel):**\n   - Stores nextProcessor (unwrapped)\n   - Sets up debugging, tracing, backlog tracing advices\n   - **Wraps with interceptors in reverse order** (lines 232-259):\n     ```java\n     // reverse list so first will be wrapped last (invoked first)\n     Collections.reverse(interceptors);\n     Processor target = nextProcessor;\n     for (InterceptStrategy strategy : interceptors) {\n         Processor wrapped = strategy.wrapProcessorInInterceptors(\n             route.getCamelContext(), targetOutputDef, target, next);\n         if (!(wrapped instanceof AsyncProcessor)) {\n             // Warn and wrap in bridge adapter\n             wrapped = PluginHelper.getInternalProcessorFactory(camelContext)\n                 .createWrapProcessor(wrapped, target);\n         }\n         target = wrapped;  // Chain grows outward\n     }\n     output = target;\n   ```\n\n2. **Post-Initialization (postInitChannel):**\n   - Handles JMX instrumentation processor\n   - Determines if redelivery is possible\n   - If redelivery enabled, wraps in error handler; otherwise adds as advice\n\n3. **Wrapping Pattern:**\n   - **Order:** Each interceptor is reversed so first is wrapped last\n   - This ensures first interceptor is **outermost** (invoked first)\n   - Each interceptor wraps around the next, creating a layered pipeline\n   - Error handler wraps all interceptors at the outermost layer (if present)\n\n4. **Exchange Flow Through Layers:**\n   ```\n   Exchange enters:\n   ErrorHandler\n   \u2514\u2500\u2192 Interceptor N\n       \u2514\u2500\u2192 Interceptor 2\n           \u2514\u2500\u2192 Interceptor 1\n               \u2514\u2500\u2192 NextProcessor\n   ```\n\n---\n\n### 5. **CamelInternalProcessor - Cross-Cutting Concerns**\n**File:** `/workspace/core/camel-base-engine/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/impl/engine/CamelInternalProcessor.java`\n\n**Purpose:** Internal processor wrapper that Camel routing engine uses for cross-cutting functionality\n\n**Extends:** `DelegateAsyncProcessor implements InternalProcessor`\n\n**Key Responsibilities (Lines 87-100):**\n- Execute `UnitOfWork` (transaction boundaries, synchronizations)\n- Track which route currently processes\n- Execute `RoutePolicy` callbacks\n- Gather JMX performance statistics\n- Tracing and debugging\n- Message History tracking\n- Stream Caching\n- Transformers\n\n**How Advices Execute (Lines 285-362 - process method):**\n\n```java\npublic boolean process(Exchange exchange, AsyncCallback originalCallback) {\n    // 1. Create after-task for cleanup callbacks\n    Object[] states = new Object[statefulAdvices];\n    CamelInternalTask afterTask = new AsyncAfterTask(states);\n    \n    // 2. Execute ALL advices BEFORE in order (lines 329-339)\n    for (int i = 0; i < advices.size(); i++) {\n        CamelInternalProcessorAdvice<?> task = advices.get(i);\n        Object state = task.before(exchange);\n        if (task.hasState()) {\n            states[j++] = state;\n        }\n    }\n    \n    // 3. Check for debugger skip\n    if (exchange.getProperty(ExchangePropertyKey.SKIP_OVER) == Boolean.TRUE) {\n        originalCallback.done(true);\n        return true;\n    }\n    \n    // 4. Delegate to wrapped processor\n    if (exchange.isTransacted()) {\n        return processTransacted(exchange, afterTask);  // Sync\n    }\n    return processNonTransacted(exchange, afterTask);   // Async\n}\n```\n\n**Transacted vs Non-Transacted (Lines 376-440):**\n\n**Non-Transacted (Lines 376-400):**\n```java\nprivate boolean processNonTransacted(Exchange exchange, CamelInternalTask afterTask) {\n    AsyncCallback async = beforeProcess(exchange, afterTask);\n    \n    // DELEGATE TO WRAPPED PROCESSOR\n    boolean sync = processor.process(exchange, async);\n    \n    if (!sync) {\n        EventHelper.notifyExchangeAsyncProcessingStartedEvent(camelContext, exchange);\n    }\n    return sync;\n}\n```\n\n**Transacted (Lines 418-440):**\n```java\nprivate boolean processTransacted(Exchange exchange, CamelInternalTask afterTask) {\n    // SYNCHRONOUS ONLY\n    try {\n        processor.process(exchange);  // Sync call\n    } catch (Exception e) {\n        exchange.setException(e);\n    } finally {\n        afterTask.done(true);  // Execute after-advices\n    }\n    return true;  // Always synchronous\n}\n```\n\n**After-Task Callback (Lines 232-282 - AsyncAfterTask.done):**\n```java\npublic void done(boolean doneSync) {\n    try {\n        // Execute AFTER advices in REVERSE order\n        AdviceIterator.runAfterTasks(advices, states, exchange);\n    } finally {\n        // CRITICAL: invoke original callback\n        if (originalCallback != null) {\n            reactiveExecutor.schedule(originalCallback);\n        }\n        // Release pooled task\n        if (taskFactory != null) {\n            taskFactory.release(this);\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Key Advices:**\n1. `UnitOfWorkProcessorAdvice` - Manages UoW lifecycle (lines 1114-1194)\n2. `RoutePolicyAdvice` - Route policy callbacks (lines 529-603)\n3. `RouteInflightRepositoryAdvice` - Tracks in-flight exchanges (lines 499-524)\n4. `BacklogTracerRouteAdvice` - Route-level tracing (lines 608-755)\n5. `BacklogTracerAdvice` - Node-level tracing (lines 856-1044)\n6. `TracingAdvice` - Regular tracer (lines 1356-1487)\n7. `MessageHistoryAdvice` - Message history tracking (lines 1200-1245)\n8. `StreamCachingAdvice` - Stream caching (lines 1286-1316)\n9. `DelayerAdvice` - Delaying exchange processing (lines 1321-1351)\n\n---\n\n### 6. **Navigate Interface - Graph Traversal**\n**File:** `/workspace/core/camel-api/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/Navigate.java`\n\n**Purpose:** Allows traversal of processor graph\n\n```java\npublic interface Navigate<T> {\n    /**\n     * Next group of outputs (call only once - not for loop iteration)\n     * @return next group or null if no more outputs\n     */\n    List<T> next();\n    \n    /**\n     * Are there more outputs?\n     * @return true if more outputs\n     */\n    boolean hasNext();\n}\n```\n\n**Implementations:**\n- **DefaultChannel.next()** (lines 91-98): Returns list with single `nextProcessor`\n- **DefaultChannel.hasNext()** (lines 86-88): Returns `nextProcessor != null`\n- **Pipeline.next()** (lines 248-253): Returns all processors in list\n- **Pipeline.hasNext()** (lines 256-258): Returns `!processors.isEmpty()`\n\n---\n\n### 7. **AsyncProcessorSupport - Base Implementation**\n**File:** `/workspace/core/camel-support/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/support/AsyncProcessorSupport.java`\n\n**Purpose:** Abstract base for AsyncProcessor implementations\n\n```java\npublic abstract class AsyncProcessorSupport extends ServiceSupport \n        implements AsyncProcessor {\n    \n    @Override\n    public void process(Exchange exchange) throws Exception {\n        AsyncProcessorAwaitManager awaitManager = \n            PluginHelper.getAsyncProcessorAwaitManager(exchange.getContext());\n        awaitManager.process(this, exchange);  // Bridges sync to async\n    }\n    \n    @Override\n    public CompletableFuture<Exchange> processAsync(Exchange exchange) {\n        AsyncCallbackToCompletableFutureAdapter<Exchange> callback = \n            new AsyncCallbackToCompletableFutureAdapter<>(exchange);\n        process(exchange, callback);\n        return callback.getFuture();\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 8. **AsyncProcessorConverterHelper - Sync-to-Async Bridge**\n**File:** `/workspace/core/camel-support/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/support/AsyncProcessorConverterHelper.java`\n\n**Purpose:** Converts synchronous Processor to AsyncProcessor\n\n**Inner Class: ProcessorToAsyncProcessorBridge (Lines 49-169):**\n\n```java\nprivate static class ProcessorToAsyncProcessorBridge \n        implements DelegateProcessor, AsyncProcessor, Navigate<Processor>, Service {\n    \n    protected final Processor processor;\n    \n    @Override\n    public boolean process(Exchange exchange, AsyncCallback callback) {\n        if (processor == null) {\n            callback.done(true);\n            return true;\n        }\n        try {\n            processor.process(exchange);  // Synchronous call\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            exchange.setException(e);\n        } finally {\n            callback.done(true);  // Signal completion immediately\n        }\n        return true;  // Always synchronous\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Static Methods:**\n```java\npublic static AsyncProcessor convert(Processor value) {\n    if (value instanceof AsyncProcessor asyncProcessor) {\n        return asyncProcessor;\n    }\n    return new ProcessorToAsyncProcessorBridge(value);\n}\n```\n\n**Pattern:** Wraps synchronous processors for uniform async handling; callback invoked synchronously\n\n---\n\n### 9. **DelegateAsyncProcessor - Delegation Base Class**\n**File:** `/workspace/core/camel-support/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/support/processor/DelegateAsyncProcessor.java`\n\n**Purpose:** Base class for policy implementations that wrap AsyncProcessors\n\n```java\npublic class DelegateAsyncProcessor extends AsyncProcessorSupport\n        implements DelegateProcessor, Navigate<Processor>, DisabledAware {\n    \n    protected AsyncProcessor processor;\n    \n    @Override\n    public boolean process(Exchange exchange, AsyncCallback callback) {\n        return processor.process(exchange, callback);  // Simple delegation\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 10. **PipelineHelper - Continuation Logic**\n**File:** `/workspace/core/camel-core-processor/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/processor/PipelineHelper.java`\n\n**Purpose:** Determines if pipeline should continue processing\n\n```java\npublic static boolean continueProcessing(Exchange exchange, String message, Logger log) {\n    boolean stop = exchange.isFailed() \n        || exchange.isRollbackOnly() \n        || exchange.isRollbackOnlyLast()\n        || (exchange.getExchangeExtension().isErrorHandlerHandledSet()\n            && exchange.getExchangeExtension().isErrorHandlerHandled());\n    \n    if (stop) {\n        // Log and return false - stop pipeline\n        return false;\n    }\n    \n    if (exchange.isRouteStop()) {\n        return false;\n    }\n    \n    return true;  // Continue\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 11. **PooledExchangeTask - State Management**\n**File:** `/workspace/core/camel-core-processor/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/processor/PooledExchangeTask.java`\n\n```java\npublic interface PooledExchangeTask extends Runnable {\n    void prepare(Exchange exchange, AsyncCallback callback);\n    void reset();\n}\n```\n\n**Usage:** Tasks are pooled/reused for performance; `prepare()` initializes state, `reset()` clears it for reuse\n\n---\n\n## Complete Process Flow Diagram\n\n```\nExchange.process() via Channel\n    \u2193\nChannel (wrapping interceptors) \u2192 ErrorHandler \u2192 Interceptor-N \u2192 ... \u2192 Interceptor-1\n    \u2193\nDefaultChannel.getOutput() \u2192 returns wrapped chain\n    \u2193\nCamelInternalProcessor.process()\n    \u251c\u2500 FOR EACH advice: execute BEFORE callbacks\n    \u251c\u2500 IF transacted: call processor.process(exchange) synchronously\n    \u2514\u2500 IF non-transacted: call processor.process(exchange, asyncCallback)\n        \u251c\u2500 Processor = Pipeline\n        \u2502   \u251c\u2500 Creates PipelineTask\n        \u2502   \u251c\u2500 Schedules task on ReactiveExecutor\n        \u2502   \u2514\u2500 Returns false (async)\n        \u2502\n        \u2514\u2500 PipelineTask.run() (executed by ReactiveExecutor)\n            \u251c\u2500 Check stop conditions\n            \u251c\u2500 Get processor at current index\n            \u251c\u2500 Call processor.process(exchange, this) - passes itself as callback\n            \u251c\u2500 ON CALLBACK (sync or async):\n            \u2502   \u251c\u2500 If more processors: increment index, reschedule self\n            \u2502   \u2514\u2500 If done: invoke original callback\n            \u2514\u2500 Original callback \u2192 AsyncAfterTask.done()\n                \u251c\u2500 Execute all AFTER advices in reverse order\n                \u251c\u2500 Invoke original callback\n                \u2514\u2500 Release pooled task\n```\n\n---\n\n## Key Architectural Patterns\n\n1. **Callback Chaining:** Each processor invokes next processor's callback, creating async continuation\n2. **Task Pooling:** PooledExchangeTask reused to reduce garbage collection\n3. **Advice Pattern:** Before/after advice execution without deep call stacks\n4. **Lazy Wrapping:** Interceptors wrapped in reverse so first is executed first\n5. **Transacted Synchrony:** Transacted exchanges always block (no async)\n6. **Reactive Scheduling:** Work scheduled to appropriate executor (main vs queue)\n7. **Bridge Pattern:** Sync processors automatically wrapped to AsyncProcessor\n\n---\n\nThis architecture enables Camel's high-performance, non-blocking routing with minimal stack depth and full observability through cross-cutting concerns.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Explore the Apache Camel Pipeline and Channel architecture in /workspace. These are critical processor chain components.\n\nFind and read thoroughly:\n\n1. `Pipeline` class - likely in core/camel-core-processor. This chains processors together.\n2. `CamelInternalProcessor` or `SharedCamelInternalProcessor` - internal processor wrapper\n3. `Channel` interface - in core/camel-api, wraps processors with interceptors\n4. `DefaultChannel` - the implementation of Channel\n5. Any `Navigate` interface that allows traversing the processor chain\n\nFor each, I need:\n- Exact file path\n- How Pipeline chains processors and calls them in sequence\n- How Channel wraps a processor with interceptors, error handlers\n- The process() method flow - how an Exchange moves through the chain\n- The async processing model (AsyncProcessor interface)\n\nAlso find `AsyncProcessor` interface and `AsyncProcessorSupport` / `AsyncProcessorConverterHelper`.\n\nBe very thorough.",

  31\u2192\n    32\u2192    /**\n    33\u2192     * Attempt to resolve an endpoint for the given URI if the component is capable of handling the URI.\n    34\u2192     * <p/>\n    35\u2192     * See the given uri\n    40\u2192     * @throws Exception is thrown if error creating the endpoint\n    41\u2192     * @see              #useRawUri()\n    42\u2192     */\n    43\u2192    Endpoint createEndpoint(String uri) throws Exception;\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192    /**\n    46\u2192     * Attempt to resolve an endpoint for the given URI if the component is capable of handling the URI.\n  uri        the URI to create; either raw or encoded (default)\n    51\u2192     * @param  parameters the parameters for the endpoint\n    52\u2192     * @return            a newly created {@link Endpoint} or null if this component cannot create {@link Endpoint}\n    53\u2192     *                    instances using the given uri\n    54\u2192     * @throws Exception  is thrown if error creating the endpoint\n    55\u2192     * @see               #useRawUri()\n    56\u2192     */\n    57\u2192    Endpoint createEndpoint(String uri, Map<String, Object> parameters) throws Exception;\n the parameter values is raw as well.\n    63\u2192     *\n    64\u2192     * @return <tt>true</tt> to use raw uris, <tt>false</tt> to use encoded uris (default).\n    65\u2192     */\n    66\u2192 <tt>null</tt> if the component does not support using property configurer.\n    72\u2192     */\n    73\u2192    default PropertyConfigurer getComponentPropertyConfigurer() {\n    74\u2192        return null;\n    75\u2192    }\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192    /**\n    78\u2192     * Gets the endpoint {@link PropertyConfigurer}.\n    79\u2192     *\n    80\u2192     * @return the configurer, or <tt>null</tt> if the endpoint does not support using property configurer.\n    81\u2192     */\n    82\u2192    default PropertyConfigurer getEndpointPropertyConfigurer() {\n    83\u2192        return null;\n    84\u2192    }\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192    /**\n    87\u2192     * Gets a list of supported extensions.\n    88\u2192     *\n    89\u2192     * @return the list of extensions.\n Optional.empty();\n   103\u2192    }\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192    /**\n   106\u2192     * Set the {@link Component} context if the component is an instance of {@link ComponentAware}.\n   107\u2192
