componentAware.setComponent(component);\n   111\u2192        }\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192        return object;\n   114\u2192    }\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192    /**\n   117\u2192     * Gets the default name of the component.\n   118\u2192     */\n   119\u2192    default String getDefaultName() {\n   120\u2192        return null;\n   121\u2192    }\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192    /**\n This is used for automatic autowiring options (the option must be marked as\n   125\u2192     * autowired) by looking up in the registry to find if there is a single instance of matching type, which then gets\n   126\u2192     * configured on the component. This can be used for automatic configuring JDBC data sources, JMS connection\n   127\u2192     * factories, AWS Clients, etc.\n   128\u2192     */\n   129\u2192    default boolean isAutowiredEnabled() {\n   130\u2192        return true;\n   131\u2192    }\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192}\n   134\u2192"

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     3\u2192 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\n     4\u2192 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n     5\u2192 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n     6\u2192 * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n     7\u2192 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     8\u2192 *\n     9\u2192 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    10\u2192 *\n    11\u2192 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    12\u2192 * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192package org.apache.camel;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import java.util.Map;\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192import org.apache.camel.support.service.ServiceSupport;\n    22\u2192import org.apache.camel.util.StringHelper;\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192/**\n    25\u2192 * An <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/endpoint.html\">endpoint</a> implements the\n    26\u2192 * <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/message-endpoint.html\">Message Endpoint</a> pattern and represents an endpoint that\n    27\u2192 * can send and receive message exchanges\n    28\u2192 *\n    29\u2192 * @see Exchange\n    30\u2192 * @see Message\n    31\u2192 */\n    32\u2192public interface Endpoint extends IsSingleton, Service, ComponentAware {\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192    /**\n    35\u2192     * Returns the string representation of the endpoint URI\n    36\u2192     *\n    37\u2192     * @return the endpoint URI\n    38\u2192     */\n    39\u2192    String getEndpointUri();\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192    /**\n    42\u2192     * Returns the default exchange pattern to use when creating an exchange.\n    43\u2192     */\n    44\u2192    ExchangePattern getExchangePattern();\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192    /**\n    47\u2192     * Returns the string representation of the base endpoint URI (without query parameters).\n    48\u2192     */\n    49\u2192    default String getEndpointBaseUri() {\n    50\u2192        String value = getEndpointUri();\n    51\u2192        return StringHelper.before(value, \"?\", value);\n    52\u2192    }\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192    /**\n    55\u2192     * Returns a string key of this endpoint.\n    56\u2192     * <p/>\n    57\u2192     * This key is used by {@link org.apache.camel.spi.LifecycleStrategy} when registering endpoint. This allows to\n    58\u2192     * register different instances of endpoints with the same key.\n    59\u2192     * <p/>\n    60\u2192     * For JMX mbeans this allows us to use the same JMX Mbean for all endpoints that are logical the same but have\n    61\u2192     * different parameters. For instance the http endpoint.\n    62\u2192     *\n    63\u2192     * @return the endpoint key\n    64\u2192     */\n    65\u2192    String getEndpointKey();\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192    /**\n    68\u2192     * Create a new exchange for communicating with this endpoint.\n    69\u2192     * <p/>\n    70\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> Consumers should use {@link Consumer#createExchange(boolean)} to create an exchange for which\n    71\u2192     * the consumer received a message.\n    72\u2192     *\n    73\u2192     * @return a new exchange\n    74\u2192     */\n    75\u2192    Exchange createExchange();\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192    /**\n    78\u2192     * Create a new exchange for communicating with this endpoint with the specified {@link ExchangePattern} such as\n    79\u2192     * whether its going to be an {@link ExchangePattern#InOnly} or {@link ExchangePattern#InOut} exchange\n    80\u2192     * <p/>\n    81\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> Consumers should use {@link Consumer#createExchange(boolean)} to create an exchange for which\n    82\u2192     * the consumer received a message.\n    83\u2192     *\n    84\u2192     * @param  pattern the message exchange pattern for the exchange\n    85\u2192     * @return         a new exchange\n    86\u2192     */\n    87\u2192    Exchange createExchange(ExchangePattern pattern);\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192    /**\n    90\u2192     * Configures a newly created {@link Exchange}.\n    91\u2192     *\n    92\u2192     * @param exchange the new exchange\n    93\u2192     */\n    94\u2192    void configureExchange(Exchange exchange);\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192    /**\n    97\u2192     * Returns the context which created the endpoint\n    98\u2192     *\n    99\u2192     * @return the context which created the endpoint\n   100\u2192     */\n   101\u2192    CamelContext getCamelContext();\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192    /**\n   104\u2192     * Creates a new producer which is used send messages into the endpoint.\n   105\u2192     *\n   106\u2192     * Important: Do not do any initialization in the constructor of the {@link Producer}. Instead use\n   107\u2192     * {@link ServiceSupport#doInit()} or {@link ServiceSupport#doStart()}.\n   108\u2192     *\n   109\u2192     * @return           a newly created producer\n   110\u2192     * @throws Exception can be thrown\n   111\u2192     */\n   112\u2192    Producer createProducer() throws Exception;\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192    /**\n   115\u2192     * Whether this endpoint creates singleton producers\n   116\u2192     */\n   117\u2192    default boolean isSingletonProducer() {\n   118\u2192        return isSingleton();\n   119\u2192    }\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192    /**\n   122\u2192     * Creates a new producer which is used send messages into the endpoint\n   123\u2192     *\n   124\u2192     * Important: Do not do any initialization in the constructor of the {@link Producer}. Instead use\n   125\u2192     * {@link ServiceSupport#doInit()} or {@link ServiceSupport#doStart()}.\n   126\u2192     *\n   127\u2192     * @return           a newly created producer\n   128\u2192     * @throws Exception can be thrown\n   129\u2192     */\n   130\u2192    AsyncProducer createAsyncProducer() throws Exception;\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192    /**\n   133\u2192     * Creates a new <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/event-driven-consumer.html\">Event Driven Consumer</a> which\n   134\u2192     * consumes messages from the endpoint using the given processor\n   135\u2192     *\n   136\u2192     * Important: Do not do any initialization in the constructor of the {@link Consumer}. Instead use\n   137\u2192     * {@link ServiceSupport#doInit()} or {@link ServiceSupport#doStart()}.\n   138\u2192     *\n   139\u2192     * @param  processor the given processor\n   140\u2192     * @return           a newly created consumer\n   141\u2192     * @throws Exception can be thrown\n   142\u2192     */\n   143\u2192    Consumer createConsumer(Processor processor) throws Exception;\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192    /**\n   146\u2192     * Creates a new <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/polling-consumer.html\">Polling Consumer</a> so that the caller can\n   147\u2192     * poll message exchanges from the consumer using {@link PollingConsumer#receive()},\n   148\u2192     * {@link PollingConsumer#receiveNoWait()} or {@link PollingConsumer#receive(long)} whenever it is ready to do so\n   149\u2192     * rather than using the <a href=\"http://camel.apache.org/event-driven-consumer.html\">Event Based Consumer</a>\n   150\u2192     * returned by {@link #createConsumer(Processor)}"

You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     8\u2192 *\n     9\u2192 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    10\u2192 *\n    11\u2192 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    12\u2192 * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192package org.apache.camel;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.UnitOfWork;\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192/**\n    22\u2192 * A consumer of message exchanges from an {@link Endpoint}.\n    23\u2192 * <p/>\n    24\u2192 * Important: Do not do any initialization in the constructor. Instead use\n    25\u2192 * {@link org.apache.camel.support.service.ServiceSupport#doInit()} or\n    26\u2192 * {@link org.apache.camel.support.service.ServiceSupport#doStart()}.\n    27\u2192 */\n    28\u2192public interface Consumer extends Service, EndpointAware {\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192    /**\n    31\u2192     * The processor that will process the {@link Exchange} that was consumed.\n    32\u2192     */\n    33\u2192    Processor getProcessor();\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192    /**\n    36\u2192     * Creates an {@link Exchange} that was consumed.\n    37\u2192     * <p/>\n    38\u2192     * <b>Important:</b> If the auto release parameter is set to <tt>false</tt> then the consumer is responsible for\n    39\u2192     * calling the {@link #releaseExchange(Exchange, boolean)} when the {@link Exchange} is done being routed. This is\n    40\u2192     * for advanced consumers that need to have this control in their own hands. For normal use-cases then a consumer\n    41\u2192     * can use autoRelease <tt>true</tt> and then Camel will automatic release the exchange after routing.\n    42\u2192     *\n    43\u2192     * @param autoRelease whether to auto release the exchange when routing is complete via {@link UnitOfWork}\n    44\u2192     */\n    45\u2192    Exchange createExchange(boolean autoRelease);\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192    /**\n    48\u2192     * Releases the {@link Exchange} when its completed processing and no longer needed.\n    49\u2192     *\n    50\u2192     * @param exchange    the exchange\n    51\u2192     * @param autoRelease whether the exchange was created with auto release\n    52\u2192     */\n    53\u2192    void releaseExchange(Exchange exchange, boolean autoRelease);\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192    /**\n    56\u2192     * The default callback to use with the consumer when calling the processor using asynchronous routing.\n    57\u2192     *\n    58\u2192     * This implementation will use {@link org.apache.camel.spi.ExceptionHandler} to handle any exception on the\n    59\u2192     * exchange and afterwards release the exchange.\n    60\u2192     *\n    61\u2192     * @param  exchange    the exchange\n    62\u2192     * @param  autoRelease whether the exchange was created with auto release\n    63\u2192     * @return             the default callback\n    64\u2192     */\n    65\u2192    default AsyncCallback defaultConsumerCallback(Exchange exchange, boolean autoRelease) {\n    66\u2192        return null;\n    67\u2192    }\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192}\n    70\u2192"

Input
{
  "description": "Explore RouteReifier bridge",
  "prompt": "Explore the RouteReifier and route reification process in /workspace. This bridges the DSL model to runtime.\n\nFind and read thoroughly:\n\n1. `RouteReifier` - likely in core/camel-core-reifier. This converts RouteDefinition to a runtime Route.\n2. `RouteDefinition` - the model/DSL representation of a route, likely in core/camel-core-model\n3. `ProcessorReifier` - base class for reifying processor definitions into runtime processors\n4. `PipelineReifier` - reifies pipeline definitions\n5. `DefaultRoute` or `Route` implementation - the runtime route object\n6. `RouteController` - manages route lifecycle\n\nFor each, I need:\n- Exact file path\n- How RouteReifier.createRoute() works\n- How it creates the processor chain from the definition\n- How the Channel wiring happens during reification\n- How error handlers and interceptors get wired in\n- The relationship between RouteDefinition model nodes and runtime processors\n\nAlso look for `Reifier` base class and how the reifier pattern works.\n\nBe very thorough - read full files where possible.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a579c2f",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary document of the route reification process.\n\n## Route Reification Deep Dive Summary\n\nBased on my thorough exploration of the Apache Camel codebase, here is a comprehensive analysis of the route reification process:\n\n### Key Files and their Locations\n\n1. **RouteReifier** - `/workspace/core/camel-core-reifier/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/reifier/RouteReifier.java`\n2. **RouteDefinition** - `/workspace/core/camel-core-model/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/model/RouteDefinition.java`\n3. **ProcessorReifier** (base class) - `/workspace/core/camel-core-reifier/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/reifier/ProcessorReifier.java`\n4. **AbstractReifier** (base class) - `/workspace/core/camel-core-reifier/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/reifier/AbstractReifier.java`\n5. **PipelineReifier** - `/workspace/core/camel-core-reifier/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/reifier/PipelineReifier.java`\n6. **DefaultRoute** (runtime) - `/workspace/core/camel-base-engine/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/impl/engine/DefaultRoute.java`\n7. **Route** (interface) - `/workspace/core/camel-api/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/Route.java`\n8. **DefaultChannel** - `/workspace/core/camel-base-engine/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/impl/engine/DefaultChannel.java`\n9. **Channel** (interface) - `/workspace/core/camel-api/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/Channel.java`\n10. **RoutePipeline** - `/workspace/core/camel-core-processor/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/processor/RoutePipeline.java`\n11. **DefaultRouteController** - `/workspace/core/camel-base-engine/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/impl/engine/DefaultRouteController.java`\n\n---\n\n## How RouteReifier.createRoute() Works\n\n### Entry Point (Lines 87-98)\n```java\npublic Route createRoute() {\n    try {\n        return doCreateRoute();\n    } catch (FailedToCreateRouteException e) {\n        throw e;\n    } catch (Exception e) {\n        // wrap and prepare source location\n        throw new FailedToCreateRouteException(...)\n    }\n}\n```\n\nThe `createRoute()` method is the main entry point that wraps `doCreateRoute()` with error handling and source location tracking.\n\n### Phase 1: Route Creation and Configuration (Lines 102-226)\n\n**1. Endpoint Resolution (Lines 103-112)**\n- Gets the input endpoint from the route definition's input\n- Can be resolved from either:\n  - Already-set endpoint object\n  - EndpointConsumerBuilder\n  - Endpoint URI string\n\n**2. Route Object Creation (Lines 114-120)**\n```java\nRoute route = PluginHelper.getRouteFactory(camelContext).createRoute(\n    camelContext, definition, id, desc, note, endpoint, definition.getResource());\n```\nCreates a `DefaultRoute` instance through the RouteFactory plugin.\n\n**3. Route Configuration (Lines 122-227)**\nSets various runtime properties on the Route:\n- Error handler factory\n- Variable receive mode (for capturing input)\n- Tracing, message history, log mask settings\n- Stream caching and delayer configuration\n- Auto startup and startup order\n- Shutdown behavior (ShutdownRoute and ShutdownRunningTask)\n- Intercept strategies\n- Route policies (configured or from factories)\n\n**4. Output Validation (Lines 216-226)**\nVerifies the route has at least one output processor. Throws exception if route definition has no outputs.\n\n### Phase 2: Processor Chain Creation (Lines 228-250)\n\n**Key Loop - Recursively Reify Output Processors:**\n```java\nList<ProcessorDefinition<?>> list = new ArrayList<>(definition.getOutputs());\nfor (ProcessorDefinition<?> output : list) {\n    ProcessorReifier<?> reifier = ProcessorReifier.reifier(route, output);\n    reifier.addRoutes();  // Recursively process outputs\n}\n```\n\n**ProcessorReifier.reifier() Factory Pattern (Lines 184-202):**\n- Checks custom reifier registry first\n- Falls back to core reifier creation via `coreReifier()` factory method\n- Maps each ProcessorDefinition subclass to its corresponding reifier:\n  - `ToDefinition` \u2192 `SendReifier`\n  - `FilterDefinition` \u2192 `FilterReifier`\n  - `ChoiceDefinition` \u2192 `ChoiceReifier`\n  - `MulticastDefinition` \u2192 `MulticastReifier`\n  - `PipelineDefinition` \u2192 `PipelineReifier`\n  - etc. (50+ mappings)\n\n### Phase 3: Event-Driven Processor Assembly (Lines 252-268)\n\n**Building the Final Processor Chain:**\n```java\nList<Processor> eventDrivenProcessors = route.getEventDrivenProcessors();\nif (eventDrivenProcessors.isEmpty()) {\n    return null;\n}\n\n// Create RoutePipeline wrapping all event-driven processors\nRoutePipeline target = new RoutePipeline(camelContext, eventDrivenProcessors);\ntarget.setRouteId(id);\n\n// Wrap in unit of work processor\nInternalProcessor internal = PluginHelper.getInternalProcessorFactory(camelContext)\n    .addUnitOfWorkProcessorAdvice(camelContext, target, route);\n```\n\n**Key Points:**\n- `eventDrivenProcessors` is populated by `reifier.addRoutes()` calls (lines 231-250)\n- Each reifier converts a ProcessorDefinition to a runtime Processor\n- The RoutePipeline is a composite processor that chains all processors together\n- Wrapped in InternalProcessor with UnitOfWork advice for transactional boundaries\n\n### Phase 4: Route Policy Configuration (Lines 270-315)\n\n**Policy Lookup and Registration:**\n1. Route-specific policies from `definition.getRoutePolicies()`\n2. Policies from `definition.getRoutePolicyRef()` references\n3. Policies from context factories (`camelContext.getRoutePolicyFactories()`)\n\n**Policy Integration:**\n```java\nfor (RoutePolicy policy : routePolicyList) {\n    if (policy instanceof RouteAware ra) {\n        ra.setRoute(route);\n    }\n    camelContext.addService(policy);\n}\ninternal.addRoutePolicyAdvice(routePolicyList);\n```\n\n### Phase 5: Wrapping with Cross-Cutting Concerns (Lines 316-365)\n\nThe internal processor is wrapped with multiple advice layers:\n\n1. **Route Inflight Repository Advice** (Line 318)\n   - Tracks number of inflight exchanges per route\n\n2. **Management Intercept Strategy** (Lines 321-324)\n   - JMX instrumentation for performance statistics\n\n3. **Route Lifecycle Advice** (Line 327)\n   - Manages route start/stop lifecycle hooks\n\n4. **Rest Binding Advice** (Lines 330-342)\n   - Applied only if rest binding definition exists\n\n5. **Contract Advice** (Lines 344-359)\n   - Input/output type validation and transformation\n   - Enables data type feature when contract types specified\n\n6. **Variable Advice** (Lines 361-364)\n   - Moves message body to variable when using variableReceive mode\n\n### Phase 6: Final Assembly and Return (Lines 366-419)\n\n```java\nroute.setProcessor(internal);\nroute.getProperties().putAll(routeProperties);\nroute.setStartupOrder(startupOrder);\nroute.setAutoStartup(isAutoStartup);\ninternal.setRouteOnAdvices(route);\n```\n\n**Error Handler Injection:**\n```java\nErrorHandlerFactory builder = route.getErrorHandlerFactory();\nProcessor errorHandler = ((ModelCamelContext) camelContext).getModelReifierFactory()\n    .createErrorHandler(route, builder, null);\nprepareErrorHandlerAware(route, errorHandler);\n```\n- Creates the error handler\n- Injects it into ErrorHandlerAware processors\n\n**Cleanup and Services:**\n- Route model is cleared to free memory during startup (via bootstrap callback)\n- Route template context services are registered for lifecycle management\n\n---\n\n## How the Processor Chain is Created from Definition\n\n### ProcessorReifier.createOutputsProcessor() Flow (Lines 762-804)\n\n**Recursive Processing:**\n```java\nprotected Processor createOutputsProcessor(Collection<ProcessorDefinition<?>> outputs, boolean optimize) throws Exception {\n    List<Processor> list = new ArrayList<>();\n    for (ProcessorDefinition<?> output : outputs) {\n        // 1. Allow pre-processing\n        reifier(route, output).preCreateProcessor();\n        \n        // 2. Create the processor\n        Processor processor = createProcessor(output);\n        \n        // 3. Set ID and route ID\n        if (processor instanceof IdAware idAware) {\n            idAware.setId(getId(output));\n        }\n        if (processor instanceof RouteIdAware routeIdAware) {\n            routeIdAware.setRouteId(route.getRouteId());\n        }\n        \n        // 4. Wrap in Channel with error handlers and interceptors\n        Processor channel = wrapChannel(processor, output);\n        list.add(channel);\n    }\n    \n    // 5. Create composite if multiple, or return single\n    if (optimize && list.size() == 1) {\n        return list.get(0);\n    } else {\n        return createCompositeProcessor(list);  // Returns Pipeline\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### ProcessorReifier.createProcessor() (Lines 806-829)\n\n**Delegation Pattern:**\n```java\nprotected Processor createProcessor(ProcessorDefinition<?> output) throws Exception {\n    String outputId = output.idOrCreate(...);\n    \n    // First try custom ProcessorFactory\n    final ProcessorFactory processorFactory = PluginHelper.getProcessorFactory(camelContext);\n    if (processorFactory != null) {\n        processor = processorFactory.createProcessor(route, output);\n    }\n    \n    // Fallback to reifier\n    if (processor == null) {\n        processor = reifier(route, output).createProcessor();\n    }\n    return processor;\n}\n```\n\n**Key Design:**\n- Custom processor factories can override default behavior\n- Falls back to the specific reifier's `createProcessor()` implementation\n- Each reifier (e.g., SendReifier, FilterReifier) implements domain-specific logic\n\n---\n\n## How Channel Wiring Happens During Reification\n\n### ProcessorReifier.wrapChannel() (Lines 650-712)\n\n**Channel Creation and Initialization:**\n```java\nprotected Channel wrapChannel(Processor processor, ProcessorDefinition<?> child, Boolean inheritErrorHandler)\n        throws Exception {\n    // 1. Create channel\n    Channel channel = PluginHelper.getInternalProcessorFactory(camelContext).createChannel(camelContext);\n    \n    // 2. Gather interceptors (in order: context, route, local)\n    List<InterceptStrategy> interceptors = new ArrayList<>();\n    interceptors.addAll(camelContext.getCamelContextExtension().getInterceptStrategies());\n    interceptors.addAll(route.getInterceptStrategies());\n    interceptors.addAll(definition.getInterceptStrategies());\n    \n    // 3. Force ID assignment on definitions\n    RouteDefinitionHelper.forceAssignIds(camelContext, definition);\n    \n    // 4. Fix parent/child relationships\n    if (child != null && definition != child) {\n        child.setParent(definition);\n    }\n    \n    // 5. Initialize channel\n    channel.initChannel(this.route, definition, child, interceptors, processor, route, first);\n    \n    // 6. Wrap in error handler if needed\n    boolean wrap = ProcessorDefinitionHelper.shouldWrapInErrorHandler(camelContext, definition, child, inheritErrorHandler);\n    if (wrap) {\n        wrapChannelInErrorHandler(channel, inheritErrorHandler);\n    }\n    \n    // 7. Post-init processing\n    channel.postInitChannel();\n    return channel;\n}\n```\n\n### DefaultChannel.initChannel() Flow (Lines 149-271)\n\n**1. Store Route and Next Processor**\n```java\nthis.route = route;\nthis.nextProcessor = nextProcessor;\nCamelContextAware.trySetCamelContext(nextProcessor, camelContext);\n```\n\n**2. Setup Management Instrumentation**\n```java\nManagementInterceptStrategy managed = route.getManagementInterceptStrategy();\nif (managed != null) {\n    instrumentationProcessor = managed.createProcessor(targetOutputDef, nextProcessor);\n}\n```\n\n**3. Wire Debugging and Tracing**\n- Debugger advice (if enabled)\n- Backlog debugger advice (for breakpoints)\n- Backlog tracer advice (for message tracing)\n- Message history advice (for route context tracking)\n\n**4. Apply Interceptor Strategies (Lines 231-259)**\n```java\nCollections.reverse(interceptors);  // Reverse so first is wrapped last\nProcessor target = nextProcessor;\nfor (InterceptStrategy strategy : interceptors) {\n    Processor wrapped = strategy.wrapProcessorInInterceptors(\n        route.getCamelContext(), targetOutputDef, target, next);\n    target = wrapped;\n}\n```\n\n**5. Apply Stream Caching and Delayer Advice** (Lines 261-267)\n- Caches stream-based message bodies if enabled\n- Applies configurable delay between processing steps\n\n**6. Set Output**\n```java\noutput = target;  // The wrapped processor chain\n```\n\n### DefaultChannel.postInitChannel() (Lines 273-294)\n\n**JMX Instrumentation Setup:**\n- If redelivery is possible, wraps the output in instrumentation processor for metrics\n- Otherwise, uses advice mechanism for less overhead\n\n---\n\n## How Error Handlers and Interceptors Get Wired In\n\n### Error Handler Factory Creation (Lines 722-731)\n\n**Error Handler Wrapping:**\n```java\nprotected Processor wrapInErrorHandler(Processor output) throws Exception {\n    ErrorHandlerFactory builder = route.getErrorHandlerFactory();\n    \n    Processor errorHandler = ((ModelCamelContext) camelContext).getModelReifierFactory()\n        .createErrorHandler(route, builder, output);\n    \n    if (output instanceof ErrorHandlerAware errorHandlerAware) {\n        errorHandlerAware.setErrorHandler(errorHandler);\n    }\n    return errorHandler;\n}\n```\n\n**Key Pattern:**\n- The error handler wraps the next processor\n- Provides exception handling, redelivery, dead letter routing\n- If processor is ErrorHandlerAware, it's notified of its error handler\n\n### Route-Level Error Handler Injection (Lines 388-391 in RouteReifier)\n\n```java\nErrorHandlerFactory builder = route.getErrorHandlerFactory();\nProcessor errorHandler = ((ModelCamelContext) camelContext).getModelReifierFactory()\n    .createErrorHandler(route, builder, null);\nprepareErrorHandlerAware(route, errorHandler);\n```\n\n**prepareErrorHandlerAware()** (Lines 422-429):\n```java\nprivate void prepareErrorHandlerAware(Route route, Processor errorHandler) {\n    List<Processor> processors = route.filter(\"*\");  // Get all processors\n    for (Processor p : processors) {\n        if (p instanceof ErrorHandlerAware errorHandlerAware) {\n            errorHandlerAware.setErrorHandler(errorHandler);\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\nThis ensures all processors in the route are aware of the error handler for fine-grained control.\n\n### Interceptor Wiring Flow\n\n**Three Levels of Interceptors (ProcessorReifier line 662-665):**\n```java\nList<InterceptStrategy> interceptors = new ArrayList<>();\ninterceptors.addAll(camelContext.getCamelContextExtension().getInterceptStrategies());  // Global\ninterceptors.addAll(route.getInterceptStrategies());                                     // Route-scoped\ninterceptors.addAll(definition.getInterceptStrategies());                                // Node-scoped\n```\n\n**Wrapping Order (DefaultChannel lines 232-234):**\n- Interceptors are sorted by `@Ordered` annotation\n- Reversed so first interceptor wraps last (becomes innermost, closest to processor)\n- Each wraps the previous result: `strategy.wrapProcessorInInterceptors(...)`\n\n**Result Chain Structure:**\n```\nOutput \n  \u2193 (wrapped by Interceptor1)\nInterceptor1\n  \u2193 (wrapped by Interceptor2)\nInterceptor2\n  \u2193 (wrapped by Interceptor3)\nInterceptor3\n  \u2193\nNextProcessor\n```\n\n---\n\n## RouteDefinition Model to Runtime Processor Relationship\n\n### RouteDefinition Structure\n- **input**: `FromDefinition` - defines the consumer endpoint (from(\"uri\"))\n- **outputs**: `List<ProcessorDefinition<?>>` - chain of EIP definitions (to, filter, choice, etc.)\n- **errorHandler**: Error handling configuration\n- **routePolicies**: Route lifecycle policies\n- **interceptStrategies**: Intercept strategies\n- **properties**: Route metadata (id, group, description, etc.)\n\n### Mapping During Reification\n\n```\nRouteDefinition\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 input (FromDefinition)\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 \u2192 resolves to Endpoint\n\u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 \u2192 creates Consumer when route starts\n\u2502\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 outputs (List of ProcessorDefinition)\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 ProcessorDefinition[0] (e.g., ToDefinition)\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2192 reifier creates Processor\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2192 wrapped in Channel with interceptors/error handlers\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2192 added to eventDrivenProcessors\n\u2502   \u2502\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 ProcessorDefinition[1] (e.g., FilterDefinition)\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2192 reifier creates Processor\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2192 children recursively processed\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 wrapped in Channel\n\u2502   \u2502\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 ... (continues)\n\u2502\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 Route (runtime)\n    \u251c\u2500\u2500 processor = RoutePipeline\n    \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 eventDrivenProcessors (all wrapped in Channels)\n    \u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 wrapped in InternalProcessor with UnitOfWork advice\n    \u2502\n    \u251c\u2500\u2500 routePolicies\n    \u251c\u2500\u2500 errorHandlerFactory\n    \u2514\u2500\u2500 properties\n```\n\n### Key Insight: Two-Phase Design\n\n1. **Design Time** (RouteDefinition)\n   - Hierarchical model of the route structure\n   - Can be introspected, modified, templated\n\n2. **Reification Time** (RouteReifier.doCreateRoute)\n   - Flattens the hierarchy into eventDrivenProcessors list\n   - Creates runtime Processors from definitions\n   - Wires in error handlers, interceptors, channels\n   - Sets up lifecycle management\n\n3. **Runtime** (Route + Processors)\n   - Consumer receives messages from endpoint\n   - Passes to RoutePipeline.process()\n   - Each Processor in chain wraps next processor in Channel\n   - Channel provides error handling, interceptors, tracing\n\n---\n\n## RouteController Role\n\n### DefaultRouteController (Facade Pattern)\n\nThe `DefaultRouteController` delegates to `InternalRouteController`:\n\n```java\nprotected RouteController getInternalRouteController() {\n    return camelContext.getCamelContextExtension().getInternalRouteController();\n}\n\n@Override\npublic void startRoute(String routeId) throws Exception {\n    getInternalRouteController().startRoute(routeId);\n}\n\n@Override\npublic void stopRoute(String routeId) throws Exception {\n    getInternalRouteController().stopRoute(routeId);\n}\n```\n\n**Responsibilities:**\n- Route lifecycle management (start/stop/suspend/resume)\n- Route status queries\n- Route group operations\n- Route reloading\n- Integration with SupervisingRouteController for fault tolerance\n\n---\n\n## Summary: Complete Flow\n\n```\n1. RouteDefinition (XML/Java DSL) \u2192 Model representation\n   \n2. RouteReifier.createRoute() called\n   \u251c\u2500 Create Route object (DefaultRoute)\n   \u251c\u2500 Resolve input endpoint\n   \u251c\u2500 Configure route properties\n   \u251c\u2500 For each output definition:\n   \u2502  \u2514\u2500 Get reifier (ProcessorReifier subclass)\n   \u2502     \u2514\u2500 Create Processor\n   \u2502     \u2514\u2500 Wrap in Channel\n   \u2502        \u251c\u2500 Apply interceptors (3 levels)\n   \u2502        \u251c\u2500 Apply error handler\n   \u2502        \u2514\u2500 Wire debugger/tracer/management\n   \u2502     \u2514\u2500 Add to eventDrivenProcessors\n   \u251c\u2500 Create RoutePipeline from eventDrivenProcessors\n   \u251c\u2500 Wrap in InternalProcessor with UnitOfWork advice\n   \u251c\u2500 Add cross-cutting concerns (policies, contracts, etc.)\n   \u251c\u2500 Inject error handler into ErrorHandlerAware processors\n   \u2514\u2500 Return Route\n   \n3. Route.start() when CamelContext starts\n   \u251c\u2500 Create Consumer from input endpoint\n   \u251c\u2500 Consumer receives messages\n   \u2514\u2500 Passes to Route.processor (RoutePipeline)\n   \n4. Message flows through processor chain\n   \u251c\u2500 RoutePipeline routes to first Processor\n   \u251c\u2500 Each Channel:\n   \u2502  \u251c\u2500 Applies interceptors\n   \u2502  \u251c\u2500 Applies error handling\n   \u2502  \u2514\u2500 Routes to nextProcessor\n   \u2514\u2500 Final processor sends to output endpoint(s)\n   \n5. RouteController manages lifecycle\n   \u251c\u2500 Start/stop routes\n   \u251c\u2500 Monitor health\n   \u2514\u2500 Integrate with supervision\n```\n\nThis architecture provides a clean separation between the declarative model (RouteDefinition) and the executable runtime (Route + Processors), with the Reifier pattern bridging the two through code generation-like transformation.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Explore the RouteReifier and route reification process in /workspace. This bridges the DSL model to runtime.\n\nFind and read thoroughly:\n\n1. `RouteReifier` - likely in core/camel-core-reifier. This converts RouteDefinition to a runtime Route.\n2. `RouteDefinition` - the model/DSL representation of a route, likely in core/camel-core-model\n3. `ProcessorReifier` - base class for reifying processor definitions into runtime processors\n4. `PipelineReifier` - reifies pipeline definitions\n5. `DefaultRoute` or `Route` implementation - the runtime route object\n6. `RouteController` - manages route lifecycle\n\nFor each, I need:\n- Exact file path\n- How RouteReifier.createRoute() works\n- How it creates the processor chain from the definition\n- How the Channel wiring happens during reification\n- How error handlers and interceptors get wired in\n- The relationship between RouteDefinition model nodes and runtime processors\n\nAlso look for `Reifier` base class and how the reifier pattern works.\n\nBe very thorough - read full files where possible.",
  "status": "completed",
  "totalDurationMs": 87989,
  "totalTokens": 87996,
  "totalToolUseCount": 26,
  "usage": {
    "cache_creation": {
      "ephemeral_1h_input_tokens": 2743,
      "ephemeral_5m_input_tokens": 0
    },
    "cache_creation_input_tokens": 2743,
    "cache_read_input_tokens": 79816,
    "inference_geo": "",
    "input_tokens": 1,
    "iterations": [],
    "output_tokens": 5436,
    "server_tool_use": {
      "web_fetch_requests": 0,
      "web_search_requests": 0
    },
    "service_tier": "standard",
    "speed": "standard"
  }
}

You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     8\u2192 *\n     9\u2192 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    10\u2192 *\n    11\u2192 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    12\u2192 * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192package org.apache.camel;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture;\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192/**\n    22\u2192 * An <b>asynchronous</b> processor which can process an {@link Exchange} in an asynchronous fashion and signal\n    23\u2192 * completion by invoking the {@link AsyncCallback}.\n    24\u2192 * <p/>\n    25\u2192 * Any processor can be coerced to have an {@link AsyncProcessor} interface by using the\n    26\u2192 * {@link org.apache.camel.support.AsyncProcessorConverterHelper#convert AsyncProcessorConverterHelper.convert} method.\n    27\u2192 */\n    28\u2192public interface AsyncProcessor extends Processor {\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192    /**\n    31\u2192     * Processes the message exchange. Similar to {@link Processor#process}, but the caller supports having the exchange\n    32\u2192     * asynchronously processed.\n    33\u2192     * <p/>\n    34\u2192     * If there was a failure processing then the caused {@link Exception} would be set on the {@link Exchange}.\n    35\u2192     *\n    36\u2192     * @param  exchange the message exchange\n    37\u2192     * @param  callback the {@link AsyncCallback} will be invoked when the processing of the exchange is completed. If\n    38\u2192     *                  the exchange is completed synchronously, then the callback is also invoked synchronously. The\n    39\u2192     *                  callback should therefore be careful of starting recursive loop.\n    40\u2192     * @return          (doneSync) <tt>true</tt> to continue execute synchronously, <tt>false</tt> to continue being\n    41\u2192     *                  executed asynchronously\n    42\u2192     */\n    43\u2192    boolean process(Exchange exchange, AsyncCallback callback);\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192    CompletableFuture<Exchange> processAsync(Exchange exchange);\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192}\n    48\u2192"

You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     8\u2192 *\n     9\u2192 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    10\u2192 *\n    11\u2192 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    12\u2192 * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192package org.apache.camel;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import java.util.List;\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.ErrorHandler;\n    22\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.InterceptStrategy;\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192/**\n    25\u2192 * Channel acts as a channel between {@link Processor}s in the route graph.\n    26\u2192 * <p/>\n    27\u2192 * The channel is responsible for routing the {@link Exchange} to the next {@link Processor} in the route graph.\n    28\u2192 */\n    29\u2192public interface Channel extends AsyncProcessor, Navigate<Processor> {\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192    /**\n    32\u2192     * Initializes the channel. If the initialized output definition contained outputs (children) then the\n    33\u2192     * childDefinition will be set so we can leverage fine grained tracing\n    34\u2192     */\n    35\u2192    void initChannel(\n    36\u2192            Route route,\n    37\u2192            NamedNode definition,\n    38\u2192            NamedNode childDefinition,\n    39\u2192            List<InterceptStrategy> interceptors,\n    40\u2192            Processor nextProcessor,\n    41\u2192            NamedRoute routeDefinition,\n    42\u2192            boolean first)\n    43\u2192            throws Exception;\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192    /**\n    46\u2192     * Post initializes the channel.\n    47\u2192     *\n    48\u2192     * @throws Exception is thrown if some error occurred\n    49\u2192     */\n    50\u2192    void postInitChannel() throws Exception;\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192    /**\n    53\u2192     * Gets the {@link ErrorHandler} this Channel uses.\n    54\u2192     *\n    55\u2192     * @return the error handler, or <tt>null</tt> if no error handler is used.\n    56\u2192     */\n    57\u2192    Processor getErrorHandler();\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192    /**\n    60\u2192     * Sets the {@link ErrorHandler} this Channel uses.\n    61\u2192     *\n    62\u2192     * @param errorHandler the error handler\n    63\u2192     */\n    64\u2192    void setErrorHandler(Processor errorHandler);\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192    /**\n    67\u2192     * Gets the wrapped output that at runtime should be delegated to.\n    68\u2192     *\n    69\u2192     * @return the output to route the {@link Exchange} to\n    70\u2192     */\n    71\u2192    Processor getOutput();\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192    /**\n    74\u2192     * Gets the next {@link Processor} to route to (not wrapped)\n    75\u2192     *\n    76\u2192     * @return the next processor\n    77\u2192     */\n    78\u2192    Processor getNextProcessor();\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192    /**\n    81\u2192     * Gets the {@link Route}\n    82\u2192     *\n    83\u2192     * @return the route context\n    84\u2192     */\n    85\u2192    Route getRoute();\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192}\n "     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     3\u2192 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\n     4\u2192 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n     5\u2192 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n     6\u2192 * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n     7\u2192 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     8\u2192 *\n     9\u2192 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    10\u2192 *\n    11\u2192 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    12\u2192 * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192package org.apache.camel;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import java.util.List;\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192/**\n    22\u2192 * Implementations support navigating a graph where you can traverse forward and each next returns a {@link List} of\n    23\u2192 * outputs of type <tt>T</tt> that can contain <tt>0..n</tt> nodes.\n    24\u2192 */\n    25\u2192public interface Navigate<T> {\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192    /**\n    28\u2192     * Next group of outputs\n    29\u2192     * <p/>\n    30\u2192     * Important only invoke this once, as this method do not carry state, and is not intended to be used in a while\n    31\u2192     * loop, but used by a if statement instead.\n    32\u2192     *\n    33\u2192     * @return next group or <tt>null</tt> if no more outputs\n    34\u2192     */\n    35\u2192    List<T> next();\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192    /**\n    38\u2192     * Are there more outputs?\n    39\u2192     * <p/>\n    40\u2192     * Important only invoke this once, as this method do not carry state, and is not intended to be used in a while\n    41\u2192     * loop, but used by a if statement instead.\n    42\u2192     *\n    43\u2192     * @return <tt>true</tt> if more outputs\n    44\u2192     */\n    45\u2192    boolean hasNext();\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192}\n    48\u2192"

"     1\u2192/*\n     2\u2192 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n     3\u2192 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with\n     4\u2192 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n     5\u2192 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n     6\u2192 * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n     7\u2192 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n     8\u2192 *\n     9\u2192 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n    10\u2192 *\n    11\u2192 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n    12\u2192 * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n    13\u2192 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n    14\u2192 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n    15\u2192 * limitations under the License.\n    16\u2192 */\n    17\u2192package org.apache.camel.impl.engine;\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192import java.util.ArrayList;\n    20\u2192import java.util.Collections;\n    21\u2192import java.util.List;\n    22\u2192import java.util.Map;\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192import org.apache.camel.AsyncProcessor;\n    25\u2192import org.apache.camel.CamelContext;\n    26\u2192import org.apache.camel.CamelContextAware;\n    27\u2192import org.apache.camel.Channel;\n    28\u2192import org.apache.camel.Exchange;\n    29\u2192import org.apache.camel.NamedNode;\n    30\u2192import org.apache.camel.NamedRoute;\n    31\u2192import org.apache.camel.Processor;\n    32\u2192import org.apache.camel.Route;\n    33\u2192import org.apache.camel.impl.debugger.BacklogTracer;\n    34\u2192import org.apache.camel.impl.debugger.DefaultBacklogDebugger;\n    35\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.BacklogDebugger;\n    36\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.Debugger;\n    37\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.ErrorHandlerRedeliveryCustomizer;\n    38\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.InterceptStrategy;\n    39\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.InterceptableProcessor;\n    40\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.ManagementInterceptStrategy;\n    41\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.MessageHistoryFactory;\n    42\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.Tracer;\n    43\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.WrapAwareProcessor;\n    44\u2192import org.apache.camel.support.OrderedComparator;\n    45\u2192import org.apache.camel.support.PatternHelper;\n    46\u2192import org.apache.camel.support.PluginHelper;\n    47\u2192import org.apache.camel.support.service.ServiceHelper;\n    48\u2192import org.apache.camel.util.StringHelper;\n    49\u2192import org.slf4j.Logger;\n    50\u2192import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192/**\n    53\u2192 * DefaultChannel is the default {@link Channel}.\n    54\u2192 * <p/>\n    55\u2192 * The current implementation is just a composite containing the interceptors and error handler that beforehand was\n    56\u2192 * added to the route graph directly. <br/>\n    57\u2192 * With this {@link Channel} we can in the future implement better strategies for routing the {@link Exchange} in the\n    58\u2192 * route graph, as we have a {@link Channel} between each and every node in the graph.\n    59\u2192 */\n    60\u2192public class DefaultChannel extends CamelInternalProcessor implements Channel {\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(DefaultChannel.class);\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192    private Processor errorHandler;\n    65\u2192    // the next processor (non wrapped)\n    66\u2192    private Processor nextProcessor;\n    67\u2192    // the real output to invoke that has been wrapped\n    68\u2192    private Processor output;\n    69\u2192    private ManagementInterceptStrategy.InstrumentationProcessor<?> instrumentationProcessor;\n    70\u2192    private Route route;\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192    public DefaultChannel(CamelContext camelContext) {\n    73\u2192        super(camelContext);\n    74\u2192    }\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192    @Override\n    77\u2192    public Processor getOutput() {\n    78\u2192        // the errorHandler is already decorated with interceptors\n    79\u2192        // so it contain the entire chain of processors, so we can safely use it directly as output\n    80\u2192        // if no error handler provided we use the output\n    81\u2192        // the error handlers, interceptors, etc. woven in at design time\n    82\u2192        return errorHandler != null ? errorHandler : output;\n    83\u2192    }\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192    @Override\n    86\u2192    public boolean hasNext() {\n    87\u2192        return nextProcessor != null;\n    88\u2192    }\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192    @Override\n    91\u2192    public List<Processor> next() {\n    92\u2192        if (!hasNext()) {\n    93\u2192            return null;\n    94\u2192        }\n    95\u2192        List<Processor> answer = new ArrayList<>(1);\n    96\u2192        answer.add(nextProcessor);\n    97\u2192        return answer;\n    98\u2192    }\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192    public void setOutput(Processor output) {\n   101\u2192        this.output = output;\n   102\u2192    }\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192    @Override\n   105\u2192    public Processor getNextProcessor() {\n   106\u2192        return nextProcessor;\n   107\u2192    }\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192    @Override\n   110\u2192    public void setErrorHandler(Processor errorHandler) {\n   111\u2192        this.errorHandler = errorHandler;\n   112\u2192    }\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192    @Override\n   115\u2192    public Processor getErrorHandler() {\n   116\u2192        return errorHandler;\n   117\u2192    }\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192    @Override\n   120\u2192    public Route getRoute() {\n   121\u2192        return route;\n   122\u2192    }\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192    @Override\n   125\u2192    protected void doStart() throws Exception {\n   126\u2192        // do not call super as we want to be in control here of the lifecycle\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192        // the output has now been created, so assign the output as the processor\n   129\u2192        setProcessor(getOutput());\n   130\u2192        ServiceHelper.startService(errorHandler, output);\n   131\u2192    }\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192    @Override\n   134\u2192    protected void doStop() throws Exception {\n   135\u2192        // do not call super as we want to be in control here of the lifecycle\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192        // only stop services if not context scoped (as context scoped is reused by others)\n   138\u2192        ServiceHelper.stopService(output, errorHandler);\n   139\u2192    }\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192    @Override\n   142\u2192    protected void doShutdown() throws Exception {\n   143\u2192        // do not call super as we want to be in control here of the lifecycle\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192        ServiceHelper.stopAndShutdownServices(output, errorHandler);\n   146\u2192    }\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192    @Override\n   149\u2192    public void initChannel(\n   150\u2192            Route route,\n   151\u2192            NamedNode definition,\n   152\u2192            NamedNode childDefinition,\n   153\u2192            List<InterceptStrategy> interceptors,\n   154\u2192            Processor nextProcessor,\n   155\u2192            NamedRoute routeDefinition,\n   156\u2192            boolean first)\n   157\u2192            throws Exception {\n   158\u2192        this.route = route;\n   159\u2192        this.nextProcessor = nextProcessor;\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192        // init CamelContextAware as early as possible on nextProcessor\n   162\u2192        CamelContextAware.trySetCamelContext(nextProcessor, camelContext);\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192        // the definition to wrap should be the fine grained,\n   165\u2192        // so if a child is set then use it, if not then its the original output used\n   166\u2192        NamedNode targetOutputDef = childDefinition != null ? childDefinition : definition;\n   167\u2192        LOG.trace(\"Initialize channel for target: {}\", targetOutputDef);\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192        // setup instrumentation processor for management (jmx)\n   170\u2192        // this is later used in postInitChannel as we need to setup the error handler later as well\n   171\u2192        ManagementInterceptStrategy managed = route.getManagementInterceptStrategy();\n   172\u2192        if (managed != null) {\n   173\u2192            instrumentationProcessor = managed.createProcessor(targetOutputDef, nextProcessor);\n   174\u2192        }\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192        // then wrap the output with the tracer and debugger (debugger first,\n   177\u2192        // as we do not want regular tracer to trace the debugger)\n   178\u2192        if (camelContext.isDebugStandby() || route.isDebugging()) {\n   179\u2192            final Debugger customDebugger = camelContext.getDebugger();\n   180\u2192            final MessageHistoryFactory messageHistoryFactory = camelContext.getMessageHistoryFactory();\n   181\u2192            if (customDebugger != null) {\n   182\u2192                // use custom debugger\n   183\u2192                addAdvice(new MessageHistoryAdvice(messageHistoryFactory, targetOutputDef));\n   184\u2192                addAdvice(new DebuggerAdvice(customDebugger, nextProcessor, targetOutputDef));\n   185\u2192            }\n   186\u2192            BacklogDebugger debugger = getBacklogDebugger(camelContext, customDebugger == null);\n   187\u2192            if (debugger != null) {\n   188\u2192                // use backlog debugger\n   189\u2192                if (!camelContext.hasService(debugger)) {\n   190\u2192                    camelContext.addService(debugger);\n   191\u2192                }\n   192\u2192                // skip debugging inside rest-dsl (just a tiny facade) or kamelets / route-templates\n   193\u2192                boolean skip = routeDefinition != null\n   194\u2192                        && (routeDefinition.isCreatedFromRest() || routeDefinition.isCreatedFromTemplate());\n   195\u2192                if (!skip && routeDefinition != null) {\n   196\u2192                    backlogDebuggerSetupInitialBreakpoints(definition, routeDefinition, first, debugger, targetOutputDef);\n   197\u2192                    if (first && debugger.isSingleStepIncludeStartEnd()) {\n   198\u2192                        // debugger captures message history, and we need to capture history of incoming\n   199\u2192                        addAdvice(\n   200\u2192                                new MessageHistoryAdvice(camelContext.getMessageHistoryFactory(), routeDefinition.getInput()));\n   201\u2192                        // add breakpoint on route input instead of first node\n   202\u2192                        addAdvice(new BacklogDebuggerAdvice(debugger, nextProcessor, routeDefinition.getInput()));\n   203\u2192                    }\n   204\u2192                    addAdvice(new MessageHistoryAdvice(messageHistoryFactory, targetOutputDef));\n   205\u2192                    addAdvice(new BacklogDebuggerAdvice(debugger, nextProcessor, targetOutputDef));\n   206\u2192                }\n   207\u2192            }\n   208\u2192        }\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192        if (camelContext.isBacklogTracingStandby() || route.isBacklogTracing()) {\n   211\u2192            // add jmx backlog tracer\n   212\u2192            BacklogTracer backlogTracer = getOrCreateBacklogTracer(camelContext);\n   213\u2192            addAdvice(new BacklogTracerAdvice(camelContext, backlogTracer, targetOutputDef, routeDefinition));\n   214\u2192        }\n   215\u2192        if (route.isTracing() || camelContext.isTracingStandby()) {\n   216\u2192            // add logger tracer\n   217\u2192            Tracer tracer = camelContext.getTracer();\n   218\u2192            addAdvice(new TracingAdvice(camelContext, tracer, targetOutputDef, routeDefinition, first));\n   219\u2192        }\n   220\u2192\n   221\u2192        // debugger will automatically include message history\n   222\u2192        boolean debugging = camelContext.isDebugStandby() || route.isDebugging();\n   223\u2192        if (!debugging && route.isMessageHistory()) {\n   224\u2192            final MessageHistoryFactory messageHistoryFactory = camelContext.getMessageHistoryFactory();\n   225\u2192            // add message history advice (when not debugging)\n   226\u2192            addAdvice(new MessageHistoryAdvice(messageHistoryFactory, targetOutputDef));\n   227\u2192        }\n   228\u2192        // add advice that keeps track of which node is processing\n   229\u2192        addAdvice(new NodeHistoryAdvice(targetOutputDef));\n   230\u2192\n   231\u2192        // sort interceptors according to ordered\n   232\u2192        interceptors.sort(OrderedComparator.get());\n   233\u2192        // reverse list so the first will be wrapped last, as it would then be first being invoked\n   234\u2192        Collections.reverse(interceptors);\n   235\u2192        // wrap the output with the configured interceptors\n   236\u2192        Processor target = nextProcessor;\n   237\u2192        boolean skip = target instanceof InterceptableProcessor ip && !ip.canIntercept();\n   238\u2192        if (!skip) {\n   239\u2192            for (InterceptStrategy strategy : interceptors) {\n   240\u2192                Processor next = target == nextProcessor ? null : nextProcessor;\n   241\u2192                // use the fine grained definition (eg the child if available). Its always possible to get back to the parent\n   242\u2192                Processor wrapped\n   243\u2192                        = strategy.wrapProcessorInInterceptors(route.getCamelContext(), targetOutputDef, target, next);\n   244\u2192                if (!(wrapped instanceof AsyncProcessor)) {\n   245\u2192                    LOG.warn(\"Interceptor: {} at: {} does not return an AsyncProcessor instance.\"\n   246\u2192                             + \" This causes the asynchronous routing engine to not work as optimal as possible.\"\n   247\u2192                             + \" See more details at the InterceptStrategy javadoc.\"\n   248\u2192                             + \" Camel will use a bridge to adapt the interceptor to the asynchronous routing engine,\"\n   249\u2192                             + \" but its not the most optimal solution. Please consider changing your interceptor to comply.\",\n   250\u2192                            strategy, definition);\n   251\u2192                }\n   252\u2192                if (!(wrapped instanceof WrapAwareProcessor)) {\n   253\u2192                    // wrap the target so it becomes a service and we can manage its lifecycle\n   254\u2192                    wrapped = PluginHelper.getInternalProcessorFactory(camelContext)\n   255\u2192                            .createWrapProcessor(wrapped, target);\n   256\u2192                }\n   257\u2192                target = wrapped;\n   258\u2192            }\n   259\u2192        }\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192        if (route.isStreamCaching()) {\n   262\u2192            addAdvice(new StreamCachingAdvice(camelContext.getStreamCachingStrategy()));\n   263\u2192        }\n   264\u2192\n   265\u2192        if (route.getDelayer() != null && route.getDelayer() > 0) {\n   266\u2192            addAdvice(new DelayerAdvice(route.getDelayer()));\n   267\u2192        }\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192        // sets the delegate to our wrapped output\n   270\u2192        output = target;\n   271\u2192    }\n   272\u2192\n   273\u2192    @Override\n   274\u2192    public void postInitChannel() throws Exception {\n   275\u2192        // if jmx was enabled for the processor then either add as advice or wrap and change the processor\n   276\u2192        // on the error handler. See more details in the class javadoc of InstrumentationProcessor\n   277\u2192        if (instrumentationProcessor != null) {\n   278\u2192            boolean redeliveryPossible = false;\n   279\u2192            if (errorHandler instanceof ErrorHandlerRedeliveryCustomizer erh) {\n   280\u2192                redeliveryPossible = erh.determineIfRedeliveryIsEnabled();\n   281\u2192                if (redeliveryPossible) {\n   282\u2192                    // okay we can redeliver then we need to change the output in the error handler\n   283\u2192                    // to use us which we then wrap the call so we can capture before/after for redeliveries as well\n   284\u2192                    Processor currentOutput = erh.getOutput();\n   285\u2192                    instrumentationProcessor.setProcessor(currentOutput);\n   286\u2192                    erh.changeOutput(instrumentationProcessor);\n   287\u2192                }\n   288\u2192            }\n   289\u2192            if (!redeliveryPossible) {\n   290\u2192                // optimise to use advice as we cannot redeliver\n   291\u2192                addAdvice(CamelInternalProcessor.wrap(instrumentationProcessor));\n   292\u2192            }\n   293\u2192        }\n   294\u2192    }\n   295\u2192\n   296\u2192    static BacklogTracer getOrCreateBacklogTracer(CamelContext camelContext) {\n   297\u2192        BacklogTracer tracer = null;\n   298\u2192        if (camelContext.getRegistry() != null) {\n   299\u2192            // lookup in registry\n   300\u2192            Map<String, BacklogTracer> map = camelContext.getRegistry().findByTypeWithName(BacklogTracer.class);\n   301\u2192            if (map.size() == 1) {\n   302\u2192                tracer = map.values().iterator().next();\n   303\u2192            }\n   304\u2192        }\n   305\u2192        if (tracer == null) {\n   306\u2192            tracer = camelContext.getCamelContextExtension().getContextPlugin(BacklogTracer.class);\n   307\u2192        }\n   308\u2192        if (tracer == null) {\n   309\u2192            tracer = BacklogTracer.createTracer(camelContext);\n   310\u2192            tracer.setEnabled(camelContext.isBacklogTracing() != null && camelContext.isBacklogTracing());\n   311\u2192            tracer.setStandby(camelContext.isBacklogTracingStandby());\n   312\u2192            tracer.setTraceTemplates(camelContext.isBacklogTracingTemplates());\n   313\u2192            tracer.setTraceRests(camelContext.isBacklogTracingRests());\n   314\u2192            camelContext.getCamelContextExtension().addContextPlugin(BacklogTracer.class, tracer);\n   315\u2192        }\n   316\u2192        return tracer;\n   317\u2192    }\n   318\u2192\n   319\u2192    /**\n   320\u2192     * @param  camelContext   the camel context from which the {@link BacklogDebugger} should be found.\n   321\u2192     * @param  createIfAbsent indicates whether a {@link BacklogDebugger} should be created if it cannot be found\n   322\u2192     * @return                the instance of {@link BacklogDebugger} that could be found in the context or that was\n   323\u2192     *                        created if {@code createIfAbsent} is set to {@code true}, {@code null} otherwise.\n   324\u2192     */\n   325\u2192    private static BacklogDebugger getBacklogDebugger(CamelContext camelContext, boolean createIfAbsent) {\n   326\u2192        BacklogDebugger debugger = null;\n   327\u2192        if (camelContext.getRegistry() != null) {\n   328\u2192            // lookup in registry\n   329\u2192            Map<String, BacklogDebugger> map = camelContext.getRegistry().findByTypeWithName(BacklogDebugger.class);\n   330\u2192            if (map.size() == 1) {\n   331\u2192                debugger = map.values().iterator().next();\n   332\u2192            }\n   333\u2192        }\n   334\u2192        if (debugger == null) {\n   335\u2192            debugger = camelContext.hasService(BacklogDebugger.class);\n   336\u2192        }\n   337\u2192        if (debugger == null && createIfAbsent) {\n   338\u2192            // fallback to use the default backlog debugger\n   339\u2192            debugger = DefaultBacklogDebugger.createDebugger(camelContext);\n   340\u2192        }\n   341\u2192        return debugger;\n   342\u2192    }\n   343\u2192\n   344\u2192    private void backlogDebuggerSetupInitialBreakpoints(\n   345\u2192            NamedNode definition, NamedRoute routeDefinition, boolean first,\n   346\u2192            BacklogDebugger debugger, NamedNode targetOutputDef) {\n   347\u2192        // setup initial breakpoints\n   348\u2192        if (debugger.getInitialBreakpoints() != null) {\n   349\u2192            boolean match = false;\n   350\u2192            String id = definition.getId();\n   351\u2192            for (String pattern : debugger.getInitialBreakpoints().split(\",\")) {\n   352\u2192                pattern = pattern.trim();\n   353\u2192                match |= BacklogDebugger.BREAKPOINT_ALL_ROUTES.equals(pattern) && first;\n   354\u2192                if (!match) {\n   355\u2192                    match = PatternHelper.matchPattern(id, pattern);\n   356\u2192                }\n   357\u2192                // eip kind so you can match with (setBody*)\n   358\u2192                if (!match) {\n   359\u2192                    match = PatternHelper.matchPattern(definition.getShortName(), pattern);\n   360\u2192                }\n   361\u2192                // location and line number\n   362\u2192                if (!match && pattern.contains(\":\")) {\n   363\u2192                    // try with line number and location\n   364\u2192                    String pnum = StringHelper.afterLast(pattern, \":\");\n   365\u2192                    if (pnum != null) {\n   366\u2192                        String ploc = StringHelper.beforeLast(pattern, \":\");\n   367\u2192                        String loc = definition.getLocation();\n   368\u2192                        // strip schema\n   369\u2192                        if (loc != null && loc.contains(\":\")) {\n   370\u2192                            loc = StringHelper.after(loc, \":\");\n   371\u2192                        }\n   372\u2192                        String num = String.valueOf(definition.getLineNumber());\n   373\u2192                        if (PatternHelper.matchPattern(loc, ploc) && pnum.equals(num)) {\n   374\u2192                            match = true;\n   375\u2192                        }\n   376\u2192                    }\n   377\u2192                }\n   378\u2192                // line number only\n   379\u2192                if (!match) {\n   380\u2192                    Integer pnum = camelContext.getTypeConverter().tryConvertTo(Integer.class, pattern);\n   381\u2192                    if (pnum != null) {\n   382\u2192                        int num = definition.getLineNumber();\n   383\u2192                        if (num == pnum) {\n   384\u2192                            match = true;\n   385\u2192                        }\n   386\u2192                    }\n   387\u2192                }\n   388\u2192            }\n   389\u2192            if (match) {\n   390\u2192                if (first && debugger.isSingleStepIncludeStartEnd()) {\n   391\u2192                    // we want route to be breakpoint (use input)\n   392\u2192                    id = routeDefinition.getInput().getId();\n   393\u2192                    debugger.addBreakpoint(id);\n   394\u2192                    LOG.debug(\"BacklogDebugger added breakpoint: {}\", id);\n   395\u2192                } else {\n   396\u2192                    // first output should also be breakpoint\n   397\u2192                    id = targetOutputDef.getId();\n   398\u2192                    debugger.addBreakpoint(id);\n   399\u2192                    LOG.debug(\"BacklogDebugger added breakpoint: {}\", id);\n   400\u2192                }\n   401\u2192            }\n   402\u2192        }\n   403\u2192    }\n   404\u2192\n   405\u2192    @Override\n   406\u2192    public String toString() {\n   407\u2192        // just output the next processor as all the interceptors and error handler is just too verbose\n   408\u2192        return \"Channel[\" + nextProcessor + \"]\";\n   409\u2192    }\n   410\u2192\n   411\u2192}\n   412\u2192"

24\u2192import org.apache.camel.AsyncProcessor;\n    25\u2192import org.apache.camel.CamelContext;\n    26\u2192import org.apache.camel.Exchange;\n    27\u2192import org.apache.camel.Navigate;\n    28\u2192import org.apache.camel.Processor;\n    29\u2192import org.apache.camel.Traceable;\n    30\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.IdAware;\n    31\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.ReactiveExecutor;\n    32\u2192import org.apache.camel.spi.RouteIdAware;\n    33\u2192import org.apache.camel.support.AsyncProcessorConverterHelper;\n    34\u2192import org.apache.camel.support.ExchangeHelper;\n    35\u2192import org.apache.camel.support.service.ServiceHelper;\n    36\u2192import org.slf4j.Logger;\n    37\u2192import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192import static org.apache.camel.processor.PipelineHelper.continueProcessing;\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192/**\n    42\u2192 * Creates a Pipeline pattern where the output of the previous step is sent as input to the next step, reusing the same\n    43\u2192 * message exchanges\n    44\u2192 */\n    45\u2192public class Pipeline extends BaseProcessorSupport implements Navigate<Processor>, Traceable, IdAware, RouteIdAware {\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(Pipeline.class);\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192    private final CamelContext camelContext;\n    50\u2192    private final ReactiveExecutor reactiveExecutor;\n    51\u2192    private final List<AsyncProcessor> processors;\n    52\u2192    private final int size;\n    53\u2192    private PooledExchangeTaskFactory taskFactory;\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192    private String id;\n    56\u2192    private String routeId;\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192    private final class PipelineTask implements PooledExchangeTask, AsyncCallback {\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192        private Exchange exchange;\n    61\u2192        private AsyncCallback callback;\n    62\u2192        private int index;\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192        PipelineTask() {\n    65\u2192        }\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192        @Override\n    68\u2192        public void prepare(Exchange exchange, AsyncCallback callback) {\n    69\u2192            this.exchange = exchange;\n    70\u2192            this.callback = callback;\n    71\u2192            this.index = 0;\n    72\u2192        }\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192        @Override\n    75\u2192        public void reset() {\n    76\u2192            this.exchange = null;\n    77\u2192            this.callback = null;\n    78\u2192            this.index = 0;\n    79\u2192        }\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192        @Override\n    82\u2192        public void done(boolean doneSync) {\n    83\u2192            reactiveExecutor.schedule(this);\n    84\u2192        }\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192        @Override\n    87\u2192        public void run() {\n    88\u2192            boolean stop = exchange.isRouteStop();\n    89\u2192            int num = index;\n    90\u2192            boolean more = num < size;\n    91\u2192            boolean first = num == 0;\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192            if (!stop && more && (first || continueProcessing(exchange, \"so breaking out of pipeline\", LOG))) {\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192                // prepare for next run\n    96\u2192                ExchangeHelper.prepareOutToIn(exchange);\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192                // get the next processor\n    99\u2192                AsyncProcessor processor = processors.get(index++);\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192                processor.process(exchange, this);\n   102\u2192            } else {\n   103\u2192                // copyResults is needed in case MEP is OUT and the message is not an OUT message\n   104\u2192                ExchangeHelper.copyResults(exchange, exchange);\n   105\u2192\n   106\u2192                // logging nextExchange as it contains the exchange that might have altered the payload and since\n   107\u2192                // we are logging the completion it will be confusing if we log the original instead\n   108\u2192                // we could also consider logging the original and the nextExchange then we have *before* and *after* snapshots\n   109\u2192                if (LOG.isTraceEnabled()) {\n   110\u2192                    LOG.trace(\"Processing complete for exchangeId: {} >>> {}\", exchange.getExchangeId(), exchange);\n   111\u2192                }\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192                AsyncCallback cb = callback;\n   114\u2192                taskFactory.release(this);\n   115\u2192                reactiveExecutor.schedule(cb);\n   116\u2192            }\n   117\u2192        }\n   118\u2192    }\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192    public Pipeline(CamelContext camelContext, Collection<Processor> processors) {\n   121\u2192        this.camelContext = camelContext;\n   122\u2192        this.reactiveExecutor = camelContext.getCamelContextExtension().getReactiveExecutor();\n   123\u2192        this.processors = processors.stream().map(AsyncProcessorConverterHelper::convert).toList();\n   124\u2192        this.size = processors.size();\n   125\u2192    }\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192    public static Processor newInstance(CamelContext camelContext, List<Processor> processors) {\n   128\u2192        if (processors.isEmpty()) {\n   129\u2192            return null;\n   130\u2192        } else if (processors.size() == 1) {\n   131\u2192            return processors.get(0);\n   132\u2192        }\n   133\u2192        return new Pipeline(camelContext, processors);\n   134\u2192    }\n   135\u2192\n   136\u2192    public static Processor newInstance(final CamelContext camelContext, final Processor... processors) {\n   137\u2192        if (processors == null || processors.length == 0) {\n   138\u2192            return null;\n   139\u2192        } else if (processors.length == 1) {\n   140\u2192            return processors[0];\n   141\u2192        }\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192        final List<Processor> toBeProcessed = new ArrayList<>(processors.length);\n   144\u2192        for (Processor processor : processors) {\n   145\u2192            if (processor != null) {\n   146\u2192                toBeProcessed.add(processor);\n   147\u2192            }\n   148\u2192        }\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192        return new Pipeline(camelContext, toBeProcessed);\n   151\u2192    }\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192    @Override\n   154\u2192    public boolean process(Exchange exchange, AsyncCallback callback) {\n   155\u2192        try {\n   156\u2192            // create task which has state used during routing\n   157\u2192            PooledExchangeTask task = taskFactory.acquire(exchange, callback);\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192            if (exchange.isTransacted()) {\n   160\u2192                reactiveExecutor.scheduleQueue(task);\n   161\u2192            } else {\n   162\u2192                reactiveExecutor.scheduleMain(task);\n   163\u2192            }\n   164\u2192            return false;\n   165\u2192        } catch (Exception e) {\n   166\u2192            exchange.setException(e);\n   167\u2192            callback.done(true);\n   168\u2192            return true;\n   169\u2192        }\n   170\u2192    }\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192    @Override\n   173\u2192    protected void doBuild() throws Exception {\n   174\u2192        boolean pooled = camelContext.getCamelContextExtension().getExchangeFactory().isPooled();\n   175\u2192        if (pooled) {\n   176\u2192            taskFactory = new PooledTaskFactory(getId()) {\n   177\u2192                @Override\n   178\u2192                public PooledExchangeTask create(Exchange exchange, AsyncCallback callback) {\n   179\u2192                    return new PipelineTask();\n   180\u2192                }\n   181\u2192            };\n   182\u2192            int capacity = camelContext.getCamelContextExtension().getExchangeFactory().getCapacity();\n   183\u2192            taskFactory.setCapacity(capacity);\n   184\u2192        } else {\n   185\u2192            taskFactory = new PrototypeTaskFactory() {\n   186\u2192                @Override\n   187\u2192                public PooledExchangeTask create(Exchange exchange, AsyncCallback callback) {\n   188\u2192                    return new PipelineTask();\n   189\u2192                }\n   190\u2192            };\n   191\u2192        }\n   192\u2192        LOG.trace(\"Using TaskFactory: {}\", taskFactory);\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192        ServiceHelper.buildService(taskFactory, processors);\n   195\u2192    }\n   196\u2192\n   197\u2192    @Override\n   198\u2192    protected void doInit() throws Exception {\n   199\u2192        ServiceHelper.initService(taskFactory, processors);\n   200\u2192    }\n   201\u2192\n   202\u2192    @Override\n   203\u2192    protected void doStart() throws Exception {\n   204\u2192        ServiceHelper.startService(taskFactory, processors);\n   205\u2192    }\n   206\u2192\n   207\u2192    @Override\n   208\u2192    protected void doStop() throws Exception {\n   209\u2192        ServiceHelper.stopService(taskFactory, processors);\n   210\u2192    }\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192    @Override\n   213\u2192    protected void doShutdown() throws Exception {\n   214\u2192        ServiceHelper.stopAndShutdownServices(taskFactory, processors);\n   215\u2192    }\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192    @Override\n   218\u2192    public String toString() {\n   219\u2192        return id;\n   220\u2192    }\n   221\u2192\n   222\u2192    @Override\n   223\u2192    public String getTraceLabel() {\n   224\u2192        return \"pipeline\";\n   225\u2192    }\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192    @Override\n   228\u2192    public String getId() {\n   229\u2192        return id;\n   230\u2192    }\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192    @Override\n   233\u2192    public void setId(String id) {\n   234\u2192        this.id = id;\n   235\u2192    }\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192    @Override\n   238\u2192    public String getRouteId() {\n   239\u2192        return routeId;\n   240\u2192    }\n   241\u2192\n   242\u2192    @Override\n   243\u2192    public void setRouteId(String routeId) {\n   244\u2192        this.routeId = routeId;\n   245\u2192    }\n   246\u2192\n   247\u2192    @Override\n   248\u2192    public List<Processor> next() {\n   249\u2192        if (!hasNext()) {\n   250\u2192            return null;\n   251\u2192        }\n   252\u2192        return new ArrayList<>(processors);\n   253\u2192    }\n   254\u2192\n   255\u2192    @Override\n   256\u2192    public boolean hasNext() {\n   257\u2192        return processors != null && !processors.isEmpty();\n   258\u2192    }\n   259\u2192}\n   260\u2192"
